itinerary.title: Cologne
itinerary.description:
Depart the UK and arrive in Cologne, self transfer to Hotel in Cologne
Free time and overnight at hotel

itinerary.title: Cologne
itinerary.description: We will set off on our exciting Rhine cruise from here, heading for the northern or the southern Rhine. But you shouldn’t miss out on the opportunity to explore this cathedral city, with its wealth of monuments and attractions. The best way is to arrive the day before your cruise departs, so that you can relax – or simply extend your stay in Cologne after the cruise has ended.

itinerary.title: Rüdesheim
itinerary.description: The town describes its biggest attraction, the world-famous Drosselgasse, as ‘144 metres of joie de vivre’. This centre of Rhine Romanticism once inspired poets and musicians – it was here that Brahms composed his Symphony No. 3. Rüdesheim is the best-known spot in the Rheingau and has plenty of cultural attractions, including stately homes and fortifications. And no visit to the towns and countryside of this area of the Rhine would be complete without a Rüdesheim coffee, which was invented here.

itinerary.title: Germersheim (Speyer)
itinerary.description: Welcome to the Upper Rhine. Germersheim is only about 20 km from the city of Speyer, which is over 2000 years old. Speyer is Germany’s oldest planned medieval town. Speyer’s most prominent monument is its cathedral. As one of the country’s most important High Romanesque cathedrals, it is listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. You are sure to have an enjoyable day in Speyer.

itinerary.title: Breisach
itinerary.description: The little town of Breisach perches on a steep cliff along the Rhine, to the south-west of the Kaiserstuhl hills. Its main landmark is the cathedral, Münster Sankt Stephan. Set high above the town on a hill (Latin name: Mons Brisiacus), the church overlooks the Rhine valley, housing an impressive collection of artworks. Geldermann, a well-known producer of sparkling wine, is also based here. A visit to Breisach is an absolute must during your Rhine cruise.

itinerary.title: Strasbourg (Kelh)
itinerary.description: Today your Rhine river cruise will call at Strasbourg – a centre of European policymaking. With its medieval half-timbered houses and its elegant city palaces, Strasbourg really stands out from the crowd. But the highlight is the cathedral. From its viewing platform, the city’s half-timbered houses appear as small as pieces from a model railway set. Enjoy your day in the French part of the Rhine region!

itinerary.title: Mainz
itinerary.description: Mainz bleibt Mainz (or ‘Mainz is always Mainz’) is more than a carnival motto. It is an expression of the easy-going way of life that makes the locals proud of their city, makes it easy for non-locals to settle in and helps visitors to enjoy their stay here. With 2,000 years of history, Mainz is not just one of the oldest cities in Germany – it is also a place where you can see history come alive wherever you turn.

itinerary.title: Koblenz
itinerary.description: Discover Koblenz during your Rhine cruise. Its history dates back over 2,000 years, making it one of Germany’s oldest cities – and one of its most picturesque, with a wealth of sights and cultural attractions. Marvel at Koblenz’s palaces, stately homes, grand townhouses, narrow lanes and romantic corners. And watch as our A-ROSA ship sails close to the famous Deutsches Eck headland.

itinerary.title: Cologne
itinerary.description: After an exciting cruise on the Rhine, you will be docking back in Cologne today. However, before you set off on your journey home with a thousand memories of your trip, why not use the day to explore North Rhine-Westphalia and the city of Cologne? The imposing cathedral of Cologne is a must-see. See you soon and ahoy!

accommodations.name: Flandrischer Hof
accommodations.description:
Flandrischer Hof

 Centrally located in the city centre, directly on the streets of Hohenzollern ring and Rudolfplatz, this hotel is just a 15 minutes' walk from the city centre. Accessibility to public transport is a mere 100 metres away ensuring that guests can travel with ease to and from the hotel.

 Breath-taking views of the river Rhine and Bonn can be viewed at ease. It is a mere 20 minutes from Cologne/Bonn airport, whilst DÃ¼sseldorf airport is only 45 minutes from the hotel.

 The simple, yet elegantly furnished hotel rooms are suitable to any traveller wishing to enjoy a restful and rejuvenating night's sleep. Guests can enjoy a meal at the restaurant, which is known for its classy yet relaxed style, or may prefer to unwind with a refreshing drink in the inviting cocktail bar.

accommodations.name: A-ROSA BRAVA
accommodations.description:
A-ROSA BRAVA

 River cruise on the Rhine with A-ROSA BRAVA

Cruising on a river is certainly not the fastest mode of transportation. And the countless meanders of the Rhine, Danube and Rhône are not the fastest way to get from A to B either. But that’s precisely what we want to give you: a little time for you to relax and unwind. As you leisurely take in the breathtaking riverscape, you can really take time for yourself. Make yourself comfortable in your cosy cabin or simply daydream in SPA-ROSA and on the sun deck. If you like, you can also take part in our many exciting activities. Our A-ROSA BRAVA has been a popular choice among German-speaking guests in the past, but she offers a warm and welcoming atmosphere for all who seek to explore the beautiful Rhône and Saône Rivers.

title: Summer In Germanys South
description:
Experience summer along the majestic waterways of Europe - the Rhine, Main and Danube - in a very special way. Your journey begins in Basel, where the Rhine flows gently out of the Alps. Surrounded by snow-capped peaks, blooming meadows and green valleys, a city rich in culinary and cultural delights awaits you, making it the perfect place to start.

In Strasbourg, the heart of Alsace, marvel at the impressive Gothic cathedral and enjoy a stroll through the picturesque tanners' quarter as the city glows in warm sunlight. Continue on to Frankfurt, where glass skyscrapers coexist harmoniously with old half-timbered houses - an exciting contrast between tradition and modernity.

Würzburg impresses with its baroque residence, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and a magnificent view of the vineyards. In Nuremberg, medieval city walls, romantic squares and lively summer festivals bring history to life.

Your journey continues to Linz, where modern art and traditional culture come together. In Melk, the magnificent Melk Abbey, situated high above the Danube, impresses with a fantastic view of the summery river landscape.

The crowning glory of your trip is Vienna - the city of music and Empress Sisi. Stroll through the magnificent palace gardens, be enchanted by a concert or enjoy a glass of wine in a cozy pub garden. This trip is a true summer dream along the Alps and the great rivers of Europe - full of beauty, history and unforgettable moments.
excerpt: Experience summer along the majestic waterways of Europe - the Rhine, Main and Danube - in a very special way. Your journey begins in Basel, where the Rhine flows gently out of the Alps. Surrounded by snow-capped peaks, blooming meadows and green valleys, a city rich in culinary and cultural delights awaits you, making it the perfect place to start.
whats_included: Flight from UK to Basel
12 Nights Cruise on Board of the VIVA ENJOY
Free Upgrade to Balcony Cabin
All Meals included including drinks at the bar
daily freshly stocked minibar and reusable water bottle in the cabin
Selected beauty products from RITUALS
Free WLAN on board - for undisturbed reception
Tips for the entire crew
Flight back from Vienna to UK
highlights: Start your journey in Basel, where the Rhine flows out of the Alps
Discover the charming canals and Gothic cathedral of Strasbourg
Experience the striking skyline and historic quarter of Frankfurt
Visit Würzburg, home to the UNESCO-listed Würzburg Residence and scenic vineyards
Explore medieval streets and lively squares in Nuremberg
Enjoy contemporary art and Danube charm in Linz
Admire the magnificent Melk Abbey, overlooking the Danube
Conclude in elegant Vienna, city of music and imperial palaces
Scenic cruising through picturesque river valleys and vineyard landscapes
A perfect blend of culture, history, architecture and summer scenery

accommodations.name: VIVA ENJOY
accommodations.description: Düsseldorf-based river tour operator VIVA Cruises is continuing to grow: after the VIVA ONE (2022) and the VIVA TWO (2023), the third newbuild VIVA ENJOY will join the fleet next year. The ship was previously announced with the working title VIVA THREE, but in keeping with the company motto "Enjoy the Moment", the final name has now been decided. The maiden voyage of the VIVA ENJOY will start on September 22, 2024 from Frankfurt in the direction of Vienna. The tour operator is responding to the increasing demand for river cruises by expanding its fleet. "We have grown strongly over the last five years, as has our customer base and the demand from international guests, especially on the Danube. The third new building in combination with our remaining fleet gives us new scope again, not only with regard to our routings, but also with regard to our international partners and guests," explains Andrea Kruse, Managing Director of VIVA Cruises. The VIVA ENJOY will be the sister ship of the VIVA TWO and therefore identical in design. The ship has a total of 95 outside cabins with space for a maximum of 190 passengers. With a total of three restaurants, an open-plan lounge, a spacious sun deck and a fitness and wellness area, the ship is a floating premium hotel with a new, modern look and a pure feel-good ambience.

title: Enjoy 7 Night Corfu Cruise With Transfers
description:
Kick off your Greek island-hopping tour on the mainland of Corfu, taking some time to explore its Old Town (a UNESCO World Heritage Site) and picture-perfect architecture before setting sail to Mourtos, a small village on mainland Greece. 

Shortly after, you’ll proceed to Sivota, a tiny cluster of islands that are home to lush landscapes and the famous Blue Lagoon Bay.

You’ll then head to picturesque Parga, a resort town known for its natural environment. Feast your eyes on the crystal-clear bays fringing its main town and its iconic Venetian fortress before sampling local wine during a tasting session. 

After that, you’ll become acquainted with the sparkling beaches and bays of Antipaxos (a small yet breathtakingly beautiful island all the same) before heading to nearby Paxos and spending the following two days sampling sensational seafood at local taverns, whiling away the hours in Lakka village and soaking up the sun at the beach.
whats_included: Return flight from UK
7 nights’ accommodation aboard a cruise boat
Half Board meal plan
Welcome captain’s dinner
Standard Cabin w/ ensuite
Wi-Fi
Free use of Snorkelling equipment
Hassle free private transfers
highlights: Spend seven nights sailing over sparkling blue seas and stopping off at secluded bays, islands and beaches for swimming, snorkeling and superb scenery
Wine and dine in traditional Greek taverns and enjoy a daily breakfast aboard the boat, as well as a delicious captain’s dinner
Visit a range of islands including Sivota (home to the famous Blue Lagoon Bay) and beautiful Antipaxos
Get to know the island of Paxos with a two-day tour, docking at Gaios Port and stopping off at the beaches of Kanoni and Harami

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL IN CORFU
itinerary.description:
We’ll set sail from the Old Port (NAOK Marina) after sunset, giving you plenty of time to explore Corfu Island during the day beforehand. Visit the UNESCO World Heritage town of Kerkyra (Corfu Old Town) and marvel at its cultural heritage. From the towering fortresses to narrow medieval streets, there are plenty of layers to Corfu’s history to unravel. If you have time to see more of the island, head to the fishing village of Agni Bay, the dramatic cliff drops of Cape Drastis, the picturesque channel of the Canal d’Amour and the community of Palaiokastritsa, a serene village that sits tucked away.

In the evening, you’ll settle into your home away from home for the next week, kicking off your stay with a delicious dinner in front of ocean views as we start sailing towards Kassiopi.

itinerary.title: MOURTOS AND SIVOTA ISLANDS
itinerary.description:
Today we’ll sail to the village of Mourtos and Sivota Islands, stopping off to marvel at their lush, verdant landscapes. There are thick forests, natural coves, green hills and olive groves that merge with crystalline waters and golden beaches.

You’ll have the chance to visit the striking Papanikolis Cave and the famous Blue Lagoon Bay where you can stop off for a swim. There’s also the option to go on a coach tour to Acheron River. Here, beside breathtaking landscapes and river views, learn how to prepare delicious traditional dishes (Moussaka and Tzatziki) during an optional cooking lesson with an expert chef. 

In the evening, you can stroll around the port village, wine and dine in one of the taverns lining the promenade and watch the sunset bleed into the horizon.

itinerary.title: PARGA
itinerary.description:
Today we’ll visit Parga, nestled on the Ionian coastline and described as one of the region’s most beautiful villages. It boasts two crystal-clear bays fringing its main town as well as an iconic Venetian fortress. 

The natural environment here is stunning and there will be ample opportunity for you to head to one of the beachside hangouts or try your hand at water sports (especially towards the west of Parga town). 

We’ll then have lunch on board and explore the town a little more during the evening, tucking into some of the region’s specialties. There’s also an optional wine tasting session today, taking place in an area of the museum and featuring five different wine types (red, rose, white, dry and sweet) to cater to everybody’s tastes. All of the wines will come from the islands of the Ionian Sea and the Western and North Western part of Greece – regions that are well-known for their wine production.

Please note: if we’re able to dock in the port, we won’t need to use a water taxi. However, if we do have to use one, this cost is not covered in the overall package.

itinerary.title: ANTIPAXOS
itinerary.description:
We’ll visit Antipaxos today, a small island near the larger island of Paxos. You’ll tuck into a delicious lunch before stopping off at some of the island’s beaches and bays, including Voutoumi and Vrika. 

Snorkel through beautiful blue waters, stroll along scenic clifftop routes and pay a visit to one of the three main towns (Gaios, Loggos and Lakka). 

We’ll then have some food at a pop-up beach tavern in a secluded bay before watching the starry sky from the deck of the yacht later on.

itinerary.title: PAXOS PT. 1
itinerary.description:
We’ll then venture to Paxos, home to white pebble-strewn shores, striking blue water and lush countryside. We’ll dock at Gaios Port and explore the island for a whole day, seeking out secluded coves, marveling at Venetian architecture and sampling the world-renowned olive oil native to the island’s groves. 

In addition to seeing how the fishermen operate day to day, you’ll also hunker down over “the best catch of the day” (some of the best and freshest seafood) at one of the local taverns.

itinerary.title: PAXOS PT. 2
itinerary.description:
Today, we’ll explore a different part of Paxos Island. First, enjoy lunch on board while we head towards the bustling Lakka, the second largest village on the island and set in a horseshoe bay against a backdrop of olive and cypress trees. 

Visit the pebble-strewn Kanoni Beach and sandy beach of Harami before strolling along the seafront promenade. There are plenty of bars and traditional taverns here in Lakka, giving the village a very authentic Greek feel. 

We’ll dock overnight at a picturesque bay here and in the evening you’ll enjoy a captain’s dinner. 

Please note: the vessel can’t dock in the marina here.

itinerary.title: BENITSES BEACH AND VILLAGE
itinerary.description:
Before making our way back to the mainland of Corfu, we’ll pay a visit to Benitses Beach and village on the east coast of the island, tucking into a tasty lunch on the way. 

Today you’ll also have the chance to go on a bus tour of Corfu. If you do decide you’d like to go on this tour, your first stop will be the KOUM KOUAT Distillery where you’ll watch a short presentation of the distilling process of the koum kouat (kumquat). 

You’ll then stop off at Achilleion Palace, a 19th century summer palace that boasts spectacular gardens. Afterwards, head to the Mouse Island viewpoint and then drive through Corfu Town, witnessing sites such as the Old Harbour, the Old Venetian Castle and Esplanade Square. There will be some free time at the end of the tour for you to explore Old Corfu. 

After strolling through the romantic Old Town, we’ll reach Markas, one of the oldest taverns in the Old Town and then watch traditional dancing while sampling some of Corfu’s local dishes during optional “Corfu Greek Night”. 

Spend the night in the marina of Corfu Old Town.

itinerary.title: RETURN TO CORFU MAINLAND
itinerary.description: Our tour ends today as we arrive back at Corfu mainland again. We hope you’ve enjoyed the cruise and fallen head over heels for the Greek islands!

accommodations.name: Ionian Cruise
accommodations.description:
Ionian Cruise

 The beautiful motorised ocean vessel accommodate up to 25 passengers with 11 cabins – operates the Ionian Cruise. We will spend a week at sea, exploring the very best of this island cluster paradise, stopping in Sivota, Parga, Antipaxos, Gaios and Lakka in a round trip from Corfu.

This trip is best suited for those who want to set sail for discovery, heritage and historical sightseeing, the best of nature and deserted beaches, on-deck sea-view unwinding and the friendly atmosphere of a small ship cruise.

The friendly crew will attend to all your needs along the way. There are freshly prepared meals (Breakfast and Lunch), and all cabins include a private bathroom and air-conditioning.

title: Enjoy 7 Night Rhodes Cruise With Transfers
description: This Rhodes cruise offers a delightful blend of adventure, relaxation, and cultural exploration. Starting in Rhodes Harbour, you’ll explore the UNESCO-listed Old Town before cruising to the stunning Nanou Bay and picturesque Symi. Days are filled with swimming in crystal-clear waters, visiting historic sites like the Monastery of Panormitis, and soaking in the tranquil beauty of islands like Tilos and Chalki. Along the way, enjoy charming villages, serene bays, and local tavernas offering authentic Greek cuisine. Highlights include Anthony Quinn Bay, Kalithea Beach, and the medieval wonders of Rhodes. The journey concludes with a farewell dinner and a final morning on board, leaving you with unforgettable memories of Greece's vibrant island life.
whats_included: Return flight from UK
7 nights’ accommodation aboard a cruise boat
Half Bard meal plan
Welcome captain’s dinner
Standard Cabin w/ ensuite
Wi-Fi
Free use of Snorkeling equipment
Hassle free private transfers
highlights: Nanou Bay (Symi): Swim in crystal-clear waters surrounded by lush greenery.
Symi Port: Discover a charming, fairytale-like Greek village with idyllic tavernas.
St. George Bay & Panormitis: Relax in pristine bays and visit the historic Monastery of Archangel Michael Panormitis.
Tilos: Experience untouched nature, visit the abandoned village of Mikro Chorio, and dine in local tavernas.
Chalki Island: Enjoy peace, privacy, and the unspoiled beauty of Trakhia Bay and Nimporio village.
Anthony Quinn Bay: Swim at a beach named after the actor, with stunning scenery.
Kalithea Beach: Relax in beautiful coves with a vibrant atmosphere.
Medieval Rhodes: Wander through one of the best-preserved medieval towns in the world.

itinerary.title: Rhodes Harbour
itinerary.description: Our Rhodes cruise starts with boarding after 4:00 pm. Disembarkation takes place exactly one week after embarkation, at 09:00. With this in mind, we start our journey from Rhodes Harbour. Rhodes Harbour is a vibrant area filled with Greek tavernas, local product markets, and plenty of antique shops. The UNESCO World Heritage-listed “Old Town” of Rhodes offers a unique scenic experience with its distinctive architecture and historic charm. Dinner and overnight stay in Rhodes New Marina.

itinerary.title: Nanou Bay-Symi
itinerary.description: Between 10:00 and 13:00, after formalities and taking fuel, we cruise towards Symi, one of the Dodecanese Islands and a real gem. Symi is known as one of Greece’s most beautiful little islands. Our first stop will be Nanou Bay, where we will enjoy lots of swimming in its crystal-clear waters, surrounded by lush forests and green hills. In the afternoon, around 4:00 pm, we will cruise to Symi Port. Be sure to have your camera ready to capture wonderful images of this small, fairytale-like Greek village. While dinner will be served on board, you may wish to dine ashore at one of the numerous idyllic tavernas lining the marina. If you choose to do so, please inform the captain in advance; the local tavernas on Symi are very tempting, and the captain will understand your choice. Overnight stay in Symi. (B-L)

itinerary.title: St George Bay-Panormitis/Pedi
itinerary.description: Today, we leave Symi port after breakfast between 09:00 and 10:00 am. Our first stop will be St George Bay or one of the other many bays for lunch, swimming, and relaxing in the crystal-clear waters. In the afternoon, around 4:00 pm, we will cruise towards Panormitis, located in the southern part of Symi. Here, you can visit the Monastery of the Archangel Michael Panormitis. This Greek Orthodox Monastery, built in the early 18th century, is still inhabited by monks. It attracts visitors from around the world, and many Greeks pay yearly homage to St. Michael of Panormitis. Please note that loud music is not allowed here, to preserve the tranquility of the monastery. If sea conditions are not optimal, we may need to cancel our visit to Panormitis and go to Pedi instead. In the evening, enjoy a relaxing dinner and take in the serene surroundings. Overnight stay in Panormitis. (B-L-D)

itinerary.title: Symi-Akrogiali Bay-Tilos
itinerary.description: Today, after breakfast between 09:00 and 10:00 am, we cruise towards the beautiful island of Tilos. Tilos is surrounded by high and rocky mountains, steep coasts, beaches with transparent waters, and caves. We will enjoy the day at Akrogiali Bay, where you can relax and take in the stunning scenery. In the late afternoon, around 4:00 pm, we will enter Tilos harbor. This will give you a chance to stretch your legs and walk around the charming little town. In the center of the island, there is an abandoned village called Mikro Chorio, situated on a hilltop and reachable by taxi or bus. You can also visit Mikro Chorio in the evening to have a drink at the bar and experience its unique atmosphere. Later in the night, enjoy a dinner at a local tavern, savoring traditional Greek cuisine. Note that this late-night dinner is not included in the initial package price. Overnight stay in Tilos harbor. (B-L-D)

itinerary.title: Trakhia Bay-Chalki port
itinerary.description: Today, we cruise to the beautiful Island of Chalki, northwest of Rhodes. Depending on the sea conditions, we will start cruising before or after breakfast (between 06:00 and 10:00 am). Chalki is a perfect destination for peace and privacy, as tourism has not developed much here. The island is famous for its crystal-clear beaches. We will enjoy the day at Trakhia Bay or one of the other beautiful bays. In the late afternoon, around 4 pm, we cruise to Nimporio, the island’s only village and main port. The beach promenade of Nimporio is lined with charming cafeterias and taverns. Be sure to visit the local church, where there is a mass between 7:30 and 8:00 pm. After a day of exploration and relaxation, enjoy a traditional local Greek seafood dinner or Meze at one of the many local taverns run by the local fishermen. Overnight stay in Chalki. (B-L-D)

itinerary.title: Chalki Port-Anthony Quinn Bay-Kalithea Beach
itinerary.description: Today, we have an early start towards Rhodes Island. Breakfast will be served on the way or upon arrival at Anthony Quinn Bay, depending on weather conditions. This beach is named after the actor who bought this part of the island while filming “The Guns of Navarone.” Afterward, we cruise to Kalithea, where we will spend the rest of the day. Kalithea is home to beautiful coves and beaches, perfect for swimming and relaxing. Its vibrant atmosphere and charming surroundings make it a delightful destination for our last full day. Dinner and overnight stay in Kalithea. (B-L-D)

itinerary.title: Rhodes Port
itinerary.description: We arrive at the New Marina in Rhodes between 09:00 and 10:00 am, giving us plenty of time to explore. Today is a free day to visit one of the best-preserved medieval towns in the world and a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site. We spend the night at the New Marina of Rhodes, where we enjoy an optional traditional dinner with the group for our final farewell party. After that, we head to the gulet to enjoy our last night together on board. The next day, early in the morning, we sadly depart

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: As we have successfully cruised around Rhodes and its island complex, this is our departure day. Enjoy your last delicious Mediterranean breakfast on board. Disembarkation in Rhodes is scheduled for 09:00. We say goodbye to each other, take some last photos, and head back! Hopefully, though, we will see you again. Until then, we wish you all the best!

accommodations.name: Rhodes Cruise
accommodations.description:
Rhodes Cruise

 Explore the stunning Greek Islands – Rhodes, Symi, Chalki, Tilos, and other lesser-known gems of the Dodecanese Island group. 

Join our Gulet Aurora cruise, a comfy gulet with 9 cabins for up to 25 passengers. Other gulets like M/S Harmonia, M/S Aphrodite, M/S Eros, M/S Agiotis, and M/S Panagiota operate the Ionian cruise too. 

Enjoy a week-long itinerary covering Rhodes, Symi, Chalki and Tilos for an adventure and historical sightseeing.

 Cabins offer comfort mattress, wardrobe, reading light, a/c, a hairdryer, and a private shower.

title: Enjoy 7 Night Zante Cruise
description:
Located just off the west coast of mainland Greece, Zakynthos enjoys a reputation as both a party destination and a family-friendly getaway. Packed with foreign tourists along its eastern and southeastern coastline, the island also features numerous tranquil pockets in its central region of forested mountains and rolling hills.

The northern capes of the island also offer the calmest waters, which lap a string of developed beaches with plenty of modern facilities.

Kick off your Greek island-hopping tour on the island of Zante, taking some time to explore the wonders of Zante Island. In the evening, with a view of the ocean sail towards Agios Nikolaos. Shortly after, you’ll proceed to centrally located, beautiful and unspoilt Agia Ethimia.

You’ll then head to Ithaca (Vathi) and after that will visit Meganisi where you can enjoy snorkelling in the turquoise waters, feel the pebbles under your feet.

After Meganisi head to Fiskardo which is one of Greece’s best-kept secrets. Later you will visit Poros, Greece’s well-kept secret. Full of bars and tavernas Poros is the epitome of old school Greek charm.
whats_included: Return flight from UK
7 nights’ accommodation aboard a cruise boat
Half Bard meal plan
Welcome captain’s dinner
Standard Cabin w/ ensuite
Wi-Fi
Free use of Snorkeling equipment
Hassle free private transfers
highlights: Spend seven nights sailing over sparkling blue seas and stopping off at secluded bays, islands and beaches for swimming, snorkeling and superb scenery
Wine and dine in traditional Greek taverns and enjoy a daily breakfast aboard the boat, as well as a delicious captain’s dinner
Visit a range of islands throughout your tour.

itinerary.title: ZANTE--AGIOS NIKOLAOS
itinerary.description: As today is the arrival day and we don’t set sail from the Port until after sunset, you have plenty of time to explore the wonders of Zante Island. If you have time to see more of the island, don’t miss the chance to explore the Byzantine Museum, the National Marine Park in Laganás Bay. In the evening, we get acquainted with our sailing home for the next few days and get settled in, starting by dining with a view of the ocean as we set sail towards Agios Nikolaos.

itinerary.title: AGIOS NIKOLAOS--AGIA ETHIMIA
itinerary.description:
The rainfall on the Ionian Islands is what enables its lush and verdant landscapes to flourish. Think of thick forests, natural coves, green hills with olive groves, and ardent valleys that sit back-to-back with crystal clear waters and sparkling white beaches. In the evening, stroll the traditional Marina village and take your pick of Greek eats from the idyllic tavern-lined promenade, where you can watch the sunset. We head towards Agia Ethimia

You can also choose an optional Day Cruise to Navagio at an additional cost.

itinerary.title: AGIA ETHIMIA--ITHACA (VATHI)
itinerary.description:
Nestled on the Ionian coastline of the Greece Mainland, Ithaca has been described as one of the region’s most beautiful villages. This is because of its two crystal clear bays that line either side of its main town and the iconic Venetian fortress that forms an impressive backdrop. Known for its stunning natural environment that gives it an island feel. We will have lunch onboard, but stay in town for an evening of Greek feasting and hospitality. 

You can also choose an optional Odysseus Coach Tour available at an additional cost.

itinerary.title: ITHACA (VATHI)--MEGANISI
itinerary.description: Not many people know of Meganisi, overshadowed by the main island of Corfu, but you will be glad you got to experience this lesser-known beauty spot. First, we fuel up with a lunch before stopping for some coastal relaxation time at some of the islands secluded. Go snorkelling in the turquoise waters, feel the pebbles under your feet, take a walk amongst the cliff top surroundings and visit the Town . We will dine at a pop-up beach tavern in a secluded bay tucked away from the crowds, and later watch the stars from the deck of the yacht.

itinerary.title: MEGANISI--FISKARDO
itinerary.description: With white pebble beaches, striking blue seas and lush green countryside, Fiskardo is one of Greece’s best-kept secrets. We will dock at the port of Gaios ready for a full day on the island. You can easily explore with a motorcycle, although it’s also easy by foot, bike or car too. Spend the day seeking out the secluded coves and bays for swimming. Watch the fisherman go about their work from one of the seafront cafes, before enjoying some of the best and freshest seafood at one of the excellent local tavernas that stock the best catch of the day.

itinerary.title: FISKARDO--POROS
itinerary.description: Dotted with secluded bays and sea caves, with shallow waters for those keen to explore the underworld of the Ionian Sea, this part of Kefalonia Island is an explorer’s dream. Enjoy lunch on the vessel while we sail towards bustling Poros, the second largest village on the island set in a horseshoe bay to a backdrop of olive and cypresses trees. Visit the pebbled Kanoni beach and sandy Harami beach and wander amongst nature. Later, stroll the vibrant promenade along the seafront. Full of bars and tavernas Poros is the epitome of old school Greek charm. We will dock overnight at a picturesque bay here.

itinerary.title: POROS--ZANTE
itinerary.description:
Before we head back to the mainland of Zante Island, with free time to explore, we take the chance to fit in one more of Zante’s long sandy beaches. We visit the beautiful Shipwreck Beach and village on the east coast of the island, enjoying an onboard lunch on the way for some final sweeping sea views. We spend the night in the Marina of Zante Old Town, where we enjoy a Captain’s dinner with the group in our final farewell. 

You can choose optional wine tasting tour at an additional cost where you can relax and have a unique experience.

itinerary.title: DEPARTURE DAY
itinerary.description: As we come back in full circle to Zante, this is our departure day, where you’ll say goodbye to your new friends and your crew.

accommodations.name: Zante Cruise
accommodations.description:
Zante Cruise

 The beautiful motorised ocean vessel that can accommodate up to 16 passengers with 7cabins – operates the Ionian Cruise. We will spend a week at sea, exploring the very best of this island cluster paradise, stopping in Poros Agia Efimia , VAthi , FIskardo , Meganisi in a round trip from Zante.

This trip is best suited for those who want to set sail for discovery, heritage and historical sightseeing, the best of nature and deserted beaches, on-deck sea-view unwinding and the friendly atmosphere of a small ship cruise.

The friendly crew will attend to all your needs along the way. There are freshly prepared meals (Breakfast and Lunch), and all cabins include a private bathroom and air-conditioning.

title: Enjoy 7 Nights Mykonos Cruise
description: This Athens to Mykonos cruise offers an unparalleled blend of adventure, relaxation, and vibrant Greek culture. Starting from Laurium, you'll sail to breathtaking islands like Kea, Siros, Mykonos, and Kythnos, each offering unique experiences. Swim in crystal-clear waters at hidden coves like Makronisos, Rhenia, and Kolona Beach, and enjoy lively onboard parties with music, cocktails, and unmatched views. Explore charming villages such as Kea’s Vourkari and Siros’ Ermoupoly, indulge in local delicacies like Loukoumi and fresh seafood, and immerse yourself in the iconic Cycladic architecture of Mykonos. The journey is capped with Mykonos’ legendary nightlife, private cove stops, and a farewell celebration back in Laurium. With its perfect balance of culture, relaxation, and nonstop fun, this cruise creates memories that will last a lifetime.
whats_included: Return flight from UK
7 nights’ accommodation aboard a cruise boat
Daily Brunch
Daily Shots
Onboard DJ Party
Onboard Toga Party
Standard Cabin w/ ensuite
Wi-Fi
Free use of Snorkeling equipment
Hassle free Private Transfers
highlights: Scenic Swim-Stops: Enjoy crystal-clear waters at spots like Makronisos, Rhenia Island, Kolona Beach, and more.
Vibrant Island Hopping: Explore iconic Cycladic islands, including Kea, Siros, Mykonos, and Kythnos.
Onboard Parties: Experience DJ-led parties and celebrations with cocktails, music, and incredible views.
Cultural Exploration: Discover the cosmopolitan charm of Siros, Mykonos' iconic architecture, and Kythnos' historical significance.
Local Flavors: Savor traditional Greek delicacies such as Loukoumi, Halvadopita, and fresh seafood.
Charming Villages: Visit picturesque towns like Vourkari (Kea) and Ermoupoly (Siros).
Unforgettable Mykonos: Dive into the nightlife, shopping, and stunning scenery of Greece’s most famous party island.
Relaxation and Adventure: A perfect mix of sunbathing, swimming, and exploring hidden coves.
Final Celebration: Wrap up the journey with a farewell night in Laurium, creating lasting memories.

itinerary.title: Athens
itinerary.description:
On day one, we board the vessel at around 14:00, and have a couple of drinks, just to start off.
We then, start getting to know each other better and of course, start off with the partying!
While we are staying for the first night at Laurium, we can choose between roaming around Laurium’s famous bar strip, or staying onboard!
It’s up to you!
Note: On the rare occasion of any issues that may result from late arrivals, there is a possibility that we do not depart from Athens on Saturday, but on Sunday instead.

itinerary.title: Athens - Kea
itinerary.description:
After our first day, we will enjoy our lovely brunch as we depart, heading to Kea’s Island complex, of course, accompanied by great music and loads of excitement!
During our trip, we’ll make a stop at Makronisos, to enjoy our first swim-stop in the crystal clear waters of the Aegean.
The trip to Kea will include our first cove party, though a chance to explore Kea’s most beautiful village, Vourkari, as well!

itinerary.title: Kea - Siros
itinerary.description:
We will leave Kea behind, as we sail to Siros, the capital of the Cyclades with the dynamic character and cosmopolitan grace of a queen!
Syros Island is famous for its capital Ermoupoly, its excellent restaurants and its delicious local/traditional sweets, Loukoumi (Turkish delight) and Halvadopita (a soft kind of nougat).

During our stay at Siros, and of course, after our scheduled daily swim-stop, it’s time to let the DJ do his thing!
Nothing better than an onboard summer afternoon-party in Greece!!
Dive into the sea if you feel you need to cool off, or grab a cocktail and just relax onboard, with the best party-company out there, as we are just getting started!

itinerary.title: Siros - Mykonos
itinerary.description:
This is the day! We leave Siros and head to our trip’s main highlight, Mykonos!
The stunning aesthetic hits you in between the eyes, and the island’s aura is just exceptional. Mykonos is literally packed with high-end shops and stores, worldwide famous clubs and bars and it is the dream of every young person who loves to socialize, party and dance!!

The island of winds, as Mykonos is called, is particularly picturesque with stone-paved alleys, whitewashed houses, country chapels and windmills that ooze calmness and peace!
Before you reach the mainland, enjoy a swim-stop at Rhenia and get ready to experience a combination of, on and off-boat parties, that will make up for a day you will remember for sure!

itinerary.title: Mykonos
itinerary.description:
On the fifth day, we will see all the great aspects of this beautiful Aegean Island, as we will roam around its Island complex while partying non-stop!

We will make a couple of private stops, where we will create some moments and after all the “celebrations” we anchor at some cove, where we will make it for the night!

itinerary.title: Mykonos - Kythnos
itinerary.description:
For the sixth day, the plan is to leave Mykonos and travel to Kythnos.
On our way, we will stop at Kolona beach for a relaxing swim and some sunbathing.
The Island’s name derives from the ancient Greek word “Kefthemon”, meaning a dim place to hide, since Kythnos was chosen by many as a hiding place from intruders and pirates of nearby settlements, during old days!
Kythnos is also well known, for its great local Greek seafood!
Of course, during our stay at Kythnos, we will make it count, as we will be stopping at different coves, during the day, just to have even further fun!

itinerary.title: Kythnos - Athens
itinerary.description:
After Kythnos, we slowly start to end our trip, as we sail back from where we initially departed from, Laurium!
We arrive at the port, early in the afternoon, though the fun does not stop there, as we have one remaining night to create our last moments!

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description:
As we have successfully cruised, to and around Mykonos, this is our departure day..
We say goodbye to each other, exchange info with the ones we got close to, take some last photos and head back!
Hopefully, though, we will see you again. Until then, we wish you all the best!

accommodations.name: Mykonos Cruise
accommodations.description:
Mykonos Cruise

 The Cyclades, known for their iconic white and blue architecture, traditional lifestyle, folk music, and warm hospitality, are a must-visit island group in Greece. Among them, Mykonos stands out as the island of the winds. 

Mykonos offers a mix of flashy cosmopolitanism, with celebrity visitors, famous clubs, and bars, and a tranquil and picturesque atmosphere with stone-paved alleys, windmills, and the classic Aegean aesthetic.

 Join us aboard the M/S Artemis, accommodating up to 46 passengers in 16 cabins, for a weekly onboard party filled with music, good company, and drinks. All cabins are ensuite with comfortable amenities. Our friendly crew will cater to your needs, and our Chef will prepare daily brunch.

title: Greek Treasure Trail, With All Inclusive Crete, Transfers and Flights
description:
The holiday starts with a 2 night stay at the ‘eternal’ city of Athens, the capital of one of the most powerful civilizations of the ancient world. Walk hand in hand with history and visit some of its architectural masterpieces like the Acropolis and the Parthenon temple. A visit to Athens would seem incomplete without a visit to its shining jewels, the Acropolis museum and the National Archaeological Museum.

In Athens we've chosen Travelers' Choice 2022 Polis Grand Hotel . Polis Grand Hotel (Travelers' Choice 2022 ) is a luxury 4-star hotel located right in the heart of Athens. With its privileged location, Polis Grand provides unique accessibility to the historic sights and attractions of Athens.

After Athens, you will visit Santorini, possibly the most romantic and picturesque Greek Island, born from an ancient volcanic eruption, whose stupendous cliffs rise 900 feet from a half-moon caldera which also makes it a fascinating island to explore. Picturesque villages teeter on the rocky sides and whitewashed Cycladic houses perfectly complement the blue domed rooftops. In Santorini, you will be staying at the Aegean Plaza Hotel. It is ideally situated between the town of Fira and Pyrgos village offering breathtaking panoramic views of Santorini Island, the renowned Caldera and Volcano, Fira, Oia, Akrotiri and the crystal clear blue waters of the Aegean. The hotel is build on top of a hill surrounded by vineyards and combines the traditional architecture of the Cycladic islands with modern interior design.

The last 5 nights will be spent in Crete at the Travelers' Choice 2022 Hersonissos Hotel. The 4-star Heronissos Hotel is located in the mesmerising part of Hersonissos, close to Aquaworld Aquarium & Reptile Rescue Centre. Boasting close proximity to Church of Saint Paraskevi, this venue with air-conditioned rooms is ideal for fans of billiard, ping pong and golf or those willing to enjoy themselves in massage treatments, a wellness area and a spa area.

Hotel has 160 rooms. Many of the rooms can fit as many as four people in each, making it a great choice for families and groups. There is also the option of a deluxe sea view or ga

title: 3 Nights Budapest with Breakfast and Flights
description:
Budapest, Hungary’s capital, is paradise for explorers. Dramatically set along the Danube River to a backdrop of the Buda Hills, Budapest is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful cities in Europe. It is a city in a stunning natural setting with a rich architectural and historical heritage, offering an unmatched combination of culture and fine cuisine. 

And nowhere does this better than Budapest, a city of creatives, of ruin pubs where young minds and ideas meet from every corner of the world and exquisite aromas emanating from the restaurants of Váci Utca. A night might start in one of these bistros and end with a raucous party in a centennial thermal water spa. Thermal baths, decadent cafés and arty pubs also highlight how this hip Hungarian capital offers up incredible original experiences. Somewhere in the heart of the city a bottle of passion is waiting for you!

Marmara Hotel Budapest is centrally located in Budapest, walking distance from Mystique Room and close to Museum of Ethnography. This 4-star hotel is within close proximity of Parliament Building and The Glass House.

With numerous amenities for guests such as 24 hour front desk, concierge, parking - self, you will wake up at Marmara Hotel Budapest feeling ready to go every day for your time in Budapest.

title: Europas River Passage
description:
Experience an unforgettable journey along the great rivers of Europe - the Rhine, Main and the Danube. Your journey begins in Budapest, where the Danube flows majestically through the Hungarian capital and paves the way to historic cities such as Bratislava and Vienna. These metropolises enchant with their rich culture, magnificent palaces, classical music tradition and impressive architecture.

Your journey then continues to Passau and Regensburg, where medieval old towns, Gothic cathedrals and winding alleyways offer a real journey through European history. Be impressed by the Marienberg Fortress in Würzburg and the imposing Imperial Castle in Nuremberg - two places where history comes alive.

Further downstream, you will experience a city full of Roman traces in Mainz and discover a city full of history in Strasbourg and discover the combination of German and French culture with highlights such as the Gothic cathedral and the picturesque La Petite France district.

The crowning glory is Basel. The modern architecture, the renowned art museum and the historic center make Basel a final highlight of this trip.

This cruise is more than just a journey - it is a homage to Europe's cultural heritage, a feast for the senses and a unique experience, that harmoniously combines nature, culture and history.

These metropolises enchant with their rich culture, magnificent palaces, classical music tradition and impressive architecture.

highlights: Sail Three Iconic Rivers – Journey along the Rhine, Main and Danube in one spectacular cruise.
Imperial Capitals – Explore the elegance of Budapest, Vienna and Bratislava with their grand palaces and classical music heritage.
Medieval Masterpieces – Discover Regensburg and Passau’s charming old towns and Gothic architecture.
Historic Fortresses & Castles – Visit Marienberg Fortress in Wurzburg and the impressive Imperial Castle in Nuremberg.
Cultural Fusion – Experience the unique blend of German and French culture in Strasbourg’s La Petite France district.
Roman Heritage – Wander through Mainz and uncover its rich Roman history.
Art & Modern Elegance – Conclude in Basel, home to world-class museums and striking contemporary architecture.
Scenic River Landscapes – Enjoy vineyard-covered hills, dramatic river valleys and picturesque towns along the way.
Regional Flavours – Taste authentic Austrian, German, French and Swiss cuisine and wines.
Relaxed Luxury – Unpack once and enjoy panoramic views from the comfort of your river cruise ship.

accommodations.name: VIVA ENJOY
accommodations.description: Düsseldorf-based river tour operator VIVA Cruises is continuing to grow: after the VIVA ONE (2022) and the VIVA TWO (2023), the third newbuild VIVA ENJOY will join the fleet next year. The ship was previously announced with the working title VIVA THREE, but in keeping with the company motto "Enjoy the Moment", the final name has now been decided. The maiden voyage of the VIVA ENJOY will start on September 22, 2024 from Frankfurt in the direction of Vienna. The tour operator is responding to the increasing demand for river cruises by expanding its fleet. "We have grown strongly over the last five years, as has our customer base and the demand from international guests, especially on the Danube. The third new building in combination with our remaining fleet gives us new scope again, not only with regard to our routings, but also with regard to our international partners and guests," explains Andrea Kruse, Managing Director of VIVA Cruises. The VIVA ENJOY will be the sister ship of the VIVA TWO and therefore identical in design. The ship has a total of 95 outside cabins with space for a maximum of 190 passengers. With a total of three restaurants, an open-plan lounge, a spacious sun deck and a fitness and wellness area, the ship is a floating premium hotel with a new, modern look and a pure feel-good ambience.

title: 10 Days Charming North Kerala
description:
This 10-day immersive tour through North Kerala is a beautifully curated journey that weaves together the region’s rich cultural heritage, diverse landscapes, and deep-rooted traditions. Beginning in the historic port city of Kochi, you’ll step into a world shaped by centuries of Portuguese, Dutch, and British influence. The cobbled streets of Fort Kochi, the centuries-old synagogues, ornate churches, Chinese fishing nets, and the vibrant spice markets provide an evocative introduction to Kerala's colonial and maritime legacy. From there, the route traces the Muziris Heritage Trail, where stops at the Jewish Heritage Museum in Paravur and St. Thomas Church in Kodungallur offer a powerful glimpse into the state’s ancient Christian and Jewish past.

The tour then unfolds into the vibrant coastal city of Calicut (Kozhikode), the historic landing site of Vasco da Gama, where you can walk along Kappad Beach, soak in the sunset, and savor local seafood in the very place where global trade once flourished. From the coast, your journey ascends into the mist-covered hills of Wayanad, a green paradise tucked away in the Western Ghats. Here, nature and culture converge—tranquil lakes, tea plantations, and forested tribal villages provide a deeply authentic experience. You’ll meet local tribespeople, witness eucalyptus oil being made, go on a scenic trek, and enjoy a thrilling jeep safari through Wayanad Wildlife Sanctuary, where elephants, panthers, and rare birds roam freely.

Next, you descend to the untouched coastline of Thottada Beach, a quiet retreat nestled near Kannur. The experience here is both rejuvenating and culturally rich—you'll visit Tellicherry Fort, drive along the unique Muzhappilangad Beach (Asia’s only drive-in beach), and witness the awe-inspiring Theyyam performance, a traditional temple ritual that blurs the line between theatre and spirituality. The handloom villages of Kannur further immerse you in the artistry and rhythm of local life.

The final leg of your journey leads to the serene Nileshwaram backwaters, where a peaceful country boat cruise takes you past palm-fringed canals, local fishermen, and flocks of native birds. You'll end the trip with a visit to Bekal Fort, Kerala’s largest and most majestic seaside fort, and the stunning Kappil Beach and Kodi Cliff, where the backwaters meet the Arabian Sea in a breathtaking panorama. With a perfect mix of historic exploration, cultural encounters, natural beauty, beachside relaxation, and wildlife adventure—combined with comfortable stays, private transfers, and expert local guidance—this tour is designed to offer an unforgettable and holistic Kerala experience that touches both the senses and the soul.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Kochi
itinerary.description: Upon arriving at Kochi International Airport, you will be greeted by a representative and comfortably transferred to your hotel. The rest of the day is at leisure, allowing you to relax after your journey or explore the vibrant local markets, taste some authentic Kerala cuisine, or simply unwind and soak in the tropical ambiance of the city.

itinerary.title: Kochi Sightseeing
itinerary.description: After a hearty breakfast, you’ll set off on a fascinating tour of Kochi, a city shaped by centuries of colonial influence. Begin at Fort Kochi, a coastal area steeped in Portuguese, Dutch, and British history. Walk along Vasco da Gama Square and visit the beautifully preserved Santa Cruz Basilica and St. Francis Church—the oldest European church in India. Explore the charming lanes of Mattancherry, where you’ll see the VOC Gate, the Dutch Palace with its intricate murals, and the centuries-old Jewish Synagogue. Later, head to the seafront to witness local fishermen skillfully operate massive Chinese fishing nets—a signature image of Kochi. In the afternoon, travel to the serene village of Kumbalangi, where you’ll enjoy lush green surroundings, traditional fishing demonstrations, and a tranquil country boat ride. This experience offers a glimpse into the authentic rural lifestyle of Kerala.

itinerary.title: Kochi to Calicut via Paravur
itinerary.description: After breakfast, check out and begin your journey towards Calicut (Kozhikode), making enriching stops along the way. First, visit the Jewish Heritage Museum at Paravur, housed in a historic synagogue that showcases the long-standing Jewish presence in Kerala. Continue to St. Thomas Church (Marthoma Pontifical Shrine), built on the banks of the Periyar River, which is believed to be one of the earliest centers of Christianity in India and houses a sacred relic of St. Thomas the Apostle. After this spiritual and historical immersion, drive to Calicut, a coastal city that was once the gateway for spice traders from across the globe. In the evening, visit the historic Kappad Beach where Vasco da Gama first landed in 1498. Enjoy a peaceful stroll, watch migratory birds, and indulge in local seafood delicacies at nearby beach shacks.

itinerary.title: Calicut to Wayanad
itinerary.description: Today, you’ll leave the coast behind and ascend into the misty hills of Wayanad—a lush green region nestled in the Western Ghats. The drive offers scenic views of spice plantations, forested hills, and winding mountain roads. On arrival, check into a luxury farm resort surrounded by nature. Spend your afternoon at leisure—walk through nearby villages, interact with locals, or just relax while taking in the sights and sounds of the countryside.

itinerary.title: Exploring Wayanad
itinerary.description: Begin your day with a visit to a pristine freshwater lake, hidden amidst dense forest and sloping hills—a perfect spot for serene reflection. Then, head to tribal hamlets like Nellarachal, where you’ll meet indigenous communities and experience their way of life. Witness tribal weapon-making, traditional cooking, and eucalyptus oil production, while enjoying the hospitality of the locals. In the afternoon, embark on an exciting Jeep safari through Wayanad Wildlife Sanctuary, where you may spot elephants, panthers, tigers, wild boar, and a diverse array of bird species amidst the thick jungle canopy.

itinerary.title: Wayanad to Thottada Beach (near Kannur)
itinerary.description: After breakfast, bid farewell to the hills and descend towards the coastal beauty of Thottada Beach, a lesser-known paradise near Kannur. This 800-meter stretch of golden sand is bordered by palm groves and offers tranquility and natural beauty far from the crowds. Later, visit the historic town of Tellicherry, where you’ll enjoy a traditional Kerala lunch before exploring the colonial-era Tellicherry Fort and the elegant St. John’s Anglican Church. The town’s rich heritage and seaside charm make for a delightful afternoon.

itinerary.title: Muzhappilangad Beach & Kannur Culture
itinerary.description: Start your day with a visit to the majestic St. Angelo’s Fort, a 16th-century structure built by the Portuguese, offering panoramic views of the Arabian Sea and Mopilla Bay. Then, drive along Muzhappilangad Beach—India’s only drive-in beach—where the packed sand allows for a unique driving experience along the shoreline. Later, explore Kannur’s handloom villages, renowned for their traditional weaving practices. As evening falls, witness a live performance of Theyyam—an extraordinary ritual art form combining music, dance, and elaborate costumes that narrate mythical stories and local folklore.

itinerary.title: Thottada Beach to Nileshwaram Backwaters
itinerary.description: After breakfast, head north to the peaceful backwaters of Nileshwaram, a hidden gem of North Kerala. Upon arrival, set out on a two-hour country boat cruise through coconut-lined canals, watching local fishermen at work and spotting water birds in their natural habitat. The unhurried pace and scenic beauty of this region offer a deeply relaxing experience.

itinerary.title: Nileshwaram & Bekal Exploration
itinerary.description: Enjoy a leisurely morning at your beach resort before departing for Bekal in the afternoon. Visit the iconic Bekal Fort, Kerala’s largest and best-preserved fort, perched dramatically beside the Arabian Sea. Take in panoramic views from its high walls, then unwind at the nearby Bekal Beach. End your day with a visit to the unspoiled Kappil Beach and a short hike to Kodi Cliff, from where you can soak in the sweeping views of the coast and backwaters merging into the sea.

itinerary.title: Departure from Calicut
itinerary.description: After breakfast, check out and begin your return journey to Calicut International Airport for your onward flight. With hearts full of memories and bags packed with spices, stories, and souvenirs, your unforgettable Kerala adventure comes to an end.

accommodations.name: Radisson Blu Kochi
accommodations.description:
Radisson Blu Kochi

 This stylish hotel,Radisson Blu Kochi is located in the heart of Ernakulam City. Radisson Blu is a 45-minute drive from Cochin International Airport and an hour’s drive from Cherai Beach.

title: 10 Days Golden Triangle and Goa
description:
For 5 nights the Golden Triangle circuit, comprised of Delhi, Jaipur and Agra, will be your home. All three of these spellbinding cities bring something different to the circuit and you’ll hop from one to the other via dedicated vehicle for our passengers, safe from the glare of the Indian sun. Delhi, the Indian Capital, is home to three World Heritage Sites, the Qutab Minar, Red Fort and Humayun’s Tomb, as well as the India Gate and Lotus Temple, most of which will feature on your itinerary. Its bustling streets and lively ambiance will mean there’s never a dull moment, but if you’re set on experiencing life like a true Delhite, be sure to h    itch a ride on a rickshaw during your stay. 

Your next stop is the “Pink City” for Agra and it’s Mughal-era architecture, containing tombs and mausoleums left behind by the Mughal empire. Located in the northern  state of Uttar Pradesh, Agra is also home to the UNESCO World Heritage Sites of Agra Fort , however its most noteworthy definitely has to be the phenomenal Taj Mahal, a veritable symbol of India which is breath taking to behold. Later you will visit aipur, the capital of the Indian state of Rajasthan and a vibrant hub of art and flamboyancy. The honey-colored Hawa Mahal, the impressive astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar and the World Heritage Site of Amer Fort are three important landmarks which perhaps helped Jaipur gain the status of number one Indian destination in 2015 by Trip Advisor. 

The next leg of your trip will be spent in Goa. Although India’s smallest state, Goa makes up for its size with idyllic beaches, raucous nightlife and ancient churches and temples. Goa’s two World Heritage Sites, the Bom Jesus Basilica and the churches and convents of Old Goa, are definitely worth a visit, not to mention the various forts of Aguada, Tiracol and Chapora. History buffs can satisfy their appetites at the Naval Aviation Museum or in the cultural quarter of Fontain in Panaji, while thrill-seekers can while away the hours enjoying Goa’s myriad of water sports at Bagatelle and Calangute Beaches. Then, dance the night away in a place that is ranked 6th in the top 10 nightlife cities in the world by National Geographic, or get to grips with typical culinary delights in swanky restaurants  beneath the soft glow of the moonlight. Whatever your tastes,  your four nights spent here are guaranteed to be both memorable and jam-packed. 

Upgrade Goa beach stay to All Inclusive in Whispering Palms Beach Resort from an additional £169 per person!

itinerary.title: ARRIVE DELHI
itinerary.description:
Your journey of a lifetime begins in New Delhi. Arrive at the international airport, where you’ll be met and transferred to your hotel. It is recommended that you spend your first evening at leisure in your hotel to recuperate from your international journey. Few places on earth combine beauty, history and escapism to such dazzling effect as the great city of Delhi- the sprawling and fascinating capital of India. The title “A Tale of Two Cities” is apt as Delhi bridges together two different worlds. The Old Delhi, once the capital of Islamic India, contrasts with the British-built centre of New Delhi. On this journey you will discover a land sprinkled with dazzling gems of India’s “golden” legacy. 

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, your visit will include a tour of Old Delhi and the immense Jama Masjid, the greatest mosque in India. You will also see the legendary Red Fort and Raj Ghat where Mahatma Gandhi was cremated. Finally, awaken the senses with a thrilling rickshaw ride through the busy markets of Chandni Chowk. 

Also visit Rajghat - memorial of Mahatma Gandhi & go on a rickshaw ride through the silver street in Chandni Chowk. New Delhi offers a fascinating contrast: here, you will walk the wide, straight boulevards and pass the monuments of the Empire. Walking the imperial residence, Rashtrapati Bhavan, to the mighty arch of India Gate, designed by Edwin Lutyens in the 1920s, the eternal flame burns day and night to remember the soldiers in the Indo-Pakistan war. Continue to the Laxmi Narayan Temple, Humayun’s Tomb and the Qutub Minar. Another highlight is the Bahai Temple with its lotus dome.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI-AGRA
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll drive to the Mughal city Agra through Yamuna Expressway which is a 6-lane, 205 km long, controlled-access expressway. In the afternoon visit the beautiful Agra Fort, built by the great Emperor Akbar. 

Evening will be at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN AGRA

itinerary.title: AGRA - JAIPUR BY SURFACE
itinerary.description:
After a delicious breakfast, visit the beautiful Taj Mahal, one of the most beautiful monuments and one of the wonders of the world. It is also synonymous to love and romance. 

Later you’ll drive to the Pink City of Jaipur and check in to your next hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll really get to experience the magic of Jaipur to the maximum during a full day sightseeing tour. You’ll drive past the Hawa Mahal and in the afternoon you’ll explore the city palace and museum and walk adjacent the astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar.

In the evening, you’ll visit the Amer Fort for an incredible light and sound show.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR-DELHI BY SURFACE--DELHI TO GOA BY FLIGHT
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, you will head towards Delhi. You will then be transferred to the airport for your flight to Goa. Before you catch your flight to Goa, you could do some souvenir shopping at the domestic airport . On arrival in Goa you will be met by another representative & will be transferred to your hotel. Rest of the evening at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN GOA

itinerary.title: GOA AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
After Breakfast day will be at leisure and you can spend it the way you want to.

OVERNIGHT IN GOA

itinerary.description:
Days are at leisure, your breakfast at the hotel is included, you could just laze around on the beach, bask in the sun, jump in the pool and take it easy or could try some water sports on the beautiful stretch of the beach or you could explore the other beaches of Goa. Shopping areas are all in close proximity too.

title: 10 Nights Keralan Odyssey
description:
"Every journey is in itself a pilgrimage. Because wherever you go, you see what God created."

– An ancient Chinese proverb.

When you find yourself in the backwaters of Kerala, in southern India, you'll be astounded at how appropriate that saying is with the scenery before you. It’s not without reason that Kerala is known as‘God’s own country’. A paradise, nestled between the Western Ghats and the overwhelmingly beautiful Arabian Sea and Indian Ocean, Kerala has a little bit of everything that India has to offer.

"Right from the pristine beaches full of colourful and rich culture to delectable cuisines for you to savour, this is one holiday you must make to not just experience but rejuvenate yourself." – Lonely Planet, India 2011.

This incredible journey begins with return international flights from the UK, taking the hassle out of your travel planning. You'll enjoy a two-night stay in the vibrant city of Kochi, giving you ample time to explore the rich culture and attractions of Kerala's dynamic city. From there, you'll head to the serene tea fields of Munnar for a two-night stay in a Superior Tea View Room. In Munnar, you'll take a tour of the Tea and Spice Plantation, complete with tea tasting, and visit a tea museum. Then, it's on to Thekkady, where you'll stay for two nights, enjoying access to the lush Periyar Sanctuary. Don't miss the chance to experience wildlife up-close on a bamboo raft or motor boat!

The trip continues with a unique night aboard an air-conditioned houseboat, where you'll be treated to all three meals, including a complimentary bottle of wine. You will experience the local culture by attending performances of Kathakali dances and Kalaripayattu martial arts in Munnar. Afterwards, you'll head to the picturesque town of Poovar for a rejuvenating three-night stay. While there, pamper yourself with a full-body massage and keep yourself fit with an hour of trekking.

Throughout the journey, your sightseeing and transfers will be handled by a private air-conditioned sedan car, driven by an English-speaking chauffeur. An English-speaking local guide will be available for certain excursions to ensure you don't miss any details. Entrance fees for monuments and sightseeing places are covered as part of the package. During your hotel stays, breakfast and dinner are included, and you'll receive one mineral water bottle per person each day to keep you refreshed. Attractions such as Fort Kochi Beach, Chinese fishing nets, Mattancherry Palace, and the Jewish Synagogue are also included in this immersive itinerary.

And when you can do all of this for as little as only £1299 per person, that's almost half what it usually costs, you surely need no reason to pack your bags and find yourself on the ultimate holiday.

itinerary.title: KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Welcome to Kochi, the most fascinating city of Kerala. On arrival at Kochi International Airport, you will be met and transferred to the hotel. Enjoy the rest of the day at leisure.

Kochi is the magnificent entry to Kerala and has been featured in National Geographic Travellers “50 greatest places of a lifetime.” Spread over several islands and historically rich in culture with booming industries, the city is a fantastic beginning to exploring the vivacious diversity of Kerala. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Unlock Kochi’s history by embarking on a morning sightseeing tour that will take you into the heart of this magnificent and beautiful city. The Dutch, British, French and Portuguese have all left their outstanding imprint with beautiful architecture. You will visit Fort Cochin, which has been a cherished sea port for centuries, Vasco Da Gama Square and Santa Cruz Basilica. Continue to St. Francis’ Church, considered to be India’s oldest European church, built by Portuguese Franciscan Friars.  Vasco da Gama was buried here before being taken to Lisbon. Proceed to the VOC Gate, Dutch Palace, Jewish Synagogue and marvel at Mattancherry Palace’s murals depicting the Ramayana and Puranic legends. 

Intriguingly, the only traces of China’s influence are the gigantic nets, ‘cheenavalas’ from 1350. According to Diplomat Ma Haun, “The fishermen are tourist-friendly and will happily let you join them.” And his observation still rings true today. You will learn about their very unusual fishing method, operated from the shore. In the afternoon, experience your very own pièce de résistance with a cooking class from a renowned local TV personality. At lunch, enjoy the flavours of Keralite cooking. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI – MUNNAR
itinerary.description:
Check out after a hearty breakfast and drive to Munnar over rugged hills, rolling oceans of tubby green tea bushes, through luscious grasslands, before passing the Pallivasal tea estates. This will be your first glimpse of Munnar. On arrival, check-in at the hotel.

At a height of 1,500 metres, set amidst mountains once known as the High Range of Travancore, Munnar is a bustling hub of tea plantations.  Prepare to be captivated by its dense carpet of tea, sprinkled with Acacias and Red Gum trees, and delighted by its freshly brewed cuppas.

Enrote to Munnar take a break for Spice Plantation tour - Munnar is the heaven of spices and this spice tour will leave your senses tingling with joy. Wander in the captivating farms and witness the plantation of spices like black pepper, cardamom, ginger, curcuma, cinnamon, etc.

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: MUNNAR SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
Continue with a full day sightseeing of Munnar, watching the mountain goats in the Rajamalai wildlife Sanctuary. Then head to the the Kannan Devan Hills, the perfect getaway with panoramic views of the Munnar Tea Estates and the lake. After lunch you will proceed to Mattupetty, with the rare chance of seeing a herd of wild elephants on the way to Mattupetty Dam. 

Next up is the Tea Museum’s photographs and machinery during the revolution of the industry, which includes the original tea roller, the ‘Rotovane,’ and the ‘Pelton Wheel’. Today you will also experience the Kathakali & Martial Arts show.

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: MUNNAR- THEKKADY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the hotel and drive to the spice capital and check into your next hotel. Along the way you will also explore Thekkady. The afternoon is at your leisure. Feel free to walk through the spice market in the Kumily Village. Make sure you leave room in your suitcase for the finest cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg and mace!

First up is the Kathakali, a mixture of art, music, dance and drama, with majestic costumes, elaborate makeup, and mythological stories. Kalaripayattu is the oldest form of martial arts, with elaborate movements and the sounds of slashing swords. Return to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN THEKKADY

itinerary.title: EXPLORE THEKKADY
itinerary.description:
Post breakfast at the hotel, step out for a blissful day in the natural heaven for wildlife. 

Tourists rush from all over the country to experience the exotic wildlife at the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary which is amidst dense forests and make way for thrilling mountain walks. Experience boating in the lake where you can spot many more interesting species of animals as you admire the beauty called Thekkady.

After spending quality time with nature, head to the Tribal Heritage Museum which features different types of artifacts displaying the culture, dressing, rituals, etc. of the Mannan settlement. You can also check out the spice market in the Kumily Village and pick fresh aromatic cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg and mace from the market.

OVERNIGHT IN THEKKADY

itinerary.title: THEKKADY-ALLEPPEY
itinerary.description:
Post breakfast, check out of the hotel and depart to Alleppey via Kuttikanam which is a hill station in Idukki district and is best known for trekking and adventure sports.

You can explore the cardamom and tea plantations which surround the entire place. Later head to Alleppey and experience the memorable cruise through the iconic backwaters of Kerala. Alleppey is the most popular backwater cruising destination in Kerala and is termed as “Venice of the East”. Kerala claims its title as ‘God’s Own  Country’ due to its captivating charm and wonder.

Your comfortable and luxurious house boat will take you through paddy fields and lined up coconut trees. Indulge in some traditional Kerala cuisines served on board made with various tasty spices and flavours.

Marvel the Chinese fishing nets, ancient temples, rustic buildings, paddy fields and swaying coconut trees along the coastline. Having experienced an overnight stay in a houseboat, you have lived a perfect vacation in Kerala.

OVERNIGHT ON THE HOUSEBOAT

itinerary.title: ALLEPPEY-- KOVALAM-- POOVAR
itinerary.description:
After a mesmerising stay in the houseboat, it’s time to check out and proceed to the hotel in Poovar (Enroute to Poovar visit Hawa Beach in Kovalam).

Ever imagined ending your holiday on an island paradise of your own? Here in Poovar that is exactly what you will find. This nirvana is tipped as one of the ‘50 must visit’ places in India by ‘National Geographic Traveler’, with its coconut trees and endless golden sands. Shrouded in pristine Kerala backwaters, flanked by the Arabian Sea on the East and the enchanting towering Ghats to the West, along with a heavenly beach, Poovar Island is your very own Indian Idyll. How about reclining on the gorgeous nearby beach, swimming in translucent waters or simply unwinding with a relaxing body massage? Your perfect end to India!.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: EXPLORE POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Spend a full day at leisure sunbathing on the golden sands of Poovar Beach. The estuary shimmers in spades of wow factor where the river, sea and lake meet the land.

A complimentary hour long boat can be organised at Poovar Lake, where you can find golden sandy beaches meeting the estuaries and mangroves.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: EXPLORE POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Breakfast is at the hotel with the rest of the day at leisure. End your holiday on this wonderful paradise by treating yourself to an Ayurvedic Massage, where the body is revived and the soul replenished. Included is a full body session at the hotel or a nearby spa. Please book your slot.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR--TRIVANDRUM AIRPORT AND DEPART TO UK
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the hotel. A beautiful souvenir is the perfect way to end such a magical holiday. Take home a silk sari or handmade version, its motif conveying Kerala’s everlasting memories.

BOARD YOUR RETURN FLIGHT HOME

accommodations.description:
CGH Earth - Casino Hotel, Kochi

 The Casino Hotel is not just one of Cochin's landmarks, it's also a fine introduction to the charms of the city, and indeed, of Kerala. Here, you can get an authentic taste of Kerala's colourful life, diverse cultures and eclectic cuisine. The hotel is 6 km from Ernakulam and a 10 minute ferry ride to Fort Cochin and Mattancherry.

The Casino Hotel features regular rooms, spaciously appointed suites and a separate non-smoking wing on the ground floor. Air-conditioned guest rooms are fitted with a personal safe, a minibar and satellite television. Environmentally sensitive practices are the watchword here, as in all the CGH properties.

Many guests love whiling away a relaxed hour or two in the Casino Hotel's sunlit, spacious lounge. This is the heart of th

accommodations.name: Tea Harvester, Munnar
accommodations.description:
Tea Harvester, Munnar

 Tea Harvester is a boutique property brilliantly designed in spacious and contemporary architecture, where vintage meets vogue. Ideal for both romantics and jetsetters who like a bit of elegance and opulence. Perched on the hillside in comfort and style, about 1800 feet above sea level, amid three acres of verdant tea trees, cardamom plantation and pepper wine, lays cradled an exemplar of fine living.

All the rooms are approximately 450 square feets, including Balconies in Front offering a 180 degree view of Tea plantations &180 degree view of Cardamom plantation from the balcony behind the room. Spacious Bathrooms, large windows, harmonious colours and long verandas are some of our features designed to reminisce the planter’s charm.

The property offers a guided m

accommodations.name: Pepper County -- Deluxe Home Stay, Thekaddy
accommodations.description:
Pepper County -- Deluxe Home Stay, Thekaddy

 Pepper County is an idyllic home spread over an area of 7 acres in Kumily. The homestay is set amidst spice plantations and paddy fields.

It is a nice place to spend a peaceful, relaxing holiday in the quaint setting. The homestay offers basic but comfortable accommodation. Pepper County is indeed a paradise for nature lovers and honeymooners who come in search of tranquility. 

Traditional Kerala cuisine is served at Pepper County. There are plenty of things to do while staying at the Pepper County such as nature walks, bamboo rafting, hiking, boating and trekking. Pepper County also organises daily visits to spice plantations besides jungle jeep safaris and elephant rides. Cultural performances like Kathakali and Kalaripayatu (martial art fo

accommodations.name: Air Conditioned House Boat, Alleppey
accommodations.description:
Air Conditioned House Boat, Alleppey

 Spend your day on the traditional Houseboat also know as Kettuvallam. 

The Kettuvallam or 'boat with knots' is called so because coir ropes tied in knots hold the entire structure of the boat together. During the entire assembling work of the boat, not a single nail is used. Natural and eco-friendly products like bamboo mat, arecanut trees and coir are used to construct the boats.

Kettuvallam comprise of a separate living room with dining table, a bedroom with bathroom and a sit out area at one corner of the boat.

Guests can enjoy a calm evening right in the middle of the backwaters and watch one of the best sunsets that there could be.

title: 10 Nights Single Traveller Experience to Kerala
description:
“To awaken alone in a strange town," wrote British explorer and travel writer Freya Stark "is one of the pleasantest sensations in the world.” So what’s stopping you? Do yourself a favour and travel to the fascinating places of Kerala solo – no one to answer to, no limits and a fresh clean slate for you to splash with the colours of India! Of course, travelling by yourself doesn’t mean you will be alone – unless that is your preference. Sure, you might start your journey by yourself, but you’ll soon discover that our small-group trip allows you to explore Kerala in the company of like-minded people.

As Lonely Planet perfectly sums up: "Kerala is a world away from the frenzy of elsewhere, as if India had passed through the Looking Glass and becomes an altogether more laid-back place". Thus, naturally this part of the world attracts those looking to get up close and personal with the dazzling gems of India. You’ll enjoy the journey even more by sharing the experience with a small group of passionate travelers, over a glass of wine or meal after a packed day of treasures.

Your journey of a lifetime begins in Kochi, the glorious gateway to Kerala, then onto Munnar where you will enjoy the finest tea with new friends. Continue to Thekkady to board the wildlife viewing boat at the Periyar Tiger Reserve before setting out on an enchanting overnight house boat from Alleppey. Your final destination is Poovar where you will enjoy the "paradise" of South India, with its coconut trees and sweeping beaches!

We understand that the first step towards taking a solo holiday may feel daunting, so we're here to ensure you have the perfect adventure from the get go! Enjoy 10 nights from £1699 pp!
