itinerary.title: KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Welcome to Kochi, the most fascinating city of Kerala. On arrival at Kochi International Airport, you will be met by a rep and the other travellers before being transferred to the hotel. Enjoy the rest of the day at leisure.

Kochi is the magnificent entry to Kerala and has been featured in National Geographic Travellers “50 greatest places of a lifetime.” Spread over several islands and historically rich in culture with booming industries, the city is a fantastic beginning to exploring the vivacious diversity of Kerala. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Unlock Kochi’s history by embarking on a morning sightseeing tour that will take you and the group into the heart of this great city. The Dutch, British, French and Portuguese have all left their outstanding imprint with beautiful architecture. You will visit Fort Cochin, which has been a cherished sea port for centuries, Vasco Da Gama Square and Santa Cruz Basilica. Continue to St. Francis’ Church, considered to be India’s oldest European church, built by Portuguese Franciscan Friars.  Vasco da Gama was buried here before being taken to Lisbon. Proceed to the VOC Gate, Dutch Palace, Jewish Synagogue and marvel at Mattancherry Palace’s murals depicting the Ramayana and Puranic legends. 

Intriguingly, the only traces of China’s influence are the gigantic nets, ‘cheenavalas’ from 1350. According to Diplomat Ma Haun, “The fishermen are tourist-friendly and will happily let you join them.” And his observation still rings true today. You and the group will learn about their very unusual fishing method, operated from the shore. In the afternoon, experience your very own pièce de résistance with a cooking class from a renowned local TV personality. At lunch, enjoy the flavours of Keralite cooking. Return to the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI – MUNNAR
itinerary.description:
Check out after a hearty breakfast and drive to Munnar over rugged hills, rolling oceans of tubby green tea bushes, through luscious grasslands, before passing the Pallivasal tea estates. This will be your first glimpse of Munnar. On arrival, check-in at the hotel.

At a height of 1,500 metre, set amidst mountains once known as the High Range of Travancore, Munnar is a bustling hub of tea plantations.  Prepare to be captivated by its dense carpet of tea, sprinkled with Acacias and Red Gum trees, and delighted by its freshly brewed cuppas. 

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: MUNNAR
itinerary.description:
Continue with a full day sightseeing of Munnar, watching the mountain goats in the Rajamalai Wild Life Sanctuary. Then head to the the Kannan Devan Hills, the perfect getaway with panoramic views of the Munnar Tea Estates and the lake. After lunch you will proceed to Mattupetty, with the rare chance of seeing a herd of wild elephants on the way to Mattupetty Dam. Next up is the Tea Museum’s photographs and machinery during the revolution of the industry, which includes the original tea roller, the ‘Rotovane,’ and the ‘Pelton Wheel’. Return to the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: MUNNAR-THEKKADY
itinerary.description:
Along the way you will also explore Thekkady. After breakfast, check out from the hotel and drive to the spice capital and check into your next hotel. The afternoon is at your leisure. Feel free to walk through the spice market in the Kumily Village. Make sure you leave room in your suitcase for the finest cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg and mace!

First up is the Kathakali, a mixture of art, music, dance and drama, with majestic costumes, elaborate makeup, and mythological stories. Kalaripayattu is the oldest form of martial arts, with elaborate movements and the sounds of slashing swords. Return to the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN THEKKADY

itinerary.title: THEKKADY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, spend a morning at leisure. In the late afternoon, board the wildlife viewing boat cruise at the scenic Periyar Tiger Reserve. Amidst the hazy mountains of the Silent Valley, lies Thekkady, the opening to the renowned Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, a place where congestions are caused only by elephant crossings. Catch rare sightings of tigers playing under smouldering sun sets or watch kingfishers fluttering along the rivers. The sanctuary is an open-air collection of natural bio diversity and endangered wildlife. Return to the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN THEKKADY

itinerary.title: THEKKADY-ALLEPPEY
itinerary.description:
Your journey continues by crossing the captivating backwaters by traditional houseboat accompanied by like-minded explorers. Travelling in a group is a great way to discover Kerala with people you'd otherwise never meet.

After breakfast, depart for Alleppey. Set out in style on an enchanting one night cruise. While lounging in these floating villas, you will feel like invaders of the backwaters, with the vibrant sights of Kerala skimming by. Look out for the huge Chinese fishing nets, paddy fields, and locals making coir products, rustic buildings, ancient temples and dense coconut trees. Local fish plucked from the backwaters, grilled in spices and lemon will be served on board.

OVERNIGHT ON THE HOUSEBOAT

itinerary.title: ALLEPPEY-KOVALAM- POOVAR ( 200 Kms )
itinerary.description:
After breakfast check out from the houseboat and drive to Poovar. Poovar is one among the natural wonders where the Lake, River, Sea and Beach meet the land. Poovar is unspoilt and unexplored, envoloped by the most serene backwaters, and opening out to the sea and a dream golden sand beach. It is basically a fishing village and the only way to reach Poovar is through water. Known for its pristine and virginal beauty, Poovar Beach is haunted by those who love solitude. It is truly a window into paradise.

On the way to Poovar you will visit two major beaches in Kovalam-- Hawa beach and Samudra Beach.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Breakfast is at the hotel with the rest of the day at leisure. End your holiday on this wonderful paradise by treating yourself to an Ayurvedic Massage where the body is revived and the soul replenished. Please book your slot.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Breakfast is at the hotel with the rest of the day at leisure. Later board a traditional sunset boat and spend the afternoon cruising through the mangroves of Poovar Island. Relax and enjoy the passing landscapes, spotting the golden beach of Poovar Island, floating restaurants and then continue to the canals and backwaters. 

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR - TRIVANDRUM AIRPORT (35 Kms)
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the hotel. A beautiful souvenir is the perfect way to end such a magical holiday. Take home a silk sari or handmade version, its motif conveying Kerala’s everlasting memories. 

BOARD YOUR RETURN FLIGHT HOME

 The Casino Hotel is not just one of Cochin's landmarks, it's also a fine introduction to the charms of the city, and indeed, of Kerala. Here, you can get an authentic taste of Kerala's colourful life, diverse cultures and eclectic cuisine. The hotel is 6 km from Ernakulam and a 10 minute ferry ride to Fort Cochin and Mattancherry.

The Casino Hotel features regular rooms, spaciously appointed suites and a separate non-smoking wing on the ground floor. Many guests love whiling away a relaxed hour or two in the Casino Hotel's sunlit, spacious lounge. This is the heart of th

title: 11 Nights In Nepal And Golden Triangle Of India
description:
Nepal is a twinkling paradise of mystical mountain ranges and large, sloping plains which are home to an impressive range of wildlife, religious heritage and cultural charm. Tourists flock from all over the globe to this impressive central Himalayan country not only because of the interesting Hindu and Buddhist heritage flowing through its veins, but also because it houses eight of the world’s tallest mountains, including the jaw-dropping, snow-capped Mount Everest. It’s no wonder why Nepal features only too often on the bucket-lists of mountaineers, rock climbers and thrill-seekers alike.

Your 11 night trip starts in style in Kathmandu, Nepal’s largest metropolis and a hub of the country’s history, art, culture and economy. As a gateway to the Nepalese Himalayas,  Kathmandu bursts with exotic landscapes and dazzling monuments, such as the Pashupatinath Temple Complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site which,along with the Buddhist Bouddhanath holy shrine and Kathmandu Durbar Square, you’ll have the chance to scope out on your second day. You’ll also have the amazing opportunity to visit Patan, also known as Lalitpur, one of the towns which make up the Kathmandu Valley, where you’ll stand in awe at the beauty of the Monkey Temple and find yourself drawn to the enticingly beautiful Tibetan carpets on sale within the Tibetan refugee center.

After a jam-packed couple of days exploring Kathmandu’s hidden crevices and architectural sensations, you’ll set off for Nagarkot, a town located 32km from Kathmandu which offers maybe the best views of the Himalayas in all of the Bhaktapur District, as well as a panoramic view of the Kathmandu Valley. At sunrise, watch as Mount Everest and other peaks of the Himalayan range come into view, startlingly crystal clear, and then head off for a hike into the heart of all of this incredibly beautiful nature.

From Nagarkot, you’ll head to Bandipur, another hill-top town offering an abundance of hiking routes and fabulous views from elevated viewpoints. The quaint Old Inn in Bandipur will also be your home for a night.

Later you’ll take a trip to Pokhara, the largest city of Nepal and located 200 km west of Kathmandu. Standing a mere 15-35 miles away from the Annapurna mountain range which contains three of the ten highest mountains in the world, Pokhara often acts as a base for trekkers and offers fabulous views of the Himalayas on a clear day. During your time here, you’ll wander the Bindebasini Temple, explore the area of the bazaar, discover Devi’s Fall and end your day in style with a peaceful boat ride on the Phewa Lake.

The second portion of
 your trip will see you touch down in Delhi, India. From Delhi, you will
 be taken to the Mughal city Agra through Yamuna Expressway without 
complaining about an exhaustive journey through crammed roads. In Agra, 
you can find the UNESCO World Heritage Sites of Agra Fort however the 
most striking has to be the impossibly beautiful Taj Mahal which you’ll 
experience on day 9 of your trip. This fabulous building, built by 
Emperor Shah Jahan, is one of India’s most iconic symbols, displayed on 
millions of postcards and travel guides worldwide. If you happen to tire
 of its stunning backdrop and taking photos, Agra also offers a myriad 
of fascinating tombs and mausoleums, left behind by the Mughal Empire.

The
 trip concludes with a two night stay in the Pink City of Jaipur, the 
capital of the Indian State of Rajasthan. In 2015, TripAdvisor ranked 
this unparalleled jewel of Northern India as the number one Indian 
destination for the year in its Traveller’s Choice Awards and it doesn’t
 take a genius to work out why. Its colorful flamboyancy is echoed 
through landmarks and monuments such as the honey-colored Hawa Mahal, a 
five storey pyramid-shaped monument, as well as the astronomical 
observatory of Jantar Mantar which, along with Amer Fort, Jaipur’s star 
attraction, are classed as World Heritage Sites.

Towards the end 
of the trip you will visit the capital city Delhi. Delhi offers a wealth
 of temple complexes and shopping destinations, as well as three UNESCO 
World Heritage Sites (the Qutub Complex, Red Fort and Humayun’s Tomb) 
and the India Gate, a memorial to the soldiers of the British Indian 
Army who perished during WWI. After marveling at these impressive 
landmarks, as well as the Lotus Temple, the Raj Ghat and Chandni Chowk 
(a marketplace) you’ll then tear through the streets of Delhi on a 
rickshaw ride like a true Delhiite.

Tourists flock from all over the globe to this impressive central Himalayan country not only because of the interesting Hindu and Buddhist heritage flowing through its veins, but also because it houses eight of the world’s tallest mountains, including the jaw-dropping, snow-capped Mount Everest. It’s no wonder why Nepal features only too often on the bucket-lists of mountaineers, rock climbers and thrill-seekers alike.

itinerary.title: KATHMANDU
itinerary.description:
Welcome to Nepal! Our representative will meet and greet you at the airport and transfer you to the hotel. Kathmandu is Nepal’s capital and offers an abundance of adventure, fun and cultural delights for even the most seasoned traveler, particularly within the Kathmandu Valley, the political and cultural hub of the kingdom. Later head to the Bouddhanath, a holy shrine where Buddhists come for a pilgrimage. Then, you’ll make your way to Kathmandu Durbar Square. 

OVERNIGHT IN KATHMANDU

itinerary.title: KATHMANDU / NAGARKOT
itinerary.description:
Today make your way to Nagarkot, situated 32 kilometers east of 
Kathmandu at an altitude of 2.175 meters, meaning you’ll be on the road 
for roughly 1.5 hours. On your way there, you’ll stop off at Bhaktaur 
Durbar Square. When you reach Nagarkot you’ll be blown away by a 
panoramic view of the major peaks including Mount Everest. It is an 
outstanding hilltop that offers great views of Himalayas, breathtaking 
mountain scenery and the most impressive views of sunrise and sunset OR 
take an optional 2 nights visit to Chitwan National Park.

OVERNIGHT IN NAGARKOT

itinerary.title: NAGARKOT / BANDIPUR
itinerary.description:
Breakfast at hotel. After breakfast proceed to Bandipur, which 
takes near about 4-5 hours under normal road condition. Bandipur is a 
hilltop settlement and a rural municipality, which is located at 
elevation of 1030m. Due to its, preserved old time culture and 
atmosphere, Bandipur is one of the attractions of tourists.

OVERNIGHT IN BANDIPUR

itinerary.title: BANDIPUR / POKHARA
itinerary.description:
Next up is a visit to the Bindebasini Temple, a two-tiered temple 
dedicated to the Goddess Durga which is one of the oldest in Pokhara. 
Presenting a stunning view of the Himalaya on an uncloudy day, the 
temple radiates beauty and is located in the area of the bazaar. 2km 
southwest of Pokhara you’ll also find Devi’s Fall, also known as Patale 
Chango. This waterfall is beautiful to watch, particularly during the 
summer and rainy season, when the droplets soothe the rocks with their 
brisk landing. 

Towards the other side of Devi’s Fall, you’ll 
see Gupteshwor Cave which contains natural forms made from limestone 
deposits, although unfortunately photography is strictly prohibited 
here. Later on, you’ll be taken on a boat ride through the Phewa Lake 
and then stay overnight at the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN POKHARA

itinerary.title: POKHARA / SARANGKOT
itinerary.description:
From Pokhara, Sarangkot is the most popular tourist destination, from
 where you can enjoy the great view of the Pokhara valley and the 
magnificient view of the mountains. Sarangkot, which is at the altitude 
of 1592m, is the hightest view point for sunrise. It is also one of the 
famous destination for paragliding. After Sarangkot sightseeing tour, 
back to Pokhara city for half day city tour which includes boat ride in 
Fewa Lake, visit to Davis Falls, Gupteshwor Cave, Mahendra Cave and Bat 
Cave.

OVERNIGHT IN POKHARA

itinerary.title: POKHARA / KATHMANDU
itinerary.description:
Breakfast at hotel. After breakfast, proceed back to Kathmandu. On 
the way to Kathmandu, you can enjoy the scenic view of mountains, 
cultural villages, Trishuli river (Famous for rafting) and many other 
magnificent and panoramic views.

After arriving Kathmandu, we start with the sightseeing tour of Kathmandu city and Swayambhunath.

OVERNIGHT IN KATHMANDU

itinerary.title: KATHMANDU/ AIRPORT—ARRIVE DELHI--AGRA
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll drive for 1.5 hours to the airport to board your 
flight from Kathmandu to Delhi. Once in Delhi, you will be taken to the 
Mughal city Agra through Yamuna Expressway which is a 6-lane, 205 km 
long, controlled-access expressway.  In the afternoon visit the 
beautiful Agra Fort, built by the great Emperor Akbar. Evening will be 
at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN AGRA

itinerary.title: AGRA - JAIPUR BY SURFACE
itinerary.description:
After a delicious breakfast, visit the beautiful Taj Mahal, one of 
the most beautiful monuments and one of the wonders of the world.It is 
also synonymous to love and romance. Later you’ll drive to the Pink City
 of Jaipur and check in to your next hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll really get to experience the magic of Jaipur to the 
maximum during a full day sightseeing tour. You’ll drive past the Hawa 
Mahal and in the afternoon you’ll explore the city palace and museum and
 walk adjacent the astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar.  In the 
evening, you’ll visit the Amer Fort for an incredible light and sound 
show.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR-DELHI BY SURFACE
itinerary.description:
After a delicious breakfast, you’ll drive to the capital city 
Delhi. Explore the sites of Old Delhi, such as the Raj Ghat, Red Fort 
and Chandni Chowk, and you’ll also get to enjoy a rickshaw ride. 

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll take a wander around all that New Delhi has to offer, 
such as the Qutub Minar, the India Gate, the Rastrapathi Bhawan, the 
Humayun Tomb and the Lotus Temple. You will also visit Dilli Haat which 
is an is an open aircraft bazaar cum food plaza. It severs a single 
destination to shop for arts and crafts product as well as enjoy 
delicious dishes along with cultural activities conducted here. The 
handicrafts offered here are commendable as it includes sandalwood and 
rosewood carvings, brass ware products, metal ware, jewelry made from 
gems, camel hide footwear etc.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI-UK FLIGHT
itinerary.description:
Get ready to say goodbye! After breakfast you’ll be transferred to 
Delhi Airport to catch your flight back to the UK. We hope you’ve 
enjoyed your trip!

END OF TOUR

title: 7 Nights India Golden Triangle With Breakfast
description:
The Golden Triangle is a circuit that connects the cities of Delhi, Agra and Jaipur – three of India’s most popular destinations due to their abundance of history and culture (hence the aptly titled “golden” triangle). 

As such, travelling around the circuit is a great way to experience the many different facets of this magical country – so we’ve crammed in as much as possible into this week-long getaway. 

Kicking off with a two-night stay in Delhi (India’s capital territory), you’ll enjoy back-to-back visits to the old-fashioned and new-fangled parts of the city. Get up close to the Mughal-era Red Fort, the imposing India Gate and then take a rip-roaring rickshaw ride through Chandni Chowk Market. 

Then, spend another two nights on the banks of the Yamuna River in the magical city of Agra, home to the iconic Taj Mahal. Travellers flock here like bees to honey – such is the allure of this white marble mausoleum which you’ll get to see on day four of your trip.

The “Pink City” of Jaipur will be your home for the last leg of this trip. This sun-soaked city, bleached a dazzling pinkish colour, is the Rajasthan state’s capital. It’s home to opulent courtyards, gardens and royal buildings – not to mention the sandy-coloured Amer Fort that has its own light and sound show.
excerpt: The Golden Triangle is a circuit that connects the cities of Delhi, Agra and Jaipur – three of India’s most popular destinations due to their abundance of history and culture (hence the aptly titled “golden” triangle).

highlights: Marvel at the glistening, time-ravaged architecture of Old Delhi – with visits to Gandhi’s memorial (also known as Raj Ghat), the Red Fort and the famous Chandni Chowk marketplace too.
Get up close to India’s tallest stone tower (Qutub Minar) and the tomb of the Emperor Humayun when you swap the old-fashioned for the new-fangled with a visit to New Delhi.
Discover the historic city of Agra in the state of Uttar Pradesh. Visit its two most famous attractions: Agra Fort and the Taj Mahal.
Venture to Amer Fort to watch a sound and light show in the “Pink City” of Jaipur, home to opulent palaces, gardens and museums.

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL IN DELHI
itinerary.description:
Welcome to the Indian capital of New Delhi! One of our representatives will meet you at the airport, ready to take you to your accommodation. The rest of the day is free for you to spend however you please. 

OVERNIGHT STAY IN DELHI

itinerary.title: SIGHTSEEING IN DELHI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, you’ll head to Old Delhi – visiting the likes of the Raj Ghat (Gandhi memorial), the Red Fort (historic monument) and also taking a rickshaw ride through Chandni Chowk (marketplace). 
After that, you’ll visit New Delhi and get up close to the area’s iconic landmarks such as the Qutub Minar (India’s tallest stone tower), the India Gate, the Rastrapathi Bhawan (the Indian president’s residence), Humayun Tomb (Humayun was the second emperor of the Mughal Empire) and the Lotus Temple. 

OVERNIGHT STAY IN DELHI

itinerary.title: FROM DELHI TO AGRA
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, take a transfer to the beautiful city of Agra, sitting on the banks of the Yamuna River.

OVERNIGHT STAY IN AGRA

itinerary.title: SIGHTSEEING IN AGRA
itinerary.description:
Following a hearty hotel breakfast, you’ll head to Agra Fort, the main residence of the emperors of the Mughal Dynasty until the early 1600s. Next, make your way to the iconic Taj Mahal – a white marble mausoleum built by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife, Mumtaz Mahal. It is the ultimate symbol of love. 

In the late evening, head back to your hotel.

OVERNIGHT STAY IN AGRA

itinerary.title: DRIVE FROM AGRA TO JAIPUR
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, we’ll drive you to Jaipur so you can check into your next hotel. The capital of the Rajasthan state, Jaipur is known as the “Pink City” due to the pinkish tones of the architecture and monuments. 

In the evening, get ready for a visit to Amer Fort to watch the famous light and sound show.

OVERNIGHT STAY IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: CITY TOUR OF JAIPUR
itinerary.description:
Today, you’ll spend the morning on a full day sightseeing tour of Jaipur. One of your stop-offs will include the Hawa Mahal-a (the Palace of Winds). Then, in the afternoon, head to the City Palace and Museum and walk alongside the phenomenal astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar. 

OVERNIGHT STAY IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: FROM JAIPUR TO DELHI
itinerary.description:
After having breakfast, you’ll be driven back to Delhi for an overnight stay.

OVERNIGHT STAY IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DEPARTURE
itinerary.description:
In the morning, we’ll take you to Delhi Airport to catch your departure flight home. We hope you’ve had a lovely time exploring the cities of the Golden Triangle! 

END OF TOUR

title: Golden Triangle & Rajasthan with Tiger Safari
description:
This tour offers a fascinating glimpse of India, encompassing the forts, palaces and magnificent monuments of the golden triangle with the enchanting sights and sounds of Rajasthan.

As Lonely Planet so perfectly sums up; “Against a backdrop of palaces and forts, India puts on its greatest show”. And we’ve added more wow factor with safaris and folk dances! 

Your journey of a lifetime begins in Delhi where culture, history and escapism blend to such dazzling effect. The title a “Tale of Two Cities” is apt as Delhi bridges together two different worlds. Awaken the senses with an exhilarating rickshaw ride through lively Chandni Chowk.

Next up, discover a love poem in stone at Agra’s romantic Taj Mahal that radiates like a golden trinket underneath the blazing sun. The finest example of archaeological brilliance you will ever see! Enjoy the memorable experience of entering Amber Fort.

Move beyond and you will uncover an India of yet deeper diversity. Ranthambore’s renowned Tiger Forest and its UNESCO fort, home to the mightiest tigress. In Pushkar you will visit the Hindu temple of Bhrama. 

Your tour concludes with a stay in one of the most beautiful and enchanting cities in India: Udaipur. Here you will take a boat ride across Lake Pichola to wonder at the palaces that rise up from the shore like a soaring cliff of marble: a truly memorable way to end this trip to the gems of India.
excerpt: This tour offers a fascinating glimpse of India, encompassing the forts, palaces and magnificent monuments of the golden triangle with the enchanting sights and sounds of Rajasthan.

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL DELHI
itinerary.description:
Your journey of a lifetime begins in New Delhi. Arrive at the international airport, where you’ll be met and transferred to your hotel. It is recommended that you spend your first day at leisure to recuperate from your international journey.

Few places on earth combine beauty, history and escapism to such dazzling effect as the great city of Delhi- the sprawling and fascinating capital of India. The title “A Tale of Two Cities” is apt as Delhi bridges together two different worlds. The Old Delhi, once the capital of Islamic India, contrasts with the British-built centre of New Delhi.  On this journey you will scratch the surface and discover a land sprinkled with dazzling gems of India’s “golden” legacy.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, a daytime tour takes you through the Indian Capital. In Old Delhi visit the legendary Red Fort and Raj Ghat where Mahatma Gandhi was cremated. Then awaken the senses with an exhilarating rickshaw ride through the bustling Chandni Chowk and its back roads.

Experience the Art Metro Train Ride in Delhi with a Guide. Quick, air-conditioned, Delhi’s new metro is the smart way to take in the history, colors and flavors of this vibrant city. The silvery, sleek new metro system, in its latest stage of expansion, makes the city a much more straightforward place to visit, allowing passengers to zip about in air-conditioned comfort, avoiding its notoriously polluted and congested roads.

New Delhi offers a fascinating contrast: here, you will stroll along the wide, straight boulevards and pass the monuments of the Empire. Walk to the tallest stone tower in India, Qutub Minar and continue to the mighty India Gate. Designed by Sir Edwin Lutyens, the gate is a memorial to the 82,000 soldiers from the undivided British Indian Army who died in 1914-21 during the First World War. In testimonial to their immense bravery, 13,300 names are inscribed on the monumental gate. You will also see the Amar Jawan Jyoti, built following the Bangladesh Liberation war, bounded by four eternal flames beneath the Memorial Archway. Continue to the Rastrapathi Bhawan, Humayun Tomb and the Lotus Temple. Return to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI – AGRA
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, get ready to experience the Taj Express way from Delhi to the Mughal city of Agra and your hotel for check-in. Once the most important city in India, it was subjected to a great deal of ambitious
architecture. Babur, founder of the Mughal Empire, introduced the first Persian garden on the banks of Yamuna River.

In the afternoon check in and relax at the hotel. In the evening feel the romance in the air as we arrive at the spectacular Taj Mahal. 

Discover a love poem in stone as you behold the sight of the sun
striking its cupid arrow onto the huge golden trinket. The Taj was built by Shah Jahan as the resting place for his beloved Queen Mumtaz
Mahal. What else but great love could have inspired such outstanding beauty? Late evening transfer to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN AGRA

itinerary.title: AGRA – RANTHAMBORE
itinerary.description:
This morning after breakfast you will drive to Ranthambore. Once you reach Ranthambore, check in to your hotel and rest of the day will be at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN RANTHAMBORE

itinerary.title: RANTHAMBORE
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, visit the Tiger Forest. The park is famous for being the best place in India to get close to free-ranging tigers. Located in the Sawai Madhopur district of Rajasthan, the 400 sq km reserve, described as the “tiger friendly land” is home to approx. 30 tigers. Watching them hunt in the dense undergrowth, it is impossible not to be impressed with the sheer size and markings of a fully grown adult, but equally spectacular is seeing how they interact with their cubs. The park is also home to a variety of wildlife, including leopard, Nilgai, wild boar, Sambar, hyena, sloth bear and Chital. There are also a host of birds, so keep your eyes open for delicately hued storks, slender and elegant doves, and great white pelicans. In the afternoon, explore the Ranthambore Fort soaring above the jungle.

Founded in 944 and initially named as “Ranath Bhawar Garh” which means “a place of Rajput warriors”, the fort is today territory for the most famous Ranthambore tigress – the Machali. Machali’s daughter T19 is the current queen tigress of the park and gave birth to three surviving cubs. Walk up the steps to the fort to catch a glimpse of the mightiest tiger of Ranthambore. It was here in 1283 AD where ruler Raja Hammir Dev was crowned and controlled Ranthambore until he was defeated by the army of Ala-ud-din Khilji in AD 1301. Once inhabited by the Mughals who used it as a prison fortress, the fort was given back to the Maharaja of Jaipur and remained with them until India gained independence in 1947.

OVERNIGHT IN RANTHAMBORE

itinerary.title: RANTHAMBORE - JAIPUR
itinerary.description:
After breakfast and hotel check-out, you will drive to the Pink City, Jaipur. Check in to your Jaipur hotel. Later we have lined up an evening of drama and atmosphere at the world-famous Sound and Light Show, an entertaining way to discover the history of Jaipur and Amber Fort.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
This morning after breakfast drive past the Hawa Mahal-a Palace of winds. In the afternoon visit the City Palace and museum, before continuing to Jantar Mantar, a structural masterpiece displaying the science of early Indian architecture. Return to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR- PUSHKAR
itinerary.description:
After breakfast and hotel check-out, you will drive to the sacred city of Hindus, Pushkar. Enjoy the ripples on the golden dunes creating mystical mirages, providing a glimpse of the desert lifestyle through remote villages. Pushkar is quite unlike anywhere else in Rajasthan.

It’s a prominent Hindu pilgrimage town and devout Hindus visit at least once in their lifetime. The town envelopes a holy lake, said to have appeared when Brahma dropped a lotus flower. It is home to one of the world’s few Brahma temples. The main street is one long bazaar, selling anything from hippy-chic tie-dye to didgeridoos. Although brimming with commercialism and banana pancakes, the town is still enchantingly small and authentically mystic.

After hotel check-in, there is no better way to start your stay in Pushkar than with a walk around the Brahma Temple. Bhrama is the very God who created the universe and the temple is magnificently painted with red and blue. Here you will visit the complex with its 52 bathing ghats and 400 milky blue temples. The town often hums with puja (prayers) creating an enchanting soundtrack of chanting, drums, and gongs, and devotional songs. Return to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN PUSHKAR

itinerary.title: PUSHKAR – UDAIPUR
itinerary.description:
In the morning, bid farewell to Pushkar and drive to the Blue City, Udaipur. Udaipur sits jewel like on the shores of the Pichola Saga, placid blue lakes, encircled by an amphitheatre of barren hills, ice-whipped palaces and exotic gardens. On arrival, check into the hotel. Arabian Nights a plenty can be found during a leisurely evening walk. From the washing ghats admire a bright orange sun setting over Udaipur’s maze of marble towering over Lake Pichola like a beautiful tapestry. Watch as the last stroke of sunlight sends a blazing ripple through the water, ending in darkness. Delight in delicious Indian cuisine at one of the fine downtown restaurants for an optional dinner. Return to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN UDAIPUR

itinerary.title: UDAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, the day will begin with an enlightening city tour. Udaipur is a place of enchanting scenery, some truly breathtaking edifices, like the City Palace and museum along with the shimmering bronze statue of Maharana Pratap. You will also visit the Jagdish Temple which was built in 1651 AD and the small, quaint ornamental garden, Saheliyon-ki-Bari.

Your two hour afternoon boat ride across Lake Pichola is fittingly spectacular as it allows you to drift past the washing and bathing ghats whilst enjoying picturesque views of the Lake and City Palace. Return to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN UDAIPUR

itinerary.title: UDAIPUR – INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT FOR YOUR RETURN FLIGHT
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, it is time to head home via transfer to the International airport to board your international flight back home or onward flight to your next destination. If time permits, step outside onto the city’s lively streets. A souvenir is the perfect way to end such a magical holiday. 

Between the beautiful buildings, you will find the treasures of overflowing markets.

END OF TOUR

title: Golden Triangle Tour With Tiger Safari And Beach Stay
description:
Golden Triangle India Holiday with Tiger Safari & Goa Beach Extension

Experience the perfect blend of India's cultural treasures, wildlife adventures, and tropical relaxation with this comprehensive 11 Night/12-day holiday package. Journey through the iconic Golden Triangle cities, embark on thrilling tiger safaris in Ranthambore, and unwind on Goa's pristine beaches.

Core Golden Triangle Experience (6 Days/5 Nights)

Delhi - The Historic Capital (2 nights)

Begin your adventure in India's vibrant capital, exploring both Old and New Delhi. Discover the contrasts between the bustling lanes of Chandni Chowk with its exciting rickshaw rides and the grandeur of colonial New Delhi. Key highlights include:

Old Delhi: Raj Ghat, Red Fort, and the atmospheric Chandni Chowk bazaar

New Delhi: Imposing India Gate, Rashtrapati Bhavan, Qutub Minar (India's tallest stone tower), Humayun's Tomb, and the stunning Lotus Temple

Agra - City of Eternal Love (1 night)

Home to one of the world's most romantic monuments, Agra offers unforgettable experiences:

Taj Mahal: The breathtaking symbol of love built by Emperor Shah Jahan for his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal

Agra Fort: The magnificent fortress built by the great Emperor Akbar

Jaipur - The Pink City (2 nights)

Complete your Golden Triangle in Rajasthan's colorful capital:

Amber Fort: Experience the spectacular Light & Sound show

Hawa Mahal: The iconic Palace of Winds

City Palace & Museum: Royal heritage and artifacts

Jantar Mantar: Ancient astronomical observatory showcasing India's scientific heritage

Tiger Safari Extension - Ranthambore (2 nights)

Add the thrill of wildlife to your journey with a stay at the famous Ranthambore National Park:

 

Accommodation: 2 nights at Ranthambore on half-board basis

Safari Experience: Shared canter safari with expert naturalist/guide (upgrade to private jeep safari available)

Wildlife: Opportunity to spot the majestic Bengal tiger in its natural habitat, along with leopards, sloth bears, and diverse birdlife

Beach Relaxation - Goa (4 nights)

End your adventure with pure relaxation on India's most famous beaches:

Accommodation: 4 nights bed & breakfast at Casa De Goa or Ivy Anjuna

Location: Beautiful Anjuna area with pristine beaches, vibrant nightlife, and Portuguese colonial charm

Transfers: All airport transfers included

Journey through the iconic Golden Triangle cities, embark on thrilling tiger safaris in Ranthambore, and unwind on Goa's pristine beaches.

title: Indian Golden Triangle With Holi Colours
description:
Experience the essence of India on this 7-day Golden Triangle with Holi Celebration Tour, a journey that takes you through the country’s most captivating destinations — Agra, Jaipur, and Delhi. Blending history, romance, and vibrant culture, this itinerary showcases the best of North India while immersing you in the joyful spirit of Holi, the festival of colors.

Your adventure begins with a warm welcome in Delhi, India’s bustling capital, before heading to Agra, the city of eternal love. Here, witness the breathtaking Taj Mahal, one of the Seven Wonders of the World, as it glows in the morning light. Explore the imposing Agra Fort, an architectural masterpiece that echoes tales of Mughal grandeur.

Continue to the royal city of Jaipur, known as the Pink City, famed for its regal palaces, vibrant markets, and majestic forts. Visit the Amber Fort, perched on a hilltop, offering panoramic views and a glimpse into Rajasthan’s royal past. Discover the City Palace, a blend of Mughal and Rajasthani architecture; admire the intricately designed Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds); and explore the fascinating Jantar Mantar, an ancient astronomical observatory.

A highlight of your journey is the Holi Celebration in Jaipur — a joyful and colorful experience where you’ll join locals in playing with natural colors, enjoying music, dancing, and savoring festive sweets and delicacies. This unforgettable event offers a truly authentic connection to India’s vibrant traditions and warm hospitality.

After celebrating Holi, travel back to Delhi, where the old and new worlds blend beautifully. Explore Old Delhi’s historical landmarks such as Raj Ghat and Chandni Chowk, where you’ll enjoy an exciting rickshaw ride through its bustling lanes. In New Delhi, visit the towering Qutub Minar, the serene Lotus Temple, Humayun’s Tomb, India Gate, and the stately President’s House (Rashtrapati Bhavan).

Conclude your journey with cherished memories of India’s rich heritage, royal architecture, and festive spirit. With expert-guided tours, comfortable stays, private transfers, and authentic cultural experiences, this Golden Triangle itinerary promises a perfect blend of history, celebration, and unforgettable color.

Blending history, romance, and vibrant culture, this itinerary showcases the best of North India while immersing you in the joyful spirit of Holi, the festival of colors.

highlights: Celebrate Holi in Jaipur – Experience India’s most colorful and joyful festival with music, dance, colors, and traditional delicacies.
Witness the Taj Mahal at Sunrise – Marvel at the world’s most iconic symbol of love glowing in the early morning light.
Explore the Royal Heritage of Jaipur – Discover majestic forts, grand palaces, and vibrant bazaars in the Pink City.
Ride a Rickshaw through Old Delhi – Immerse yourself in the charm and chaos of Chandni Chowk, Delhi’s oldest market.
Attend the Light & Sound Show at Amber Fort – Relive Jaipur’s royal history through a spectacular evening performance.
Seamless Comfort & Expert Guidance – Enjoy handpicked hotels, private transfers, and knowledgeable local guides for a worry-free journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi – Transfer to Agra
itinerary.description:
Welcome to India’s capital city, Delhi! Upon arrival at the airport, meet our representative who will assist you with your transfer to Agra. Check in at your hotel and relax after your journey. The rest of the day is at leisure to unwind or explore Agra on your own.

Overnight stay in Agra

itinerary.title: Agra – Jaipur
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, visit the majestic Taj Mahal, one of the Seven Wonders of the World, built by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal. Continue to the Agra Fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site reflecting Mughal grandeur. By afternoon, drive to the Pink City – Jaipur. On arrival, check in at your hotel and relax.

Overnight stay in Jaipur

itinerary.title: Jaipur City Tour
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, begin your exploration of Jaipur. Drive past the iconic Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds) and visit the City Palace and its museum showcasing royal artifacts. Then, visit Jantar Mantar, an astronomical observatory built by Maharaja Jai Singh II. 

In the afternoon, head to the magnificent Amber Fort, a blend of Hindu and Mughal architecture.In the evening, enjoy the Light & Sound Show at Amer Fort.

Overnight stay in Jaipur

itinerary.title: Jaipur – Holi Celebration
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, immerse yourself in the festival of colors — Holi! Join locals in celebrating this joyous festival with natural colors, music, dance, and traditional sweets. Enjoy lunch during the celebration and experience the cultural vibrance of India.

Overnight stay in Jaipur

itinerary.title: Jaipur – Delhi
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, drive back to Delhi. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and relax. The rest of the day is free for leisure activities or shopping.

Overnight stay in Delhi

itinerary.title: Delhi City Tour
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, explore both Old and New Delhi.Old Delhi: Visit Raj Ghat, dedicated to Mahatma Gandhi, and Chandni Chowk, where you’ll enjoy a thrilling rickshaw ride through its bustling lanes.New Delhi: Visit the towering Qutub Minar, India Gate, President’s House (Rashtrapati Bhavan), Humayun’s Tomb, and the serene Lotus Temple.

Overnight stay in Delhi

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, transfer to Delhi International Airport for your onward flight.

END OF TOUR

title: Kerala Holidays with Golden Triangle
description:
For 5 nights the Golden Triangle circuit, comprised of Delhi, Jaipur and Agra, will be your home. All three of these spellbinding cities bring something different to the circuit and you’ll hop from one to the other via dedicated vehicle for our passengers, safe from the glare of the Indian sun. Delhi, the Indian Capital, is home to three World Heritage Sites, the Qutab Minar, Red Fort and Humayun’s Tomb, as well as the India Gate and Lotus Temple, most of which will feature on your itinerary. Its bustling streets and lively ambiance will mean there’s never a dull moment, but if you’re set on experiencing life like a true Delhite, be sure to hitch a ride on a rickshaw during your stay. 

Your next stop is Jaipur, the capital of the Indian state of Rajasthan and a vibrant hub of art and flamboyancy. The honey-colored Hawa Mahal, the impressive astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar and the World Heritage Site of Amer Fort are three important landmarks which perhaps helped Jaipur gain the status of number one Indian destination in 2015 by Trip Advisor. Then, swap the “Pink City” for Agra and it’s Mughal-era architecture, containing tombs and mausoleums left behind by the Mughal empire. Located in the northern  state of Uttar Pradesh, Agra is also home to the UNESCO World Heritage Sites of Agra Fort , however its most noteworthy definitely has to be the phenomenal Taj Mahal, a veritable symbol of India which is breathtaking to behold. 

The next leg of your trip will be spent in Kerala for 11 days. When you find yourself in the backwaters of Kerala, in southern India, you'll be astounded at how appropriate that saying is with the scenery before you. It’s not without reason that Kerala is known as‘God’s own country’. A paradise, nestled between the Western Ghats and the overwhelmingly beautiful Arabian Sea and Indian Ocean, Kerala has a little bit of everything that India has to offer.

For many travellers, Kerala is South India's most serenely beautiful state. Besides its famous backwaters, elegant houseboats, ayurvedic treatments and delicately spiced, taste-bud-tingling cuisine, Kerala is home to wild elephants, exotic birds and the odd tiger, while vibrant traditions such as Kathakali plays, temple festivals and snake-boat races frequently bring even the smallest villages to life.

And when you can do all of this for as little as only £2899 per person, you surely need no reason to pack your bags and find yourself on the ultimate holiday.

Delhi, the Indian Capital, is home to three World Heritage Sites, the Qutab Minar, Red Fort and Humayun’s Tomb, as well as the India Gate and Lotus Temple, most of which will feature on your itinerary. Its bustling streets and lively ambiance will mean there’s never a dull moment, but if you’re set on experiencing life like a true Delhite, be sure to hitch a ride on a rickshaw during your stay.

itinerary.title: ARRIVE DELHI
itinerary.description:
Your journey of a lifetime begins in New Delhi. Arrive at the international airport, where you’ll be met and transferred to your hotel. It is recommended that you spend your first evening at leisure in your hotel to recuperate from your international journey. Few places on earth combine beauty, history and escapism to such dazzling effect as the great city of Delhi- the sprawling and fascinating capital of India. The title “A Tale of Two Cities” is apt as Delhi bridges together two different worlds. The Old Delhi, once the capital of Islamic India, contrasts with the British-built centre of New Delhi. On this journey you will scratch the surface and discover a land sprinkled with dazzling gems of India’s “golden” legacy.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, your visit will include a tour of Old Delhi and the immense Jama Masjid, the greatest mosque in India. You will also see the legendary Red Fort and Raj Ghat where Mahatma Gandhi was cremated. Finally, awaken the senses with a thrilling rickshaw ride through the busy markets of Chandni Chowk.

Also visit Rajghat - memorial of Mahatma Gandhi & go on a rickshaw ride through the silver street in Chandni Chowk. New Delhi offers a fascinating contrast: here, you will walk the wide, straight boulevards and pass the monuments of the Empire. Walking the imperial residence, Rashtrapati Bhavan, to the mighty arch of India Gate, designed by Edwin Lutyens in the 1920s, the eternal flame burns day and night to remember the soldiers in the Indo-Pakistan war. Continue to the Laxmi Narayan Temple, Humayun’s Tomb and the Qutub Minar. Another highlight is the Bahai Temple with its lotus dome.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: DELHI-AGRA
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll drive to the Mughal city Agra through Yamuna Expressway which is a 6-lane, 205 km long, controlled-access expressway. In the afternoon visit the beautiful Agra Fort, built by the great Emperor Akbar.

Evening will be at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN AGRA

itinerary.title: AGRA - JAIPUR BY SURFACE
itinerary.description:
After a delicious breakfast, visit the beautiful Taj Mahal, one of the most beautiful monuments and one of the wonders of the world. It is also synonymous to love and romance.

Later you’ll drive to the Pink City of Jaipur and check in to your next hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
Today you’ll really get to experience the magic of Jaipur to the maximum during a full day sightseeing tour. You’ll drive past the Hawa Mahal and in the afternoon you’ll explore the city palace and museum and walk adjacent the astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar.

In the evening, you’ll visit the Amer Fort for an incredible light and sound show.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR TO DELHI BY SURFACE--DELHI TO KOCHI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, you will head towards Delhi. You will then be transferred to the airport for your flight to 
Kochi. On arrival in Kochi you 
will be met by another representative & will be transferred to your 
hotel. Rest of the evening at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Unlock Kochi’s history by embarking on a morning sightseeing tour 
that will take you into the heart of this magnificent and beautiful 
city. The Dutch, British, French and Portuguese have all left their 
outstanding imprint with beautiful architecture. You will visit Fort 
Cochin, which has been a cherished sea port for centuries, Vasco Da 
Gama Square and Santa Cruz Basilica. Continue to St. Francis’ Church, considered to be India’s oldest European church, built by Portuguese Franciscan Friars. Vasco da Gama was buried here before being taken to Lisbon. 

Proceed to the VOC Gate, Dutch Palace, Jewish Synagogue and marvel at Mattancherry Palace’s murals depicting the Ramayana and Puranic legends. Intriguingly, the only traces of China’s influence are the gigantic nets, ‘cheenavalas’ from 1350. According to Diplomat Ma Haun, “The fishermen are tourist-friendly and will happily let you join them.” And his observation still rings true today. You will learn about their very unusual fishing method, operated from the shore. In the afternoon, experience your very own pièce de résistance with a cooking class from a renowned local TV personality. At lunch, enjoy the flavours of Keralite cooking. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI – MUNNAR
itinerary.description:
Check out after a hearty breakfast and drive to Munnar over rugged 
hills, rolling oceans of tubby green tea bushes, through luscious grasslands, before passing the Pallivasal tea estates. This will be 
your first glimpse of Munnar. On arrival, check-in at the hotel.

At a height of 1,500 metre, set amidst mountains once known as the 
High Range of Travancore, Munnar is a bustling hub of tea 
plantations. Prepare to be captivated by its dense carpet of tea, 
sprinkled with Acacias and Red Gum trees, and delighted by its 
freshly brewed cuppas.

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: MUNNAR
itinerary.description:
Continue with a full day sightseeing of Munnar, watching the mountain goats in the Rajmalai Wild Life Sanctuary. Then head to the the Kannan Devan Hills, the perfect getaway with panoramic views of the Munnar Tea Estates and the lake. After lunch you will proceed to 
Mattupetty, with the rare chance of seeing a herd of wild elephants 
on the way to Mattupetty Dam.

 Next up is the Tea Museum’s photographs and machinery during the revolution of the industry, which includes the original tea roller, the ‘Rotovane,’ and the ‘Pelton Wheel’. Today you will also experience the Kathakali & Martial Arts show.

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: MUNNAR-THEKKADY (3 HRS DRIVE)
itinerary.description:
Continue with a full day sightseeing of Munnar, watching the mountain goats in the Rajmalai Wild Life Sanctuary. Then head to the the Kannan Devan Hills, the perfect getaway with panoramic views of the Munnar Tea Estates and the lake. After lunch you will proceed to Mattupetty, with the rare chance of seeing a herd of wild elephants on the way to Mattupetty Dam.

Next up is the Tea Museum’s photographs and machinery during the revolution of the industry, which includes the original tea roller, the ‘Rotovane,’ and the ‘Pelton Wheel’. Today you will also experience the Kathakali & Martial Arts show.

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: EXPLORE THEKKADY
itinerary.description:
Continue with a full day sightseeing of Munnar, watching the mountain goats in the Rajmalai Wild Life Sanctuary. Then head to the the Kannan Devan Hills, the perfect getaway with panoramic views of the Munnar Tea Estates and the lake. After lunch you will proceed to Mattupetty, with the rare chance of seeing a herd of wild elephants on the way to Mattupetty Dam.

Next up is the Tea Museum’s photographs and machinery during the revolution of the industry, which includes the original tea roller, the ‘Rotovane,’ and the ‘Pelton Wheel’. Today you will also experience the Kathakali & Martial Arts show.

OVERNIGHT IN MUNNAR

itinerary.title: THEKKADY-ALLEPPEY
itinerary.description:
Post breakfast, check out of the hotel and depart to Alleppey via Kuttikanam which is a hill station in Idukki district and is best known for trekking and adventure sports.

You can explore the cardamom and tea plantations which surround the entire place. Later head to Alleppey and experience the memorable cruise through the iconic backwaters of Kerala. Alleppey is the most popular backwater cruising destination in Kerala and is termed as “Venice of the East”. Kerala claims its title as ‘God’s Own  Country’ due to its captivating charm and wonder.

Your comfortable and luxurious house boat will take you through paddy fields and lined up coconut trees. Indulge in some traditional Kerala cuisines served on board made with various tasty spices and flavours.

Marvel the Chinese fishing nets, ancient temples, rustic buildings, paddy fields and swaying coconut trees along the coastline. Having experienced an overnight stay in a houseboat, you have lived a perfect vacation in Kerala

OVERNIGHT ON THE HOUSEBOAT

itinerary.title: ALLEPPEY--MARARI (Kochi) - 35 Minute Drive
itinerary.description:
Enjoy a delicious breakfast before checking out of your houseboat at around 10am and heading to Marari Beach. En route, visit the Coir Village and Coir Factory. (“Coir” is a natural fibre made from the husk of a coconut and dates back to the 1800s.) 

Afterwards, make your way to the St Andrews Basilica in Arthunkal – Kerala’s 7th basilica and the first ever in Alleppey, making it the most popular church in the Alappuzha district. Believed to possess the power to heal mental and physical disorders, the church was first constructed in the 16th century but has been renovated several times. 

You’ll then continue to Marari Beach and check into the beach resort.

 OVERNIGHT STAY IN MARARI BEACH

itinerary.title: RELAX IN MARARI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, kick off your day with some sightseeing. Your first stop will be the Velorvattom Mahadeva Temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva and believed to have been built some 700 years ago. 

Later, proceed to the Mararikulam Mahadeva Temple, said to be the only temple in Kerala where Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati were seen sitting face to face. Devotees believe that a visit to this temple will make your wishes come true. 

Afterwards, you’ll have the chance to visit the local markets, go on a stroll or bike ride through the fishing villages before heading back to the beach resort. 

OVERNIGHT STAY IN MARARI BEACH

itinerary.title: RELAX IN MARARI
itinerary.description:
Take some time to relax at your accommodation after a tasty breakfast. Today there is no set itinerary so you’re able to explore Marari Beach at your own pace. 

This is one of Kerala’s best beaches; it’s tranquil, fringed by coconut groves and there’s sand, sand, sand as far as the eye can see.

 OVERNIGHT STAY IN MARARI BEACH

itinerary.title: MARARI - KOCHI AIRPORT
itinerary.description: After a hearty breakfast, check out of your hotel and head to Kochi Airport to board your flight home. We hope you’ve had a brilliant stay in Kerala! END OF TOUR

title: Luxury And Heritage Golden Triangle With Goa Beach Stay
description:
Our Luxury and Heritage
Golden Triangle with Goa Beach Stay:

Journey through the heart of North India, exploring the iconic trio that forms the 'Golden Triangle': Delhi, Agra, and Jaipur. Each city offers a unique blend of old-world charm and modern dynamism, serving as a testament to India's rich history, diverse cultures, and the promise of its future.

Delhi: Begin in the capital city, where ancient landmarks coexist with bustling bazaars and modern infrastructure. Explore the Red Fort's grandeur and the Lotus Temple's serene  nvironment. Dive deep into local life at the vibrant Chandni Chowk. Pay respects at Rajghat, the memorial of Mahatma Gandhi.

Agra: Venture to the city of eternal love. Marvel at the timeless beauty of the Taj Mahal. Visit the Agra Fort, showcasing Mughal architectural splendour. Browse through local markets with exquisite marble handicrafts and traditional sweets.

Jaipur: Dive into the regal vibes of the Pink City. Revel in the majesty of the Amber Fort with its breathtaking hilltop views. Wander through the pink-hued streets and visit the iconic Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds). Experience local art and craft workshops, understanding the intricate art of block printing and gemstone cutting.

Goa Finale: As the tour ends, take a flight to the western coast, settling in the lap of Goa - a paradise that combines pristine beaches with a laid-back, bohemian ambience. 

Concluding your tour in Goa, the melange of experiences from the culturally rich triangle to the serene vibes of the Arabian Sea promises a journey that touches every facet of India's
vast and vibrant tapestry.

Full details of this tour can be seen below and in the itinerary section.
excerpt: Journey through the heart of North India, exploring the iconic trio that forms the 'Golden Triangle': Delhi, Agra, and Jaipur. Each city offers a unique blend of old-world charm and modern dynamism, serving as a testament to India's rich history, diverse cultures, and the promise of its future.

Hotel Narain Niwas Palace, Jaipur

 The Narain Niwas Hotel is counted as one of the finest heritage hotels in Rajasthan. A great example of the marriage of contemporary and traditionalism, this property boasts of impeccable services, an elegant decor and an attractive color palette- a visual delight for the guests.

Included in the list of the famous heritage hotels in India, Narain Niwas Palace was well-maintained 37 air-conditioned rooms that boast of art-Deco furniture. title: Royal Rajasthan Heritage Stay
description:
This tour offers a fascinating glimpse of India, encompassing the forts, palaces and magnificent monuments of the golden triangle with the enchanting sights and sounds of Rajasthan.

Your journey of a lifetime begins with discovering a love poem in stone at Agra’s romantic Taj Mahal that radiates like a golden trinket underneath the blazing sun. The finest example of archaeological brilliance you will ever see! Enjoy the memorable experience of entering Amer Fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Your next stop- Jaipur, the capital of the Indian state of Rajasthan and a vibrant hub of art and flamboyancy. The honey-colored Hawa Mahal and the impressive astronomical observatory of Jantar Mantar are the important landmarks which perhaps helped Jaipur gain the status of number one Indian destination in 2015 by TripAdvisor.

Move beyond, and you will uncover an India of yet deeper diversity. While in Rajasthan, experience your visit in Mandawa, known as an open art gallery. Along with it comes the beautiful Havelis and Forts of Bikaner and Jaisalmer. Then visit the Kumbalgarh fort, India's own 'Great Wall', it's the second largest wall after the 'Great Wall of China.'

Your tour concludes with a stay in one of the most beautiful and enchanting cities in India: Udaipur. Here you will visit an ornamental garden Saheliyon Ki Bari and take a boat ride across Lake Pichola to wonder at the palaces that rise from the shore like a soaring cliff of marble: a memorable way to end this trip to the gems of India.
excerpt: This tour offers a fascinating glimpse of India, encompassing the forts, palaces and magnificent monuments of the golden triangle with the enchanting sights and sounds of Rajasthan.

highlights: Stay in some of India’s most beautiful hotels filled will treasures
Visit the immense Junagarh Fort in Bikaner
Enjoy folk dancing and regional cuisine, Jaisalmer
Venture to Jodhpur’s iconic Mehrangarh Fort
Boat across Lake Pichola for epic views of Udaipur

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL IN DELHI
itinerary.description:
On your arrival in Delhi, you will be picked up from the airport and transferred to the selected hotel where you'll stay overnight.

OVERNIGHT IN DELHI

itinerary.title: Delhi Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Your day exploring Delhi's landmarks will be unforgettable! Start at Qutub Minar, the tallest brick minaret globally, with intricate carvings and historical significance. Head to Humayun's Tomb next, where you can admire the stunning Mughal architecture and serene gardens. Finish at Chandni Chowk, Delhi's bustling market, where you can experience the vibrant local culture, shop for unique items, and savor delicious street food.

itinerary.title: DEIHI – AGRA 225 KMS, 4 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description: This morning after breakfast transfer to Agra either by Taj express way in a car Delhi to Agra. This afternoon visit the Agra fort, built by the great Emperor Akbar, and the famous Taj Mahal-the symbol of love, built by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal. By late evening return to your hotel for overnight stay. OVERNIGHT IN AGRA

itinerary.title: AGRA - JAIPUR- 240 KMS, 5 HRS
itinerary.description:
After breakfast transfer to Jaipur. Check-in at your hotel. In the evening, visit the Amer Fort for the light & Sound show.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
This morning after breakfast drive past the Hawa Mahal, a Palace of winds. In the afternoon, you will visit the city palace, museum, and a walk adjacent to Jantar Mantar. In the afternoon, visit Jhalana reserve forest for leopard sighting safari.

OVERNIGHT IN JAIPUR

itinerary.title: JAIPUR – MANDAWA- 168 KMS 3 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description:
After breakfast drive towards Mandawa.

OVERNIGHT MANDAWA

itinerary.title: MANDAWA
itinerary.description:
Drive to Mandawa. Visit the Shekhavati villages Mandawa, Nawalgarh, Dundlod full of beautiful Havelis. This entire area is like a spectacular gallery. (Full day certified local English Guide).

OVERNIGHT MANDAWA

itinerary.title: MANDAWA – BIKANER-191 KMS 3.5 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description:
After breakfast transfer to Bikaner. Visit Junagarh fort, Karni Mata temple. (Full day certified local English Guide).

OVERNIGHT IN BIKANER

itinerary.title: BIKANER – JAISALMER- 330 KMS 6 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description:
After breakfast drive towards Jaisalmer.

OVERNIGHT AT JAISALMER

itinerary.title: JAISALMER
itinerary.description:
Fort of Jaisalmer, Salim, Patwon Singh ki Haveli, Gandhi Sagar Tank, Sam dunes in evening(Full day certified local English Guide). Sam dunes dinner at camp.

OVERNIGHT JAISALMER

itinerary.title: JAISALMER – JODHPUR- 282 KMS, 5 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description:
After early breakfast, leave for Jodhpur, Check-in at hotel in Jodhpur. Afternoon sightseeing tour of Jodhpur Mehrangarh Fort, Jaswant Thada, Jodhpur tour (Full day certified local English Guide).

OVERNIGHT JODHPUR

itinerary.title: JODHPUR – RAWLA NARLAI- 129 KMS, 2.5 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description:
After breakfast drive towards Rawla Narlai.

OVERNIGHT AT RAWLA NARLAI

itinerary.title: RAWLA NARLAI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast drive to Kumbalgarh fort.

OVERNIGHT RAWLA NARLAI

itinerary.title: RAWLA NALRAI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast opt for the optional activity at Rawla Narlai.

OVERNIGHT RAWLA NARLAI

itinerary.title: RAWLA NARLAI – UDAIPUR-115 KMS, 3 HRS DRIVE
itinerary.description:
Leave after early breakfast for Udaipur.

OVERNIGHT UDAIPUR

itinerary.title: UDAIPUR CITY TOUR
itinerary.description:
This morning after breakfast, proceed to a city tour of Udaipur. The City Palace and Museum is considered as the largest palace complex in Rajasthan. The Pratap Memorial-it has the bronze statue of Maharana Pratap, the legendary warrior king of Udaipur. Jagdish Temple which was built in 1651 AD. Saheliyon Ki Bari is a small ornamental garden. Late afternoon enjoy a two hour boat-ride on Lake Pichola visiting many Ghats (banks) of washing, bathing etc and enjoy excellent views of the Lake Palace and City Palace.

OVERNIGHT IN UDAIPUR

itinerary.title: UDAIPUR- UDAIPUR AIRPORT
itinerary.description:
After an early breakfast will transfer to Udaipur airport to catch your return flight back home or any onward flight to your next destination.

END OF THE TOUR!

accommodations.name: The Muse Sarovar, New Delhi
accommodations.description:
The Muse Sarovar, New Delhi

 The Muse Sarovar Portico is strategically located in South Delhi. The hotel offers a boutique experience to the busy traveller in bustling Nehru Place, renowned as Asia's largest IT market hub. The hotel combines style and elegance with comfort within its contemporary interiors. Conveniences for both the business and leisure traveller have been taken care of in the hotel.The hotel is located approx 12 kms from the Airport and Railway Station and also enjoys close proximity to the commercial and industrial areas like Okhla and Jasola and upmarket shopping centers like Greater Kailash,South Extension and Lajpat Nagar. AIIMS,Escorts and Apollo Hospitals are around 20 min from the hotel,Nehru Place & Kalka JI Metro Station is 1 km from the hotel.

accommodations.name: Hotel Clarks Shiraz, Agra
accommodations.description:
Hotel Clarks Shiraz, Agra

 Clarks Shiraz, Agra, is a five-star luxury hotel which combines business with pleasure, style with substance, form with function. 

Overlooking the Taj and Agra Fort, Hotel Clarks Shiraz has been synonymous with exceptional hospitality for the last five decades. The hotel has the honour of serving a number of distinguished guests. 

This 5-star resort, spread over 8 landscaped acres, has 237 well-appointed and fully renovated rooms, with maximum Taj facing rooms in the city.

Located amidst lush green lawns,outdoor pool with a minimum depth of 3 feet to maximum of 8 feet. There is a baby pool as well adjacent to the main pool. Hotel also offers indoor game like Pool Tables, Table Tennis, Air Hockey, etc.

The hotel offers an array of delectable cuisine, ranging

accommodations.name: Khatu Haveli, Jaipur
accommodations.description:
Khatu Haveli, Jaipur

 Khatu Haveli is located in the heart of the walled Pink City of Jaipur . It is just half a kilometer off the main East-West artery of the Pink City. Train Station is one and a half kilometer away while the Airport is 13 kilometers. The City Palace, Hawa Mahal and Jantar Mantar are two and a half kilometers away. Khatu Haveli was built in mid 19th Century. Since then, six generations of the Khatu family have lived in the magnificent Haveli. Khatu is a small township which is located approximately 80 km west of Jaipur.

accommodations.name: Hotel Radhika Haveli Mandawa
accommodations.description:
Hotel Radhika Haveli Mandawa

 Featuring free WiFi, a restaurant and a barbecue, Hotel Radhika Haveli, Mandawa offers accommodation in Mandawa. The hotel has a children's playground and terrace, and guests can enjoy a meal at the restaurant or a drink at the bar. Free private parking is available on site. A flat-screen TV and a private balcony are featured. Some units include a seating area for your convenience. You will find a coffee machine and a kettle in the room. The rooms include a private bathroom fitted with a shower. For your comfort, you will find slippers and free toiletries. A TV is available.

accommodations.name: Tanisha Heritage Haweli
accommodations.description:
Tanisha Heritage Haweli

 Operating a 24-hour front desk, Tanisha Heritage Haweli is conveniently situated within 1 km from Bikaner Railway Station and Bus Station. Free Wi-Fi access is available in its public areas. The property is less than a 30-minute drive from Bikaner Airport. The Spice Market is located 100 m away, while the famous Junagarh Fort is about 1.5 km from the property.

accommodations.name: Gorbandh Palace, Jaisalmer
accommodations.description:
Gorbandh Palace, Jaisalmer

 It is in this beautiful city of Jaisalmer that the Gorbandh Palace is ideally located. The very name of this Royal Retreat is inspired by the desert and its associated stories.

In this luxurious Royal Retreat you can enjoy all modern-day amenities in an authentic Rajasthani ambience. The retreats lush greenery, fountains, state of the art spa and the blue waters of its swimming pool create a tranquil haven from the heat and dust of the desert city.

With a 24-hour front desk, the property provides services like luggage storage, currency exchange and laundry. A garden is available for taking a relaxing stroll. Guests can pamper themselves at the massage parlour. A tour desk is available for sightseeing.

Rasoda, the in-house restaurant serves a selection of Raj

accommodations.name: The Thikana Heritage, Jodhpur
accommodations.description:
The Thikana Heritage, Jodhpur

 Situated in Jodhpur, 600 m from Mehrangarh Fort and 2 km from Jaswant Thada, The Thikana Heritage features accommodation with free WiFi, air conditioning, a shared lounge and a terrace. The bed and breakfast offers a flat-screen TV and a private bathroom with a hairdryer, free toiletries and bath. Guests wishing to travel light can make use of towels and linens for an additional supplement.

accommodations.name: Rawla Narlai, Jodhpur
accommodations.description:
Rawla Narlai, Jodhpur

 A paradisaical property in the desert countryside, “Rawla Narlai” is one such treasure chest that enhances the luster of the heritage coterie. Tucked away amidst the Aravali’s, among the world’s oldest fold-mountain ranges, this one-time ‘Hunting Manor’ of the Jodhpur Royals now opens its doors to those on a hunt for blissful tranquility. Today, this 17th century ‘Country Manor’, painstakingly restored to its original magnificence, caters to an exclusive clientele in its resurrected avatar of a boutique resort.

accommodations.name: Jaiwana Haveli, Udaipur
accommodations.description:
Jaiwana Haveli, Udaipur

 A 5-minute walk from Udaipur’s famous City Palace and Jagdish Temple, Jaiwana Haveli was once the private residence of the Thakur of Jaiwana. Free WiFi access is available. This heritage property also houses a shopping arcade and a rooftop restaurant. Jaiwana Haveli Udaipur is a 25-minute drive away from Udaipur Airport.

title: Single Traveller Southern India Explorer Trip
description:
If the north of India offers vivid fuchsia and turquoise, its south offers the coolest, most luxuriant greens anywhere. Covering some of the most inspiring and delightful sights of the southern-most states of India, our Southern Explorer tour starts with the atmospheric town of Bangalore.

After a spectacular stop at the fort town of Srirangapatnam, drive to Mysore to see another magnificent site, Mysore Palace. Drive through Bandipur National Park in search of elephants before crossing over to Coonoor on board a UNESCO World Heritage train. Our tour itinerary lets you connect with the Mother Nature by taking you on activities like quest to nature, night expedition, nature walk with naturalist and trek in the nearby hillocks. Moreover, it also gives you an opportunity to participate in cooking demonstration and learn how to cook the local cuisines. Unlock Kerala’s rich history by visiting the impressive architecture of Kochi.

Take a traditional houseboat along the captivating backwaters to final stop Kovalam and spend an evening under a soul-stirring sunset, in a cruise boat, on the picturesque backwaters of Poovar Island.

Two-Night Stay at the Fortune Resort Sullivan Court, Ooty with Half-Board
Learn about the history of tea with tea tasting excursion, Ooty
Visit to Botanical Garden & Lovedale in Ooty

Enroute to Wayanad visit Needle Rock View point in Gudalur, the Wayanad  Wildlife sanctuary, Pookot Lake and Chain Tree
Two-Night Stay at the Wayanad Wild CGH Earth Experience in a Superior Room with Balcony & Half-Board

Enjoy activities such as nature walk focusing on bird watching, trekking and night walk with naturalist at Wayanad Wild resort
Two-Night Stay at the Casino Hotel Kochi with Superior Room & Half-Board
Discover the dances of the Kathakali Show, Kochi

Enroute to Poovar visit the beautiful Hawa Beach

Services of English speaking local guides as per itinerary
