highlights: Simply the best of the tours in India
Stay in some of India's most opulent hotels filled with treasures
Explore the contrasting sights of thriving town, Bangalore
Savour the finest local wines at a charming local vineyard
Experiential resort with rainforest character in Wayanad
Journey through the Bandipur & Mudumalai Wildlife Sanctuary viewing animals on both the side of the road
Drive through scenery lifted straight out of the Lost World
Visit a tea plantation to learn about the history of the “cuppa”
Unlock Kochi’s history by embarking on a sightseeing tour
Grab the secret recipe from Master Chef from Wayanad Wild resort.
Delight in a sumptuous lunch with a local Keralan family
Relish the ambience of the Kathakali & martial arts in Kochi
Set out in Agatha Christie style on a house boat from Alleppey
Thunder across India’s peaks on an UNSECO train to Coonoor
Swing lazily in your hammock in the gentle sea breeze on the beach
Enjoy an Ayurvedic massage or simply lose yourself in a good book

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL BANGALORE
itinerary.description:
Your journey of a lifetime begins in Bangalore. Arrive at the international airport where you’ll be met and transferred to your hotel. There is no better way to start your adventure than with an afternoon guided sightseeing walk around the city.

Once an obscure town, Bangalore is now the hub of India’s booming IT industry and is the nation’s most progressive city. The title “A Tale of Two Cities” is apt as Bangalore bridges together two different worlds. The central district, dating back to the British Raj years, contrasts with the Silicon Valley-esque outskirts of Bangalore. On this journey you will discover a mystifying land sprinkled with the unpredictable gems of India’s legacy.

At 16:00 hrs, visit the Bangalore Palace. It is one of the top attractions in Bangalore known for its elegant splendour and royal charm. You may take a walking tour of the palace and explore the architectural marvel which is a perfect blend of Tudor and Scottish Gothic with wooden interiors. 

Travellers arriving Bangalore by afternoon flight will be taken to Bangalore palace on day 2 morning at 09:45 hrs.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGALORE

itinerary.title: BANGALORE - MYSORE
itinerary.description:
For the ultimate Indian indulgence we have arranged a day of stunning delights with the best of local wines. After breakfast and check-out, your wine tour commences with chauffeured transport in an air-conditioned car to a beautiful wine-producing vineyard situated in Channapatna,a city located 60km south-west of Bangalore. Here you will relish the strong floral and fruity notes of different varieties of wines. Your expert guide will ensure your tour flows effortlessly from start to finish, from pouring the finest wine into your ice-cool glass to surmising the age-old and modern processes of wine and cuisine.

Next, you will drive to the royal city of Mysore. Your stop along the way is equally spectacular with a visit to the fort town of Srirangapatnam. Located on an island straddling the Cauvery River, Srirangapatam is steeped in bloody history. The seat of Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan’s power, this town was the de facto capital of much of southern India during the 18th century. The epic war waged by the British against Tipu Sultan in 1799 ended Srirangapatnam’s glory days. You can still sense the history as you view the massive ramparts, battlements and some of the remaining gates of the fort.

Drive on to Mysore and check-in to your hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN MYSORE

itinerary.title: MYSORE SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, we arrive at Mysore Palace. Discover a royal heirloom in marble as you behold the sight of the sun striking its rays onto the domes that radiate like pink pearls. The palace cannot be left out of Southern India’s treasure trail! The finest example of Indo-Saracenic archaeological brilliance, it is one of India’s grandest royal buildings. Entering the Doll’s Pavilion and Elephant Gate you will be transported back to a lavish way of life, where opulent halls are richly decorated with stained-glass ceilings and a golden elephant howdah that stands like an unused TARDIS to the ancient world of the Maharajas of Mysore. 

Your afternoon exploration of the city of Palaces begins at the temple of Chamundeswari located on top of Chamundi Hills. The temple was named after a deity worshiped for centuries by Mysore Maharajas. Walk up the steps to behold the remarkable tower of the temple and its granite statues including the beautiful 16-foot statue of Nandi, gifted by the Maharaja, Dodda Devaraja Wodeyar in 1659. Also included is a drive to St. Philomena’s Church. Constructed in 1936, it is believed to be one of the oldest churches in India and its architecture was inspired by the Cologne Cathedral in Germany.

Rest of the day is at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN MYSORE

itinerary.title: MYSORE – BANDIPUR -- MUDUMALAI -- OOTY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast from the hotel, check out at 10:00 Hrs and depart for Ranganathittu Bird Sanctuary which is 14 kms from Mysore.

Spanning over 40 acres of land area, Ranganathittu Bird sanctuary lies beautifully on the banks of River Cauvery. The sanctuary houses some of the most exquisite birds and offers a picturesque setting to mesmerise tourists. Here you can spot a wide array of local and migratory birds including shag, white ibis, Darter, Peafowl, Pond Heron, Wild Duck, open-billed stork, painted stork, kingfisher, Cattle Egret, River Tern and many more. Later, visit the Lalith Mahal palace hotel, which is the second largest palace in Mysore. The construction of the palace dates back to 20th century and was build for the exclusive stay of the then Viceroy of India. The elegant palace is painted pure white and boasts of its exquisite Renaissance architecture, sprawling landscaped gardens and historical significance.

Post lunch, depart for Ooty at around 13:00 hours. Enroute to Ooty visit Bandipur Forest by car, you will proceed to Mudumalai national park & wildlife sanctuary which is 12 kms away from Bandipur national park, Covering 880 sq km expanse of wilderness between the Deccan Plateau and Western Ghats, Bandipur National Park was once the Mysore Maharajas’ wildlife reserve. It is now a protected zoo for over 100 species of mammals, including tiger, elephant, leopard and sloth bear. The park also teems with 350 species of bird, so keep your eyes open for delicately hued doves, slender and elegant herons and broad winged eagles.

Enjoy the Jeep Safari at  Mudumalai Wildlife sanctuary from 1500 to 16:00 Hrs.Then you will proceed to Ooty, which is also known as the “the queen of the hill stations” and is one of 65 hill stations set up across India by the British. The town was established in the early 19th century as the summer headquarters of the Madras government and memorably nicked-named “Snooty Ooty”. It lies among sprawling forests, and at 7,00

itinerary.title: OOTY LOVEDALE & COONOOR SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, proceed for a sightseeing tour of Ooty.  “Udhagamandalam is the "Queen of hill stations" and the capital of Nilgiris district. It is one of the most popular tourist resorts in India. Nilgiris which means "Blue Mountains" is a land of picturesque picnic spots and was once a popular summer and weekend getaway for the British during the colonial days. 

Morning visit to Botanical Garden in Ooty, Tea Factory & Lovedale Founded in 1812, the town was used by the British to escape from the heat of the plains below. Like with other hill stations –Ooty, Coonoor,  Wellington – founded by the British, Lovedale still retains its colonial flavor in the form of old-world cottages, bungalows and Gothic Church.

Afternoon proceed to Coonoor, known for the production of Nilgiri tea, Coonoor is one of three misty Nilgiri hill stations. It sits approximately 1850m above sea level and is connected to Ooty and Mettupalayam by the joyful and ingenious Nilgiri Mountain Railway, inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list. The surrounding region is beautifully carpeted in emerald tea plantations that are studded with tall trees and interspersed with waterfalls. Enjoy lunch and visit Sims park and Coonoor Tea plantations

Depart from Coonoor at 1600 hrs and proceed to Ooty at 17:30 hrs. You will be picked up from Ooty Railway station and will be transferred to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN OOTY

itinerary.title: OOTY--VYTHIRI IN WAYANAD (140 KMS)
itinerary.description:
Check out at 10'o clock and proceed to Vythiri in Wayanad. Enroute to Wayanad visit Needle Rock View point in Gudalur and later visit the Wayanad  Wildlife sanctuary. In afternoon visit Pookot Lake and Chain Tree, check in at the Wildlife resort at 15 00 hours.

Wayanad, the green paradise is nestled among the mountains of the Western Ghats, forming the border world of the greener part of Kerala. Clean and pristine, enchanting and hypnotizing, this land is filled with history and culture. Located at a distance of 80 km. from the sea shores of Kozhikode, this verdant hill station is full of tea & coffee plantations, forests and wildlife. 

Regarding geographical location, Wayanad district stands on the southern top of the Deccan plateau and its chief glory is the majestic Western Ghats with lofty ridges and rugged terrain interspersed with dense forest, tangled jungles and deep valleys. 

OVERNIGHT IN WAYANAD

itinerary.title: WAYANAD
itinerary.description:
After enjoying your breakfast, this day will be at leisure. You may relax at the hotel or can choose amongst inhouse resort activities such as nature walk with naturalist, trek to nearby hillocks, etc. In the evening there will be cooking demonstration etc would be an amazing experience for you.

OVERNIGHT IN WAYANAD

itinerary.title: WAYANAD-KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Check out at 09 00 clock and depart to Kochi by car, on the way to Kochi visit Lakkidi view point. Arrive Kochi in the evening and relax at the hotel. Kochi (formerly known as Cochin) is a cosmopolitan city in Kerala. Nowhere else in India could you find such an intriguing mix: giant fishing nets from China, a 400-year-old synagogue, ancient mosques, Portuguese houses and the crumbling remains of the British Raj. It’s a delightful place to spend some time and nap in some of India’s finest homestays and heritage accommodation. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
Unlock Kochi’s history by embarking on a guided tour that will take you into the heart of this magnificent city. Here you will visit the gigantic Chinese fishing nets, “cheenavalas”, dating from 1350 and still in use today.

You will also visit the wonderful St. Francis’ Church, considered to be India’s oldest church, built by Portuguese Franciscan Friars in 1503. Vasco Da Gama was buried here before being taken to Lisbon. Relax in the shade as you drive to the Jewish Synagogue, built over 400 years ago and discover the impressive architecture and beautiful murals of Dutch Palace.

Kochi is also a centre for Keralan arts and one of the best places to see Kathakali.  After 5:00 o'clock in the evening,  you will also experience Kathakali Show.

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI –ALLEPPEY HOUSEBOAT
itinerary.description:
Your journey continues by crossing the captivating backwaters by air conditioned houseboat.  After breakfast, depart for Alleppey. Set out in style on an enchanting one night cruise. While lounging in these floating villas, you will feel like invaders of the backwaters, with the vibrant sights of Kerala skimming by. Look out for the huge Chinese fishing nets, paddy fields, and locals making coir products, rustic buildings, ancient temples and dense coconut trees. Three meals will be served on board.

OVERNIGHT ON THE HOUSEBOAT

itinerary.title: ALLEPPEY-KOVALAM-POOVAR
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the houseboat and drive to Kovalam. Kovalam is often tipped as the ‘paradise’ of South India, with its coconut trees and sweeping beaches, reflecting the meaning of its name –a grove of coconut trees. There is a rocky promontory that has formed a fantastic bay of pristine water perfect for a dip. The beaches are divided by rocks, creating its famous crescent shape.

From Kovalam you will be going to Poovar  that lies very close to Vizhinjam, a natural harbour. The Poovar Island here is a popular tourist destination and enroute to Poovar you will be visiting the Hawa Beach.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: EXPLORE POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Spend a full day at leisure sunbathing on the golden sands of Poovar Beach. The estuary shimmers in spades of wow factor where the river, sea and lake meet the land.

A complimentary hour long boat can be organised at Poovar Lake, where you can find golden sandy beaches meeting the estuaries and mangroves.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Breakfast is at the hotel with the rest of the day at leisure. End your holiday on this wonderful paradise by treating yourself to an Ayurvedic Massage, where the body is revived and the soul replenished. Included is a full body session at the hotel or a nearby spa. Please book your slot with the Ayurveda Center at the resort at the time of check in.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR– TRIVANDRUM AIRPORT – UK
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the hotel. A beautiful souvenir is the perfect way to end such a magical holiday. Take home a silk sari or handmade version, its motif conveying Kerala’s everlasting memories.

END OF TOUR

accommodations.name: Holiday Inn Race course Road Bangalore
accommodations.description:
Holiday Inn Race course Road Bangalore

 Conveniently located along Sheshadri Road across Bangalore turf club, Holiday Inn Bengaluru Racecourse overlooks the Bangalore Golf Club ensuring great views. Located 34 kms from Kempegowda International Airport, the hotel has easy access to Bengaluru City Junction Railway Station, Bus Station, and Kempegowda Metro Station. The 272 well-appointed rooms include 22 suites. All units have attached bathroom with a power shower to recharge you after a long and busy day. Some units include a seating area for your convenience. Enjoy lavish Buffet Breakfast spread at Cafe G, The hotel also features concierge service, fitness centre, and a kid’s corner.

accommodations.name: Southern Star, Mysore
accommodations.description:
Southern Star, Mysore

 Southern Star is within 500 m from the historic Somnathpur Keshava Temple and the railway station. Surrounded by shady palm trees, Southern Star features an outdoor pool, fitness facilities and elegant rooms. Offering spacious layouts, air-conditioned rooms feature wooden flooring and neutral shades. In-room comforts include a tea/coffee maker and private bathroom with a hairdryer. For leisure, guests can relax in the spa bath or have active workouts at the gym. Dining highlights include poolside dining with hearty curries at Charcoals while La Gardenia offers internal favourites. The Pastry Shop and Lobby Bar are ideal for light bites.

accommodations.description:
Fortune Resort Sullivan Court - Member ITC Hotel Group, Ooty

 Set on the gentle slopes of Ooty, amidst lush green lawns and a colorful patterned garden, Fortune Resort Sullivan Court is a modern-day tribute to John Sullivan, who founded the scenic town in 1821. This beautiful family resort in Ooty is a perfect destination to unwind and relax. The hotel is just 1 km from the city centre, 2 km from railway station and 100 km from Coimbatore Airport.

The hotel offers a choice of 67 rooms with most of them providing scenic view of the slopes and is an ideal getaway for a fun-filled vacation with family and friends.

As one of the best hotels in Ooty, Fortune Resort Sullivan Court offers a choice of 67 beautiful rooms, comprising of 51 Standard Rooms, 12 Fortune Club Rooms, and 4 Suites. The

accommodations.name: CGH Wayanad Wild, Vythiri
accommodations.description:
CGH Wayanad Wild, Vythiri

 CGH Wayanad Wild is an experiential nature resort in Lakkidi area of Wayanad North Kerala set amidst the lush green forests in Wayanad, Kerala offering travelers a CGH style rainforest experience. Majestic landscapes, lush greenery, gurgling streams and the occasional wild encounter surrounds the property located in the heart of the woods.

The spacious rooms are well-equipped with modern facilities and amenities, and a jungle peeping in from the window. The outdoor pool offers a stunning view of the forest, while the restaurant dishes out authentic cuisine made from local produce.

Wayanad Wild has been designed to be family-friendly. Activities for children include light treks into the woods, hiking to neighbouring hillocks, zip lining, bamboo rafting in the r

accommodations.name: CGH Earth - Casino Hotel, Kochi
accommodations.description:
CGH Earth - Casino Hotel, Kochi

 The Casino Hotel is not just one of Cochin's landmarks, it's also a fine introduction to the charms of the city, and indeed, of Kerala. Here, you can get an authentic taste of Kerala's colourful life, diverse cultures and eclectic cuisine. The hotel is 6 km from Ernakulam and a 10 minute ferry ride to Fort Cochin and Mattancherry.

The Casino Hotel features regular rooms, spaciously appointed suites and a separate non-smoking wing on the ground floor. Air-conditioned guest rooms are fitted with a personal safe, a minibar and satellite television. Environmentally sensitive practices are the watchword here, as in all the CGH properties.

Many guests love whiling away a relaxed hour or two in the Casino Hotel's sunlit, spacious lounge. This is the heart of th

accommodations.name: Air Conditioned House Boat, Alleppey
accommodations.description:
Air Conditioned House Boat, Alleppey

 Spend your day on the traditional Houseboat also know as Kettuvallam. 

The Kettuvallam or 'boat with knots' is called so because coir ropes tied in knots hold the entire structure of the boat together. During the entire assembling work of the boat, not a single nail is used. Natural and eco-friendly products like bamboo mat, arecanut trees and coir are used to construct the boats.

Kettuvallam comprise of a separate living room with dining table, a bedroom with bathroom and a sit out area at one corner of the boat.

Guests can enjoy a calm evening right in the middle of the backwaters and watch one of the best sunsets that there could be.

accommodations.name: Isola Di Cocco Poovar

Isola Di Cocco Poovar

 The property is a unique heritage resort cosseted by a coconut grove. Located on the banks of the river Poovar, at the juncture where the tranquil river embraces the roaring Arabian sea! The property is tucked away in the southwestern tip of India in the State of Kerala - the internationally renowned tourist destination (projected as one of the 50 best "must see destinations" in the world by the National Geographic Traveller Magazine)

Isola Di Cocco offers rooms, suites and condominiums, spread over 30 acres studded with palms and lush greenery. Privacy abounds at this upscale resort where you'll enjoy romantic seaside dining, lively open-air bar, attentive staff, large fresh water pool, great backwater rides and lots of relaxation. title: South India Heritage Tour With Wildlife Backwaters And Sunny Beaches
description:
This 14-day journey through South India takes you across a rich blend of heritage, culture, wildlife, and beaches. Starting in Chennai and ending in Kochi, the tour covers ancient temple towns, colonial-era cities, spice-scented hills, tranquil backwaters, and golden sandy beaches.

You’ll explore UNESCO World Heritage temples at Mahabalipuram, Thanjavur, and Kumbakonam, walk through the French Quarter of Pondicherry, and witness the spiritual splendour of Madurai’s Meenakshi Temple. In Chettinad, discover palatial mansions and unique cultural traditions, before moving into Kerala to enjoy wildlife safaris in Thekkady, a memorable houseboat cruise in Alleppey, and the serene charm of Marari Beach.

Throughout the journey, you will stay in a curated selection of heritage hotels, boutique resorts, and a traditional houseboat, offering an authentic South Indian experience with modern comfort. From cultural landmarks and sacred temples to lush backwaters and pristine beaches, this tour is designed to showcase the true essence of South India in one unforgettable holiday.
excerpt: This 14-day journey through South India takes you across a rich blend of heritage, culture, wildlife, and beaches. Starting in Chennai and ending in Kochi, the tour covers ancient temple towns, colonial-era cities, spice-scented hills, tranquil backwaters, and golden sandy beaches.

highlights: Mahabalipuram’s UNESCO Monuments – Discover the Shore Temple, Arjuna’s Penance, and cave sanctuaries showcasing Pallava-era artistry.
Temple Treasures of Kumbakonam & Thanjavur – Visit Darasuram’s UNESCO-listed Chola temple, Brihadeeswarar Temple, and other sacred shrines.
Chettinad Heritage & Mansions – Experience opulent Chettinad palaces, traditional architecture, and unique local culture.
Sacred Madurai – Witness the vibrant life of one of India’s oldest cities and attend the evening Aarti at the iconic Meenakshi Temple.
Thekkady’s Nature & Culture – Cruise through Periyar Tiger Reserve, explore spice plantations, and enjoy Kathakali & Kalaripayattu performances.
Backwaters & Beaches of Kerala – Stay on a traditional Alleppey houseboat and unwind at Marari Beach, one of India’s most serene coastal getaways.

itinerary.title: Arrive Chennai, Drive to Mahabalipuram
itinerary.description: On arrival at Chennai International Airport, you will be met and driven to Mahabalipuram, also known as Mamallapuram. This coastal town, famous for its beaches and UNESCO-listed temples, was once an important centre of art and architecture during the Pallava dynasty. After checking into your beach resort, the rest of the day is free to relax and rejuvenate by the sea.

itinerary.title: Mahabalipuram to Pondicherry
itinerary.description: In the morning, explore the marvels of Mahabalipuram including the Shore Temple, Arjuna’s Penance, and its rock-cut cave temples, all dating back to the 7th–8th centuries. Later, continue your journey south to Pondicherry, a former French colony on the Coromandel Coast, known for its unique blend of Tamil and European heritage. Dinner and overnight stay in Pondicherry.

itinerary.title: Pondicherry Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Spend the day exploring Pondicherry’s French Quarter, colonial architecture, and cosmopolitan lifestyle. Highlights include the Sri Aurobindo Ashram, Pondicherry Museum, French War Memorial, Aayi Mandapam, the seaside Promenade, and Pondicherry Beach. The city’s charm lies in its mix of French influence and Tamil culture. Overnight in Pondicherry.

itinerary.title: Pondicherry to Kumbakonam via Chidambaram
itinerary.description: After breakfast, drive towards Kumbakonam with a stop at Pichavaram Mangrove Forest, the world’s second largest of its kind, where a boat ride takes you through enchanting waterways. Later, continue to Kumbakonam, known as the “temple town” and once the capital of the Medieval Cholas. Check into your heritage hotel and relax. Dinner and overnight in Kumbakonam.

itinerary.title: Kumbakonam Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Today you will visit the UNESCO-listed Darasuram Temple, an exquisite Chola-period Shiva shrine renowned for its detailed sculptures. Explore other notable temples including Kumbeswara, Sarangapani, and Ramaswamy, and learn about the town’s cultural importance as both a spiritual and educational hub. Overnight in Kumbakonam.

itinerary.title: Kumbakonam to Thanjavur & Chettinad
itinerary.description: Depart for Thanjavur, the cultural capital of Tamil Nadu. Visit the magnificent Brihadeeswarar Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the Thanjavur Maratha Palace. After sightseeing, drive onward to Chettinad, known for its opulent mansions and distinctive cuisine. Overnight stay at a luxury heritage hotel in Chettinad.

itinerary.title: Explore Chettinad
itinerary.description: Spend the day exploring Chettinad’s rich heritage, beginning with the Chettinad Museum and its unique cultural artifacts. Later, admire the grand Chettinad Palace and stroll through traditional streets showcasing the region’s unique architecture. Overnight in Chettinad.

itinerary.title: Chettinad to Madurai
itinerary.description: Drive to Madurai, one of India’s oldest cities and a major centre of Tamil culture. Visit the iconic Meenakshi Amman Temple, an architectural masterpiece, and in the evening witness the spectacular Aarti ceremony, a spiritual highlight of your journey. Overnight in Madurai.

itinerary.title: Madurai to Thekkady
itinerary.description: After breakfast, drive to Thekkady in Kerala, passing through scenic grape vineyards at Cumbum Valley. On arrival, check into your resort and later enjoy a cultural evening featuring Kerala’s traditional Kathakali dance and Kalaripayattu martial arts. Dinner and overnight in Thekkady.

itinerary.title: Thekkady Wildlife & Spices
itinerary.description: Today is dedicated to nature and wildlife. Enjoy a boat safari on Periyar Lake inside Periyar Tiger Reserve, where you may spot elephants, deer, birds, and other wildlife. Later, take a guided walk through spice plantations to experience Kerala’s aromatic treasures. Overnight in Thekkady.

itinerary.title: Thekkady to Alleppey
itinerary.description: Drive to Alleppey to board your traditional Kerala houseboat for an unforgettable overnight backwater cruise. Sail through tranquil lagoons and canals, enjoy freshly cooked Kerala cuisine, and relax on the deck as the scenery glides past. Overnight on the houseboat.

itinerary.title: Alleppey to Marari Beach
itinerary.description: Disembark from your houseboat and continue to Marari Beach, a serene stretch of golden sand along the Arabian Sea. Known as one of the world’s finest hammock beaches, Marari offers the perfect blend of tranquility and charm. Overnight stay at your beach resort.

itinerary.title: Marari Beach Leisure
itinerary.description: Spend a leisurely day at Marari Beach. You may join a yoga or meditation session, take part in a spice cooking demonstration, or cycle around local villages. The long sandy beach with coconut palms provides the perfect setting for rest and relaxation. Overnight in Marari.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, check out and drive to Kochi. Depending on your flight schedule, enjoy some last-minute shopping or sightseeing before transferring to Kochi International Airport for your onward journey.

Grande Bay Resort and Spa Mamallapuram

 Grande Bay Resort and Spa Mamallapuram is located in Mahabalipuram. It is a 5-minute walk from Mahabalipuram Beach. Popular tourist destinations like Arjunas Penance and Sea Shore Temple are within 2 km radius from the hotel.

Hotel Maison Perumal

 Hotel Maison Perumal by CGH Earth is a boutique hotel located in Pondicherry's Tamil Quarter. The heritage building features a Franco-Tamil design renowned for its excellent use of free spaces, ventilated interiors and arched colonnades, doorways and windows adorned with stained glass. This expertly restored manor house follows traditional Tamil philosophy of minimalistic yet refined décor. Its foliaged courtyards, tapered verandas and cozy rooms inspire a delightful nostalgia in the residents.

Take a step back into the past as you enter the rooms at Hotel Maison Perumal. The red oxide floors, poster beds, French cabinets and fauteuil chairs entice your heart with their antique origins and design. Each room is decorated in a unique flavor reflecting the same Franco-

Mantra Koodam-CGH Earth, Kumbakonam

 This eco-friendly hotel enjoys a scenic location between the Veera Chozha and Cauverey Rivers in Southern India's Tanjore District. The living spaces at Mantra Koodam offer an experience of the rare simplicity of village life alongside the historic opulence of South India’s regal past. Presenting wellness and peace, Mantra Koodam is within 6 km from Uppiliyappan Temple and Kumbakonam Railway Station.

Surrounded by herbal gardens, elegant cottages and villas come with both air conditioning and ceiling fans. 

The crystal-clear waters of the pool at Mantra Koodam offer the perfect way to cool off after a long day of exploring Kumbakonam’s historic wonders.

Anna Prasanna restaurant serves vegetarian and non-vegetarian cuisines prepared in a traditional

accommodations.description:
Visalam – CGH Earth hotel, Chettinad

 Visalam ( A CGH Earth Experience) is where you would want to go to experience a unique Tamil culture. Located in Kanadukathan village in chettinad, Visalam has many a stories to tell of the heritage, culture, food and people of this interesting tamil community, the chettiars. Stay at Visalam for that journey back in time and for that warm tamil hospitality.

Fortune Pandiyan Hotel, Madurai

Fortune Pandiyan features 57 elegantly appointed spacious rooms. All rooms offer a wide range of modern amenities to suit the needs of the discerning traveler. Air-conditioned rooms feature classic wood furnishings and neutral fabrics.

Fortune Pandiyan offers fresh, regional delicacies as well as popular international favorites at its signature restaurants. tart your day with a delicious, healthy breakfast at Orchid. In the evening, soak up the buzzing atmosphere and enjoy refreshing drinks at The Ranch Bar.

The resort has cozy and opulent living units with light wood furnishings and large windows which offer a great view of the green surroundings. All the rooms are equipped with contemporary amenities for a comfortable stay. 

Greenwood resort also offers a variety of recreational activities with facilities such as game room,

Flamingo Boutique Villa

The premises is enabled 24 / 7 with high speed, unlimited Wifi connectivity, so that you can stay connected and never miss out on your daily updates.

title: Southern India Explorer Tour with Private Chauffeur
description:
If the north of India offers vivid fuchsia and turquoise, its south offers the coolest, most luxuriant greens anywhere. Covering some of the most inspiring and delightful sights of the southern-most states of India, our Southern Explorer tour starts with the atmospheric town of Bangalore.

After a spectacular stop at the fort town of Srirangapatnam, drive to Mysore to see another magnificent site, Mysore Palace. Drive through Bandipur National Park in search of elephants before crossing over to Coonoor on board a UNESCO World Heritage train. Our tour itinerary lets you connect with the Mother Nature by taking you on activities like quest to nature, night expedition, nature walk with naturalist and trek in the nearby hillocks. Moreover, it also gives you an opportunity to participate in cooking demonstration and learn how to cook the local cuisines. Unlock Kerala’s rich history by visiting the impressive architecture of Kochi.

Take a traditional houseboat along the captivating backwaters to final stop Kovalam and spend an evening under a soul-stirring sunset, in a cruise boat, on the picturesque backwaters of Poovar Island.

This package is also available for single travelers starting from an additional £499 per person!

highlights: Stay in some of India's most opulent hotels filled with treasures
Explore the contrasting sights of thriving town, Bangalore

Experiential resort with rainforest character in Wayanad

Enjoy an Ayurvedic massage or simply lose yourself in a good book

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL BANGALORE
itinerary.description:
Your journey of a lifetime begins in Bangalore. Arrive at the international airport where you’ll be met and transferred to your hotel. There is no better way to start your adventure than with an afternoon guided sightseeing walk around the city.

Once an obscure town, Bangalore is now the hub of India’s booming IT industry and is the nation’s most progressive city. The title “A Tale of Two Cities” is apt as Bangalore bridges together two different worlds. The central district, dating back to the British Raj years, contrasts with the Silicon Valley-esque outskirts of Bangalore. On this journey you will discover a mystifying land sprinkled with the unpredictable gems of India’s legacy.

At 16:00 hrs, visit the Bangalore Palace. It is one of the top attractions in Bangalore known for its elegant splendour and royal charm. You may take a walking tour of the palace and explore the architectural marvel which is a perfect blend of Tudor and Scottish Gothic with wooden interiors. 

Travellers arriving Bangalore by afternoon flight will be taken to Bangalore palace on day 2 morning at 09:45 hrs.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGALORE

itinerary.title: BANGALORE - MYSORE
itinerary.description:
For the ultimate Indian indulgence we have arranged a day of stunning delights with the best of local wines. After breakfast and check-out, your wine tour commences with chauffeured transport in an air-conditioned car to a beautiful wine-producing vineyard situated in Channapatna,a city located 60km south-west of Bangalore. Here you will relish the strong floral and fruity notes of different varieties of wines. Your expert guide will ensure your tour flows effortlessly from start to finish, from pouring the finest wine into your ice-cool glass to surmising the age-old and modern processes of wine and cuisine. 

Next, you will drive to the royal city of Mysore. Your stop along the way is equally spectacular with a visit to the fort town of Srirangapatnam. Located on an island straddling the Cauvery River, Srirangapatam is steeped in bloody history. The seat of Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan’s power, this town was the de facto capital of much of southern India during the 18th century. The epic war waged by the British against Tipu Sultan in 1799 ended Srirangapatnam’s glory days. You can still sense the history as you view the massive ramparts, battlements and some of the remaining gates of the fort.

Drive on to Mysore and check-in to your hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN MYSORE

itinerary.title: MYSORE SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, we arrive at Mysore Palace. Discover a royal heirloom in marble as you behold the sight of the sun striking its rays onto the domes that radiate like pink pearls. The palace cannot be left out of Southern India’s treasure trail! The finest example of Indo-Saracenic archaeological brilliance, it is one of India’s grandest royal buildings. Entering the Doll’s Pavilion and Elephant Gate you will be transported back to a lavish way of life, where opulent halls are richly decorated with stained-glass ceilings and a golden elephant howdah that stands like an unused TARDIS to the ancient world of the Maharajas of Mysore. 

Your afternoon exploration of the city of Palaces begins at the temple of Chamundeswari located on top of Chamundi Hills. The temple was named after a deity worshiped for centuries by Mysore Maharajas. Walk up the steps to behold the remarkable tower of the temple and its granite statues including the beautiful 16-foot statue of Nandi, gifted by the Maharaja, Dodda Devaraja Wodeyar in 1659. Also included is a drive to St. Philomena’s Church. Constructed in 1936, it is believed to be one of the oldest churches in India and its architecture was inspired by the Cologne Cathedral in Germany.

Rest of the day is at leisure.

OVERNIGHT IN MYSORE

itinerary.title: MYSORE – BANDIPUR -- MUDUMALAI -- OOTY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast from the hotel, check out at 10:00 Hrs and depart for Ranganathittu Bird Sanctuary which is 14 kms from Mysore.

Spanning over 40 acres of land area, Ranganathittu Bird sanctuary lies beautifully on the banks of River Cauvery. The sanctuary houses some of the most exquisite birds and offers a picturesque setting to mesmerise tourists. Here you can spot a wide array of local and migratory birds including shag, white ibis, Darter, Peafowl, Pond Heron, Wild Duck, open-billed stork, painted stork, kingfisher, Cattle Egret, River Tern and many more. Later, visit the Lalith Mahal palace hotel, which is the second largest palace in Mysore. The construction of the palace dates back to 20th century and was build for the exclusive stay of the then Viceroy of India. The elegant palace is painted pure white and boasts of its exquisite Renaissance architecture, sprawling landscaped gardens and historical significance.

Post lunch, depart for Ooty at around 13:00 hours. Enroute to Ooty visit Bandipur Forest by car, you will proceed to Mudumalai national park & wildlife sanctuary which is 12 kms away from Bandipur national park, Covering 880 sq km expanse of wilderness between the Deccan Plateau and Western Ghats, Bandipur National Park was once the Mysore Maharajas’ private wildlife reserve. It is now a protected zoo for over 100 species of mammals, including tiger, elephant, leopard and sloth bear. The park also teems with 350 species of bird, so keep your eyes open for delicately hued doves, slender and elegant herons and broad winged eagles.

Enjoy the Jeep Safari at  Mudumalai Wildlife sanctuary from 1500 to 16:00 Hrs.

Then you will proceed to Ooty, which is also known as the “the queen of the hill stations” and is one of 65 hill stations set up across India by the British. The town was established in the early 19th century as the summer headquarters of the Madras government and memorably nicked-named “Snooty Ooty”. It lies among sprawling fores

itinerary.title: OOTY LOVEDALE & COONOOR SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, proceed for a sightseeing tour of Ooty.  “Udhagamandalam is the "Queen of hill stations" and the capital of Nilgiris district. It is one of the most popular tourist resorts in India. Nilgiris which means "Blue Mountains" is a land of picturesque picnic spots and was once a popular summer and weekend getaway for the British during the colonial days. 

Morning visit to Botanical Garden in Ooty, Tea Factory & Lovedale Founded in 1812, the town was used by the British to escape from the heat of the plains below. Like with other hill stations –Ooty, Coonoor,  Wellington – founded by the British, Lovedale still retains its colonial flavor in the form of old-world cottages, bungalows and Gothic Church.

Afternoon proceed to Coonoor, known for the production of Nilgiri tea, Coonoor is one of three misty Nilgiri hill stations. It sits approximately 1850m above sea level and is connected to Ooty and Mettupalayam by the joyful and ingenious Nilgiri Mountain Railway, inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list. The surrounding region is beautifully carpeted in emerald tea plantations that are studded with tall trees and interspersed with waterfalls. Enjoy lunch and visit Sims park and Coonoor Tea plantations

Depart from Coonoor at 1600 hrs and proceed to Ooty at 17:30 hrs. You will be picked up from Ooty Railway station and will be transferred to the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN OOTY

itinerary.title: OOTY--VYTHIRI IN WAYANAD (140 KMS)
itinerary.description:
Check out at 10'o clock and proceed to Vythiri in Wayanad. Enroute to Wayanad visit Needle Rock View point in Gudalur and later visit the Wayanad  Wildlife sanctuary. In afternoon visit Pookot Lake and Chain Tree, check in at the Wildlife resort at 15 00 hours.

Wayanad, the green paradise is nestled among the mountains of the Western Ghats, forming the border world of the greener part of Kerala. Clean and pristine, enchanting and hypnotizing, this land is filled with history and culture. Located at a distance of 80 km. from the sea shores of Kozhikode, this verdant hill station is full of tea & coffee plantations, forests and wildlife. 

Regarding geographical location, Wayanad district stands on the southern top of the Deccan plateau and its chief glory is the majestic Western Ghats with lofty ridges and rugged terrain interspersed with dense forest, tangled jungles and deep valleys. 

OVERNIGHT IN WAYANAD

itinerary.title: WAYANAD
itinerary.description:
After enjoying your breakfast, this day will be at leisure. You may relax at the hotel or can choose amongst inhouse resort activities such as nature walk with naturalist, trek to nearby hillocks, etc. In the evening there will be cooking demonstration etc would be an amazing experience for you.

OVERNIGHT IN WAYANAD

itinerary.title: WAYANAD-KOCHI
itinerary.description:
Check out at 09 00 clock and depart to Kochi by car, on the way to Kochi visit Lakkidi view point. Arrive Kochi in the evening and relax at the hotel. Kochi (formerly known as Cochin) is a cosmopolitan city in Kerala. Nowhere else in India could you find such an intriguing mix: giant fishing nets from China, a 400-year-old synagogue, ancient mosques, Portuguese houses and the crumbling remains of the British Raj. It’s a delightful place to spend some time and nap in some of India’s finest homestays and heritage accommodation. 

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI SIGHTSEEING
itinerary.description:
Unlock Kochi’s history by embarking on a guided tour that will take you into the heart of this magnificent city. Here you will visit the gigantic Chinese fishing nets, “cheenavalas”, dating from 1350 and still in use today. 

You will also visit the wonderful St. Francis’ Church, considered to be India’s oldest church, built by Portuguese Franciscan Friars in 1503. Vasco Da Gama was buried here before being taken to Lisbon. Relax in the shade as you drive to the Jewish Synagogue, built over 400 years ago and discover the impressive architecture and beautiful murals of Dutch Palace.

OVERNIGHT IN KOCHI

itinerary.title: KOCHI –ALLEPPEY HOUSEBOAT
itinerary.description:
Your journey continues by crossing the captivating backwaters by air conditioned houseboat.  After breakfast, depart for Alleppey. Set out in style on an enchanting one night cruise. While lounging in these floating villas, you will feel like invaders of the backwaters, with the vibrant sights of Kerala skimming by. Look out for the huge Chinese fishing nets, paddy fields, and locals making coir products, rustic buildings, ancient temples and dense coconut trees. Three meals will be served on board.

OVERNIGHT ON THE HOUSEBOAT

itinerary.title: ALLEPPEY-KOVALAM-POOVAR
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the houseboat and drive to Kovalam. Kovalam is often tipped as the ‘paradise’ of South India, with its coconut trees and sweeping beaches, reflecting the meaning of its name –a grove of coconut trees. There is a rocky promontory that has formed a fantastic bay of pristine water perfect for a dip. The beaches are divided by rocks, creating its famous crescent shape. 

From Kovalam you will be going to Poovar  that lies very close to Vizhinjam, a natural harbour. The Poovar Island here is a popular tourist destination and enroute to Poovar you will be visiting the Hawa Beach.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: EXPLORE POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Spend a full day at leisure sunbathing on the golden sands of Poovar Beach. The estuary shimmers in spades of wow factor where the river, sea and lake meet the land.

A complimentary hour long boat can be organised at Poovar Lake, where you can find golden sandy beaches meeting the estuaries and mangroves.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR
itinerary.description:
Breakfast is at the hotel with the rest of the day at leisure. End your holiday on this wonderful paradise by treating yourself to an Ayurvedic Massage, where the body is revived and the soul replenished. Included is a full body session at the hotel or a nearby spa. Please book your slot with the Ayurveda Center at the resort at the time of check in.

OVERNIGHT IN POOVAR

itinerary.title: POOVAR– TRIVANDRUM AIRPORT – UK
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the hotel. A beautiful souvenir is the perfect way to end such a magical holiday. Take home a silk sari or handmade version, its motif conveying Kerala’s everlasting memories.

END OF TOUR

The hotel has two restaurants and a bar. Enjoy

Fortune Resort Sullivan Court - Member ITC Hotel Group, Ooty

 Set on the gentle slopes of Ooty, amidst lush green lawns and a colorful patterned garden, Fortune Resort Sullivan Court is a modern-day tribute to John Sullivan, who founded the scenic town in 1821. This beautiful family resort in Ooty is a perfect destination to unwind and relax. The hotel is just 1 km from the city centre, 2 km from railway station and 100 km from Coimbatore Airport.

The hotel offers a choice of 67 rooms with most of them providing scenic view of the slopes and is an ideal getaway for a fun-filled vacation with family and friends.

As one of the best hotels in Ooty, Fortune Resort Sullivan Court offers a choice of 67 beautiful rooms, comprising of 51 Standard Rooms, 12 Fortune Club Rooms, and 4 Suites. The

accommodations.description:
CGH Wayanad Wild, Vythiri

 CGH Wayanad Wild is an experiential nature resort in Lakkidi area of Wayanad North Kerala set amidst the lush green forests in Wayanad, Kerala offering travelers a CGH style rainforest experience. Majestic landscapes, lush greenery, gurgling streams and the occasional wild encounter surrounds the property located in the heart of the woods.

The spacious rooms are well-equipped with modern facilities and amenities, and a jungle peeping in from the window. The outdoor pool offers a stunning view of the forest, while the restaurant dishes out authentic cuisine made from local produce.

Wayanad Wild has been designed to be family-friendly. Activities for children include light treks into the woods, hiking to neighbouring hillocks, zip lining, bamboo rafting in the r

accommodations.name: CGH Earth - Casino Hotel, Kochi
accommodations.description:
CGH Earth - Casino Hotel, Kochi

 The Casino Hotel is not just one of Cochin's landmarks, it's also a fine introduction to the charms of the city, and indeed, of Kerala. Here, you can get an authentic taste of Kerala's colourful life, diverse cultures and eclectic cuisine. The hotel is 6 km from Ernakulam and a 10 minute ferry ride to Fort Cochin and Mattancherry.

The Casino Hotel features regular rooms, spaciously appointed suites and a separate non-smoking wing on the ground floor. Air-conditioned guest rooms are fitted with a personal safe, a minibar and satellite television. Environmentally sensitive practices are the watchword here, as in all the CGH properties.

Many guests love whiling away a relaxed hour or two in the Casino Hotel's sunlit, spacious lounge. This is the heart of th

accommodations.name: Air Conditioned House Boat, Alleppey
accommodations.description:
Air Conditioned House Boat, Alleppey

 Spend your day on the traditional Houseboat also know as Kettuvallam. 

The Kettuvallam or 'boat with knots' is called so because coir ropes tied in knots hold the entire structure of the boat together. During the entire assembling work of the boat, not a single nail is used. Natural and eco-friendly products like bamboo mat, arecanut trees and coir are used to construct the boats.

Kettuvallam comprise of a separate living room with dining table, a bedroom with bathroom and a sit out area at one corner of the boat.

Guests can enjoy a calm evening right in the middle of the backwaters and watch one of the best sunsets that there could be.

accommodations.name: Isola Di Cocco Poovar
accommodations.description:
Isola Di Cocco Poovar

 The property is a unique heritage resort cosseted by a coconut grove. Located on the banks of the river Poovar, at the juncture where the tranquil river embraces the roaring Arabian sea! The property is tucked away in the southwestern tip of India in the State of Kerala - the internationally renowned tourist destination (projected as one of the 50 best "must see destinations" in the world by the National Geographic Traveller Magazine)

Isola Di Cocco offers rooms, suites and condominiums, spread over 30 acres studded with palms and lush greenery. Privacy abounds at this upscale resort where you'll enjoy romantic seaside dining, lively open-air bar, attentive staff, large fresh water pool, great backwater rides and lots of relaxation. title: 9 Nights In Kuala Lumpur Bali and Singapore
description:
Save £400 per person!

Embark on an enchanting journey, delving into Southeast Asia's vibrant cultures, stunning landscapes, and thrilling urban experiences with our specially curated holiday package.

 Your adventure begins with two nights in the mesmerizing city of Kuala Lumpur, where you'll enjoy the comfort and luxury of the 4-star Verdant Hill Hotel Kuala Lumpur. Just a stone's throw away from the city centre, the hotel allows you to immerse yourself in the energetic city life and iconic attractions of Kuala Lumpur. Explore the architectural marvel of the Petronas Twin Towers, relish the kaleidoscope of street food at Jalan Alor Night Market, and discover the spiritual tranquillity at the Batu Caves, all while knowing a peaceful retreat awaits you.

 From the urban excitement of Kuala Lumpur, you'll transition to the serene beauty of Bali for a five-night stay at Villa Coco, it is a boutique villa resort located in Seminyak, one of Bali’s most vibrant areas. It offers a mix of traditional Balinese-style bungalows and luxury private pool villas—perfect for couples, families, and groups. The property is set in lush tropical gardens, providing a peaceful retreat just steps from Seminyak's bustling streets. 

Your holiday culminates with two nights in Singapore, staying at the stylish and contemporary Village Hotel Katong. Strategically located in the city's heart, it offers easy access to its top attractions. Delight in the futuristic wonders of Gardens by the Bay, savour the panoramic views from Marina Bay Sands Skypark and dive into the rich cultural tapestry of Little India and the historic Lau Pa Sat food centre.

 Our holiday package offers unique experiences, interweaving cosmopolitan luxury, cultural immersion, and idyllic relaxation. Uncover the soul of Southeast Asia and create unforgettable memories with us on this carefully planned and exceptionally executed holiday journey.
excerpt: Your adventure begins with two nights in the mesmerizing city of Kuala Lumpur, where you'll enjoy the comfort and luxury of the 4-star Verdant Hill Hotel Kuala Lumpur. Just a stone's throw away from the city centre, the hotel allows you to immerse yourself in the energetic city life and iconic attractions of Kuala Lumpur. Explore the architectural marvel of the Petronas Twin Towers, relish the kaleidoscope of street food at Jalan Alor Night Market, and discover the spiritual tranquillity at the Batu Caves, all while knowing a peaceful retreat awaits you.

Discover the Royal King Palace, the official residence of the Supreme King of Malaysia.
See Jamek Mosque, the oldest Islamic place of worship in Kuala Lumpur
Marvel at the National Monument, a war memorial in Lake Gardens
Wander Independence Square, also known as Dataran Merdeka
Visit a Kuala Lumpur chocolate store
Flight from Kuala Lumpur to Bali
Five nights spent at the 4* Villa Coco in Bali
Flight from Bali to Singapore
Two nights’ accommodation at the 4* Village Hotel Katong  in Singapore (Travellers' Choice)
Enjoy Singapore City Tour
Hassle-free return airport transfers throughout
Enjoy delicious breakfasts throughout your trip
highlights: Fall in love with the sights of Kuala Lumpur during a spellbinding city tour, where you’ll get up close to the city’s must-sees such as Petronas Twin Towers, the Jamek Mosque and National Monument.
Visit a Kuala Lumpur chocolate store to gorge on chocolatey delights and then mingle with celebrity wax figures at Madame Tussauds
Explore beautiful Bali during a 5 night stay at a 4* resort – mingle with monkeys, frolic about in frothy ocean ripples or head off to explore the island's iconic rice paddies.

accommodations.description:
Village Hotel Katong, Singapore

 Village Hotel Katong is situated in a residential area of Singapore, 2.4 km from Geylang Serai. Renovated in 2011, the comfortable hotel has welcomed guests since 1983. An ideal setting offers an easy access to restaurants, golf courses and a stadium as well as boasts proximity to gardens and parks. Guests will find shopping centers and a market within minutes of the hotel.

For a perfect stay at Village Hotel Katong guests can make use of high-speed internet, cable TV, a storage space, a laptop safe and a wardrobe featured in contemporary rooms.A The rooms look on to the city. The rooms come with en suite bathrooms with a shower, a hairdryer and toiletries along with an electric kettle, a dishwasher and a cooktop.

There is a lounge for guests to enjoy th

Border taxes

title: Bali Dream Escape Private Villas, Poolside Bliss
description:
Bali Dream Escape - Save £600 per person!

Bali Dream Escape: Two Worlds, One Perfect Journey

Where Ancient Rainforests Meet Golden Shores

Escape to the enchanting island of Bali for an unforgettable 7-night luxury retreat that captures the soul of this mystical island through its most captivating contrasts. This isn't just a holiday – it's a journey between two distinct worlds, each offering its own magic.

Ubud: Heart of the Sacred Rainforest (4 Nights)

Begin your odyssey in Ubud, where emerald rice terraces cascade down volcanic slopes and ancient Hindu temples emerge from mist-shrouded jungle. Your sanctuary awaits at the serene Seres Hotel & Resort, where your One Bedroom Pool Villa becomes your private retreat amidst nature's cathedral.

Here, in Bali's spiritual heartland, mornings begin with the distant chanting from village temples and the gentle rustle of bamboo in tropical breezes. This is where Balinese culture thrives in its purest form – where master artisans still carve intricate sculptures, where traditional markets burst with exotic spices and tropical fruits, and where the ancient practice of wellness and healing has been perfected over millennia.

Your Ubud Experience:

 Wake to views of terraced rice paddies that have fed Balinese families for over 1,000 years

Discover hidden waterfalls cascading through pristine jungle

Explore the Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary, where ancient temples lie hidden among towering trees

Indulge in world-class spa treatments using traditional Balinese healing techniques

Savor farm-to-table cuisine featuring ingredients grown in the fertile volcanic soil


Jimbaran: Where Ocean Dreams Come Alive (3 Nights)

Your journey then sweeps you south to Jimbaran's sun-kissed shores, where the Indian Ocean stretches endlessly toward the horizon. At the sophisticated Cross Bali Breakers, your Deluxe One Bedroom Pool Villa becomes your oceanside sanctuary – an oasis of contemporary luxury where modern design meets traditional Balinese elegance.

Jimbaran Bay, with its crescent of golden sand and calm turquoise waters, has been the island's premier fishing village for generations. As evening falls, witness one of Bali's most magical moments: traditional jukung fishing boats silhouetted against spectacular sunsets that paint the sky in brilliant oranges and purples.

Your Jimbaran Experience:

Dine on the beach with fresh-caught seafood grilled over coconut husks 

Explore the famous Jimbaran Fish Market at dawn, alive with the day's catch

Discover nearby Uluwatu Temple, perched dramatically on clifftops 70 meters above crashing waves

Watch traditional Kecak fire dances performed against stunning ocean backdrops

Unwind with beachside massages as gentle waves provide nature's soundtrack

 Seamless Luxury Throughout

With private air-conditioned transfers whisking you effortlessly between destinations, every moment is designed for pure indulgence. Daily breakfasts showcase both international favorites and authentic Balinese specialties, while your luxurious accommodations provide the perfect base for exploration – or simply for savoring those precious moments of
poolside tranquility.

This is Bali as it was meant to be experienced: intimately, luxuriously, and authentically. Whether you're drawn to ancient temples hidden in jungle mists, the healing powers of traditional spa rituals, the thrill of discovering hidden waterfalls, or simply the bliss of watching perfect sunsets from your private villa – this journey captures the very essence of the Island
of the Gods.

Two sides of paradise. One unforgettable escape.

Solo Traveler's can travel From £1899

whats_included: Return flights from the UK
Four nights’ accommodation at the 5* Seres Hotel & Resort ( Villa with Private Pool ), Ubud (Travellers' Choice)
Ubud Hotel to Jimbaran Hotel Private Transfer
Three nights spent at the 5* Cross Bali Breakers ( Villa with Private Pool ), Jimbaran (Travellers' Choice)
60 minutes massage at Cross Bali Breakers
Hassle-free private return airport transfers throughout
Enjoy delicious breakfasts throughout your trip

Enjoy coastal tranquility at the stylish Cross Bali Breakers in Jimbaran
Discover the cultural and spiritual charm of Ubud
Relax on Jimbaran’s golden beaches and soak in the coastal vibes
Infinity pools, tropical greenery, sunset coastlines – picture-perfect at every turn
Comfortable, air-conditioned transfers throughout the trip – no stress, just relaxation

accommodations.name: Seres Hotel & Resort Ubud
accommodations.description:
Seres Hotel & Resort Ubud

title: Bali and Lombok Holidays with Excursions
description:
Experience the perfect blend of culture, nature, beaches, and relaxation with this beautifully curated 9-day tour across Bali and Lombok. Begin your journey in the vibrant coastal towns of Seminyak or Kuta, then venture into the cultural heart of Bali—Ubud—to explore waterfalls, temples, rice terraces, and sacred springs. Unwind along the southern coast’s stunning beaches before witnessing Bali’s legendary Kecak fire dance at Uluwatu Temple.

Then, journey across the sea to Lombok, a tranquil island paradise. Relax in Senggigi, snorkel in the crystal-clear waters of the Gili Islands, visit iconic underwater statues, and enjoy unforgettable sunsets and seafood dinners by the beach. With guided tours, scenic landscapes, and a perfect mix of activity and leisure, this tour offers a complete Indonesian island experience.

Solo traveler can book this holiday at £1899 !
excerpt: Experience the perfect blend of culture, nature, beaches, and relaxation with this beautifully curated 9-day tour across Bali and Lombok. Begin your journey in the vibrant coastal towns of Seminyak or Kuta, then venture into the cultural heart of Bali—Ubud—to explore waterfalls, temples, rice terraces, and sacred springs. Unwind along the southern coast’s stunning beaches before witnessing Bali’s legendary Kecak fire dance at Uluwatu Temple.

itinerary.title: ARRIVE BALI
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Ngurah Rai International Airport in Denpasar, Bali, you will be welcomed by an English-speaking guide and transferred to your hotel in Seminyak or Kuta. After check-in, the rest of the day is yours to relax and recover from your journey or start exploring the local surroundings at your own pace. You’ll spend the night at your hotel in Seminyak/Kuta.

itinerary.title: Seminyak/Kuta – Ubud and surrounding with waterfall – Seminyak/Kuta
itinerary.description: After breakfast, embark on a full-day tour to explore Ubud and its lush surroundings. Your first stop is the serene Sumampan Waterfall, accessible via a scenic trail and nearly a hundred steps down through tropical greenery. At the base, admire unique stone carvings created by a local artist, blending nature and culture beautifully. Next, visit Goa Gajah, or Elephant Cave, a 9th-century meditation sanctuary featuring a dramatic stone entrance. Continue north to the iconic Tegalalang Rice Terraces, where you can stroll through the emerald landscape and optionally try the famous jungle swing (at an additional cost). Lunch is served at a scenic coffee plantation overlooking a peaceful valley, where you’ll enjoy Balinese crispy duck. In the afternoon, visit Tirta Empul, a sacred water temple where locals have come to purify themselves for centuries. After a day immersed in nature and culture, return to your hotel in Seminyak/Kuta for the night.

itinerary.title: Seminyak/Kuta – beaches hopping – Uluwatu Kecak Dance – Seminyak/Kuta
itinerary.description: Enjoy breakfast at the hotel before setting off for a day of beach hopping along Bali’s stunning southern coast. Relax at your choice of three beautiful beaches—Thomas Beach, Balangan Beach, and Padang Padang—each offering its own charm, from secluded sands to cliffside views and surfing vibes. You may spend as much time as you like at each, or skip one if preferred. In the evening, make your way to the cliffs of Uluwatu Temple to watch the dramatic Kecak fire dance performance against the backdrop of a fiery sunset over the Indian Ocean. Return to your hotel in Seminyak/Kuta for an overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Seminyak/Kuta – Free at leisure
itinerary.description: Today is free for you to enjoy as you please. You can relax at your hotel, explore Seminyak’s boutiques and cafes, or choose from two optional full-day tours. The first option is a trip to Nusa Penida, including speedboat transfers, snorkeling at pristine spots known for manta rays and vibrant reefs, a visit to iconic Kelingking Beach, Broken Beach, and Angel’s Billabong, with lunch included at a beach club. The second option is a customized private tour with a guide, tailored to your interests—whether that’s temples, rice terraces, beaches, or cultural landmarks. You’ll spend the night again in Seminyak/Kuta.

itinerary.title: Seminyak – Speed boat to Lombok - Senggigi
itinerary.description: After breakfast, you’ll be privately transferred from your hotel to Padang Bai Harbor on Bali’s east coast. From there, take a speedboat across the Lombok Strait to reach Lombok Island. As Bali fades into the distance, enjoy views of the ocean and small surrounding islands. Upon arrival at Senggigi Harbor, meet your local team and transfer to your hotel in Senggigi. The rest of the day is at your leisure to relax and settle in. Overnight in Senggigi.

itinerary.title: Senggigi – Day at leisure
itinerary.description: Spend the day at your own pace, soaking up the serene beauty of Lombok. After a leisurely breakfast, you may choose to swim in the crystal-clear waters, snorkel along the nearby reefs, or simply lounge by the beach or pool. This is a perfect opportunity to recharge and enjoy the peaceful surroundings. Overnight in Senggigi.

itinerary.title: Senggigi – Three Island with snorkeling - Senggigi
itinerary.description: After breakfast, embark on an exciting island-hopping adventure to the famous Gili Islands—Trawangan, Meno, and Air. Depart from Teluk Nara by speedboat to Gili Trawangan, where you’ll board a glass-bottom boat for your first snorkeling stop at a stunning blue coral site. Continue to Gili Meno to snorkel around the enchanting underwater statues and possibly spot sea turtles. The final island, Gili Air, offers another beautiful snorkeling location, followed by a delicious lunch served on the island. You’ll have free time to relax, walk, or bike around before returning by boat to Lombok. On the way back, stop at Malimbu Cliff and Nipah Beach to enjoy a scenic sunset and a grilled seafood dinner. Overnight in Senggigi.

itinerary.title: Senggigi – Day at leisure
itinerary.description: This is another free day to enjoy the laid-back charm of Senggigi. Whether you want to pamper yourself with a spa treatment, explore local markets, or simply relax on the beach, the day is yours. Overnight in Senggigi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, enjoy some final moments of leisure before your transfer to Lombok International Airport for your flight back home. Hotel check-out time is at 12:00 PM.

Meanwhile, our verdant beautiful garden with lush vegetation generates natural backdrop and inspiring holiday living. E

 Selamat datang di Holiday Resort Lombok! Sebagai "rumah" Anda di kawasan Senggigi, hotel dengan fasilitas yang akan menyempurnakan masa menginap Anda. Terletak di salah satu pulau paling indah di Indonesia, hotel ini menawarkan kemewahan, kenyamanan, dan pemandangan spektakuler. Dengan beragam kegiatan seperti snorkeling, menyelam, atau hanya bersantai di tepi kolam renang, Hotel Holiday Resort Lombok adalah tempat ideal untuk melarikan diri dari kesibukan dan menikmati keindahan alam yang menakjubkan.

title: From Neon Nights To Tropical Dreams Hong Kong Kl Bali In 13 Days
description:
This 13-day multi-destination adventure takes you through three of Asia’s most exciting and contrasting destinations – the dazzling cityscapes of Hong Kong, the cultural and spiritual heart of Kuala Lumpur, and the serene tropical beauty of Bali.

Your journey begins in Hong Kong, a city where East meets West in a spectacular skyline of soaring skyscrapers and bustling harbor views. A hop-on hop-off bus tour gives you the freedom to discover Hong Kong’s iconic landmarks, charming neighborhoods, and vibrant markets at your own pace.

From there, travel to Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia’s lively capital, where tradition blends with modernity. The highlight is an unforgettable excursion to the Batu Caves, one of the most revered Hindu shrines in the world, where the striking golden statue of Lord Murugan stands guard over ancient limestone caves. You’ll also enjoy enriching cultural stops, from pewter craftsmanship at Royal Selangor to colorful batik artistry and sweet treats at Beryl’s Chocolate Gallery.

Finally, immerse yourself in the tranquility of Bali, one of the world’s most sought-after island getaways. With six full days at leisure, you can choose between ultimate relaxation and active exploration. Whether it’s sunbathing on golden beaches, indulging in a Balinese spa retreat, venturing into Ubud’s lush rice terraces, witnessing sunset at sea temples, or taking an island-hopping trip to Nusa Penida – the choice is yours.

This well-balanced itinerary seamlessly blends city excitement, cultural discovery, and island bliss, making it perfect for travelers who want to experience the best of Asia in a single, unforgettable holiday.
excerpt: This 13-day multi-destination adventure takes you through three of Asia’s most exciting and contrasting destinations – the dazzling cityscapes of Hong Kong, the cultural and spiritual heart of Kuala Lumpur, and the serene tropical beauty of Bali.

Enjoy delicious breakfasts throughout your trip
highlights: Big Bus Tour in Hong Kong – Explore Hong Kong’s legendary landmarks at your own pace with hop-on hop-off access.
Batu Caves Excursion (Kuala Lumpur) – Witness the towering 140-ft golden statue of Lord Murugan, climb 272 colorful steps, and explore sacred Hindu cave temples.
Cultural Stops in Kuala Lumpur – Visit the Royal Selangor Pewter Factory, learn about traditional batik painting, and indulge at Beryl’s Chocolate Gallery.
Bali Paradise Stay – Relax on pristine beaches, enjoy tropical resorts, and soak in island serenity.
Multi-Destination Journey – A perfectly balanced itinerary combining the bustling cityscapes of Hong Kong, cultural richness of Kuala Lumpur, and tropical bliss of Bali.

accommodations.description:
Dorsett Mongkok Hong Kong

 Escape to Asia's ultimate urban adventure at the award-winning Dorsett Mongkok Hotel, your gateway to Hong Kong's most electrifying district. 

This TripAdvisor Traveller's Choice winner for 13 consecutive years puts you just 7 minutes from Olympic MTR Station and in the heart of buzzing Mong Kok, where neon-lit streets pulse with authentic street food, late-night markets, and endless discoveries. 

With complimentary shuttle service whisking you to Tsim Sha Tsui and major attractions, legendarily helpful staff who go above and beyond, you're not just booking a room—you're securing your home base for conquering Hong Kong's urban jungle while enjoying the comfort of high-ceiling rooms with stunning city skyline views and all the modern amenities you need to recha

accommodations.name: Mercure Kuala Lumpur Shaw Parade
accommodations.description:
Mercure Kuala Lumpur Shaw Parade

 Mercure Kuala Lumpur Shaw Parade is praised by many travelers for its cleanliness, with rooms and public areas often described as spotless. Guests frequently commend the spacious and comfortable rooms, highlighting the beds and city views. The hotel's location is popular for its proximity to shopping and dining, though the busy road is noted by some. 

While the staff earn praise for friendliness, a few guests warn of slow check-ins. Amenities like the rooftop pool are appreciated, despite some breakfast limitations.

accommodations.name: Kuta Beach Hotel
accommodations.description:
Kuta Beach Hotel

 Kuta Beach Hotel offers a stylish stay in Bali’s vibrant district, featuring a rooftop pool with stunning Kuta Beach views. Just a short walk from top attractions, guests can explore shopping malls, a water park, and lively nightclubs. 

Whether relaxing by the beach, shopping, or enjoying Bali’s nightlife, the hotel provides the perfect base for a tropical escape. The hotel features 140 well appointed rooms, including Superior, Deluxe Balcony, Deluxe Garden View, Junior Suite, Premier Suite and a unique Thematic Family Room, perfect for every kind of traveler.

title: Luxury Hong Kong Kuala Lumpur & Bali
description:
This 13-day multi-destination adventure takes you through three of Asia’s most exciting and contrasting destinations – the dazzling cityscapes of Hong Kong, the cultural and spiritual heart of Kuala Lumpur, and the serene tropical beauty of Bali.

Your journey begins in Hong Kong, a city where East meets West in a spectacular skyline of soaring skyscrapers and bustling harbor views. A hop-on hop-off bus tour gives you the freedom to discover Hong Kong’s iconic landmarks, charming neighborhoods, and vibrant markets at your own pace.

From there, travel to Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia’s lively capital, where tradition blends with modernity. The highlight is an unforgettable excursion to the Batu Caves, one of the most revered Hindu shrines in the world, where the striking golden statue of Lord Murugan stands guard over ancient limestone caves. You’ll also enjoy enriching cultural stops, from pewter craftsmanship at Royal Selangor to colorful batik artistry and sweet treats at Beryl’s Chocolate Gallery.

The final leg of your trip takes you to Bali, Indonesia’s island paradise. Begin in Ubud, the cultural and artistic heart of Bali, surrounded by rice terraces, temples, and wellness retreats. Then move on to Seminyak, a stylish coastal town known for its golden beaches, vibrant nightlife, boutique shopping, and spectacular sunsets. With six full days at leisure, you’ll have the flexibility to relax, explore, and experience Bali your way.

This well-balanced itinerary offers the perfect combination of sightseeing, culture, and relaxation, making it ideal for travelers who want to experience the diversity and beauty of Asia in one unforgettable holiday.

accommodations.name: Nina Hotel Tsuen Wan West
accommodations.description:
Nina Hotel Tsuen Wan West

 Nina Hotel Tsuen Wan West is a genuine Hong Kong landmark that presides over the Kowloon Peninsula’s Western edge. Standing between Tai Mo Shan mountain and the Rambler Channel, it was originally planned to be the world’s tallest building. However, its proximity to the airport flightpath led to it becoming the two sparkling sister towers of today. This Nina’s location is packed with appeals. Firstly, getting here is easy. It’s less than 20 minutes by road from Central, Tsim Sha Tsui is a 12 minute train ride, and the international airport is 25 minutes by the highway. So you see, it’s an efficient base from which to explore. But be warned, you may not want to leave.

accommodations.name: MiCasa All Suite Hotel
accommodations.description:
MiCasa All Suite Hotel

 Nestled in the vibrant heart of Kuala Lumpur, the 5-star MiCasa All Suite Hotel offers travellers a blend of comfort, luxury, and convenience. This upscale hotel features exquisitely designed suites with contemporary furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, plush bedding, and expansive living areas, ensuring a home-away-from-home experience.

Dining options include the renowned Cilantro Restaurant & Wine Bar, serving a fusion of French and Japanese cuisines, and The Deli, offering international dishes and pastries in a cozy atmosphere. Guests can relax in the tropical-inspired swimming pool, stay fit in the fully equipped gym, or enjoy the tennis court. Business travellers benefit from well-appointed meeting rooms and comprehensive services.

Located in the prestigiou

accommodations.name: Ayung Resort Ubud
accommodations.description:
Ayung Resort Ubud

 Nestled in Bali's lush rainforests, Ayung Resort Ubud offers a luxurious retreat blending natural beauty with cultural heritage. Enjoy elegant accommodations with traditional Balinese aesthetics and modern amenities, gourmet dining at Wijaya Kusuma, and personalized treatments at the wellness and spa center. Engage in activities like temple tours, white-water rafting, and traditional Balinese classes. The resort’s stunning location along the Ayung River provides a tranquil escape, while exceptional service ensures a memorable stay.

accommodations.name: The Amala Boutique Retreat
accommodations.description:
The Amala Boutique Retreat

 In the ancient Sanskrit language, Amala means pure and unspoiled. The word has endured over the centuries and today graces the latest wellness retreat on the magical island of Bali. The small and discreet Amala offers its guests an oasis of tranquility, a world away from the stresses and pains of daily life. The Amala is an exquisitely designed collection of 22 luxurious villas and estates, including the intimate Honeymoon Pool Villa, the magnificent Amala Residence, and additional 2 units of Studio Rooms. At the heart, and soul, of the lush and peaceful grounds stands TULA Wellness, a modern holistic spa with one of the most comprehensive and creative ranges of wellness treatments as well as wellness retreats in Seminyak. title: Luxury Malaysia, Singapore And Bali Rainforest And Beach Stay
description:
Luxurious Rainforest and Beach Getaway: Malaysia, Singapore & Bali - Save £500 per person!

Embark on a captivating 12-day journey from the UK to Kuala Lumpur, Ubud, Seminyak, and Singapore, with all transfers included. Start with a two-night stay at the luxurious 5-star Pullman Kuala Lumpur City Centre - Hotel & Residences, exploring iconic sights like the Petronas Twin Towers and Batu Caves.

 Then, fly to Ubud, Bali, for a three-night retreat at the 5-star Ayung Resort Ubud, set amidst tropical rainforests with stunning views and access to cultural sites like the Monkey Forest. 

Next, transfer to Kuta for a four-night stay at the 5-star Montigo Resorts, enjoying beachside activities, spa treatments, and vibrant nightlife.

 Conclude your adventure with two nights at the 5-star Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel in Singapore, exploring Marina Bay Sands, Gardens by the Bay, and Orchard Road. This seamless travel experience includes private transfers and daily breakfasts, ensuring a luxurious and memorable Southeast Asian adventure.

title: 3 Night Escape to Venice with 4 star Hotel and Breakfast
description:
Nestled in a lagoon in the Adriatic, Venice is an Italian slither of paradise that excites even the most seasoned of travellers. Why? Because there is nowhere else quite like it on Earth.

A firm feature on many holidaymakers’ bucket-lists, Venice is exciting and unusual. Built on more than 100 small islands, the city has no roads – only canals. Boats and gondolas slice through the emerald-hued waters like knives to a birthday cake and the labyrinthine streets, like never-ending strands of spaghetti, bathe in the scent of rich, Italian delicacies and invite endless wandering.  

Within their innermost crevices and corners, you’ll find splendid-looking timeworn buildings, dinky little trattorias serving up fluffy mozzarella and gnocchi doused in truffle oil, and quirky antique stores. 

Emerging from the winding alleys and looping streets, feast your eyes upon the city’s central plazas and main areas of interest, including Piazza San Marco (the central square home to St Mark’s Basilica and the Campanile bell tower) and the Grand Canal thoroughfare, fringed by Renaissance and Gothic palaces. 

Visit Doge’s Palace, wander Venice’s Jewish Quarter and haggle at Rialto Market. The Rialto Bridge is one of Venice’s most renowned icons – scurry up to the highest part, huddle around the edge and put that selfie stick to good use. 

And should you want to spend one of your days away from the hustle and bustle of mainland Venice, the surrounding miniature islands (such as Murano, Burano and Torcello) are home to an abundance of nature and rainbow-coloured houses. Accessible by water taxi, they are just a short ride away. And with 24-hour travel cards priced at 20 euros, there’s no excuse not to hop around and see as much of this aquatic wonderland as possible. 

At night, rest your head at your four-star abode – the Mercure Venice Marghera Hotel. With bright, comfortable rooms and located within spitting distance of the historic centre of Venice (only a 15 minute bus ride away) expect nothing less than a cosy, accommodating stay just outside the city’s core.

title: 3 Nights Secret Hideaway in Palermo
description:
A much treasured novel entitled “The Leopard” conjures up everything that is so enticing about Palermo. The buildings, the people, the empires and the sheer beauty of the place! Lampedusa was filled with intrigue and wonder at the phantoms that must hide in waiting; “these monument of the past, standing round us like lovely mute ghosts…all these things have formed our character.”We do hope we will ignite your imaginations with our wonderful trip to Palermo!

Stroll across Palermo’s backstreets to find the facade of a derelict Baroque palazzo, marked with a plaque bearing a forgotten name. The sight of this noble structure is baffling. To stand alone at sunset watching the locals whilst savouring the scent of lemon and fresh fish wafting from the market stalls, you soon drift off into a dream of what the palace once was, but mostly you like it just as it is, a magnificent residence ravished by time and history!

The four star Porta Felice Hotel is located in the very heart of the historical centre, a charming spot home to Foro Italico, Piazza Marina and Via Alloro and manages to reflect a real sense of history and legacy. The hotel was renovated from a building of the 18th century and cultivates a sense of luxury and refinement complete with its Wellness Club offering a sauna, steam bath and Jacuzzi.
