accommodations.name: MClub Alicudi, Sicily
accommodations.description:
MClub Alicudi, Sicily

 Surrounded by 30 hectares of olive-tree groves and rich parkland, this large 4-star resort offers a panoramic location just 600 m from the sea and private sandy beaches. Near Agrigento and the Selinunte archaeological sites, MClub Alicudi provides a wide variety of sport facilities.
Stay in en suite rooms that offer a balcony, a terrace or a garden. Each room features a splendid view of the park.
MClub Alicudi features an entertainment programme. Enjoy water aerobics poolside or on the beach. There are games, contests, shows and evening events for all ages.
Relax in the large outdoor pool. Try your hand at tennis, basketball and volleyball. MClub Alicudi is equipped for bowling, archery, football and mini golf. You can go sailing, wind surfing or canoeing.

title: Sorrento and Sicily A Rail Adventure Through Southern Italy
description:
Italian Journey: Sorrento & Sicily with Sleeper Train Adventure

Experience the captivating beauty of southern Italy with this unique 7-night holiday combining coastal Sorrento with historic Sicily, connected by an enchanting overnight train journey.

Begin with three nights in Sorrento, perched above the stunning Bay of Naples. Explore this charming town at your leisure, perhaps venturing to nearby Pompeii or taking a boat trip along the spectacular Amalfi Coast. Your sea view room offers the perfect backdrop to unwind after days of exploration.

Your adventure continues aboard the Intercity Notte sleeper train from Naples to Syracuse. As you sleep in your comfortable cabin, your train crosses the Strait of Messina by ferry – a unique travel experience that maximises your holiday time.

Wake to the warm Sicilian sunshine as you arrive in Syracuse, where you'll spend three nights in the historic Ortigia district. Discover ancient Greek ruins, wander through Baroque piazzas, and perhaps relax on the local beaches before your journey home from nearby Catania.

This thoughtfully designed holiday offers the perfect balance of guided travel and independent exploration across two of Italy's most beautiful regions.

Solo travellers welcome from £799.
whats_included: Flights from UK to Naples and return from Catania
3 nights at Mar Hotel Alimuri, Sorrento in a Sea View Room with daily breakfast
Overnight Sleeper Train journey from Naples to Syracuse (Sicily)
Overnight journey on the Intercity Notte sleeper train in a 4-person couchette cabin with breakfast
3 nights at Lanterne Magiche Ortigia, Syracuse in a Studio Apartment (accommodation only)
highlights:
Sorrento: Enjoy breathtaking views across the Bay of Naples
to Mount Vesuvius

Coastal Exploration: Easy access to the stunning Amalfi
Coast and the ruins of Pompeii

Unique Travel Experience: Overnight train journey with your
cabin transported by ferry across the Strait of Messina

Syracuse: Discover Sicily's ancient heart with its
remarkable Greek and Roman ruins

Ortigia: Stay in the historic island district with its
beautiful Baroque architecture and vibrant piazzas

Authentic Sicily: Experience the relaxed Sicilian lifestyle
with easy access to local beaches

title: Splendours of Rome and Venice
description:
“Italy is a dream that keeps returning for the rest of your life” – Anna Akhmatova, poet

Italy, ah Italy! Every heart beats for this capsule of bygone and changing times. Italian art, architecture and culture have left an unforgettable mark on many. And now the Italian Dream is yours!  Why not visit both Rome and Venice in just one fantastic trip? How? As your god of Mercury, we make the arrangements, so you can explore two cities for the price of one!

First up, we fly you to the Eternal City of Rome for two nights at the 4 star The Raganelli Hotel. With round-the-clock energy and jaw-dropping views, Rome offers everything you’ve been dreaming of. Hiding more secrets than its many emperors, from the centre of civilisation to the Roman Empire, this glam city is the hub of awe and wonder.

Next up, thunder across the vastness of Italy's plains in a scenic train crossing the magnificent country. Chug across the vine-sprinkled hillsides and see village churches and solitary elderly men hoeing fields.Various timings are available so please feel free to ask for an upgrade.

Hotel Plaza is a 4-star hotel right in front of Venezia Mestre Train Station. Easy to reach from Venezia-Mestre highway exit, this hotel is the ideal base for all those who are travelling by car and want to visit the wonderful Venice and the other cities in the surroundings.

Special attention to the clientele is shown in a comfortable and relaxing environment creating all the basic necessity for a wonderful stay.

Fly back to the UK from Venice, pumped full of lasting memories of both magical cities!
whats_included: We take care of return flights from the UK
2 nights stay at the 4 star The Raganelli Hotel in Rome
Standard rail travel from Rome to Venice
2 nights accommodation at the 4 star Hotel Plaza Venice in Venice (Traveller's Choice 2022)
Both hotels include daily breakfast
Free Wi-Fi
highlights: Two Italian cities for the price of one with 4 star hotels
See Rome’s endless treasures from the Colosseum to the Forum
Marvel at the Sistine Chapel and its fresco by Michelangelo
Throw a coin into the Trevi Fountain to ensure your return
Train journey takes you through breathtaking scenery
Sit back and discover the sights of Venice on a Gondola Ride
“Don’t Look Now” but speeding your way is the water bus!
Head to a hidden myriad of historic wonders and art galleries
Get your camera ready for the oh so elegant Rialto Bridge

accommodations.name: The Raganelli Hotel
accommodations.description:
The Raganelli Hotel

 The Raganelli Hotel, situated close to the Vatican City, is an elegant hotel offering all the comforts expected from a four-star hotel.

With 45 rooms, the Raganelli Hotel has the ideal size for combining the cosiness of a small boutique hotel with the comfort of a luxurious four star hotel.

Ask at reception for details on how to reach the historic centre by public transport. The staff will be on hand to provide you with all the tourist information you may need to make your stay in Rome special. Start the day with a delicious fresh buffet breakfast in the breakfast hall, enjoy stunning views from the hotel’s roof garden during the day, or relax with a refreshing beverage at the hotel’s bar. 

Ideal for couples looking for a romantic break in the Eternal City, or fami

accommodations.name: Hotel Plaza, Venice
accommodations.description:
Hotel Plaza, Venice

 Situated opposite Venice Mestre railway station, Hotel Plaza Venice Mestre is a short drive from the famous Grand Canal and Church of San Salvador. 

The Hotel Plaza in Venice Mestre has recently been renovated to include improved sound-proofing technologies for all rooms.The Plaza's rooms feature classic furnishings in warm colors. Each has a fully equipped bathroom with modern shower. 

For breakfast, enjoy the extensive buffet, or dine in the hotel’s Soul Kitchen restaurant which is open all day and boasts an eclectic menu of Italian and traditional dishes, and enjoy wine from its international cellar. For something more informal, the modern and stylish Plaza Café provides the perfect setting for an informal brunch, cocktail or appetizing first course dish.

title: Tranquil Ischia Island Holiday with Half Board Basis
description:
Fancy crossing the brilliant blue waters of Italy to a sun-soaked paradise, including return flights,transfers, half-board and a four-night hotel stay? Then you have to head to the tiny Tyrrhenian island of Ischia, the most vibrant islet in the Bay of Naples.

Ischia faces the gulf of Naples and is part of the Phlegraean island group generated by powerful underwater volcanoes, along with Procida and the deserted Vivara. According to the Daily Mail it is “Ischia’s sense of La Dolce Vita which makes it so special.” The harbour town of Forio has retro Hollywood charm. It is easy to imagine the fifties jetset, such as Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton tanning themselves and strolling down the cobbled lanes to sip Campari at one of the delightful cafes and restaurants – this is island decadence at its best!

Throughout the year, the island acts as a backdrop for numerous events, folkloristic feasts and displays, classical and pop music concerts and theatre performances. Ischia is tradition, hospitality and undisputed beauty to be appreciated with your own eyes and enjoyed at your own pleasure. 

In this holiday, you will experience and discover the fantastic world that awaits you at Hotel Parco Dei Principi with the ancient scents of saltwater, the endless beaches, the glitter of shopping streets, the green paths, the land and sea cuisine, the oldest spa in the world starting at only £499 per person.
whats_included: We take care of return flights from the UK to Naples
4 nights accommodation in 4 star Hotel Parco Dei Principi
Stay in a Standard Double room
Hassle free return transfers
A stay in Ischia includes Half Board Basis
Stay connected with friends & family with free Wi-Fi
highlights: Sweep across the dazzling Bay of Naples
Hike Mount Epomeo to see the island and the Bay of Naples
Have access to  the hotel's outdoor pool and heated mineral pool
Immerse in the history of the Aqueduct of the Pillars
Take a very special journey by transfers
See the passionate Ndrezzarta dance in the streets of Ischia
Take a short romantic stroll to reach the nearest beach
Look out for pirates at the impressive Tower in Forio d'Ischia

accommodations.name: Hotel Parco Dei Principi
accommodations.description:
Hotel Parco Dei Principi

 Hotel Parco dei Principi is set in the residential zone in Zaro, one of the most typical places of the island of Ischia, in very panoramic position.

The hotel has 59 rooms all furnished with care and full of amenities. All the rooms are graceful and offer comfortable environment. 

The hotel features an indoor and an outdoor swimming pool with sun loungers and parasols laid out for use by the water's edge. Guests can work on their tans on the hotel's sun terrace or take advantage of relaxing massage treatments or special spa packages. Those looking to stay in shape can enjoy a workout at the gym. 

At the restaurant American dishes are served. The in-house restaurant is a great place to sample local specialities whilst enjoying a beautiful view.

title: Venice Getaway with Breakfast
description:
Located in the northeast of Italy, Venice is a romantic and enchanting city of turquoise canals, ancient palaces and serenading gondoliers. Listed as a World Heritage Site, the city’s contributions to art, architecture and music are evident round every corner. Whether you’re falling in love on a moonlit gondola ride or soothing your soul at an orchestral symphony, Venice promises to dazzle you with its artistic flair.

Even if you chose to do nothing else but wander the streets your entire stay, this city would still dazzle you with its history, culture and romance. Sit back and relax upon a Gondola and adore a scenic punt around this picturesque city to wherever your heart desires. Venice is a city of culture; home to some of the most exceptional works of art in all forms. Not only is the city itself a work of art, there’s a vast collection of works from some of the most celebrated artists in history. 

The Smart Hotel is a comfy design hotel located few minutes far from Venice city centre. Easy to reach from Venezia-Mestre highway exit, this hotel is the ideal base for all those who are travelling by car and want to visit the wonderful Venice and the other cities in the surroundings.
whats_included: Return flights from UK
3 nights accommodation in 4 Star Smart Hotel
Bed & Breakfast Board Basis
Free Wi-Fi access
highlights: Enjoy unrivalled views of the magical St Mark’s Square
Fall in love with the artistic displays on offer from Italian greats, such as Carpaccio, Tintoretto.
Duck down the side streets and discover the specialities Venice has to offer.
Take in Venice’s magnificent architecture on a romantic gondola ride along the Grand Canal.
“Don’t Look Now” but speeding your way is the water bus!
Get your camera ready for the oh so elegant Rialto Bridge
Be whisked over to Murano for a glass making tour
Try your luck at the Casinò di Venezia, Buona fortuna!

accommodations.name: Smart Hotel Holiday
accommodations.description:
Smart Hotel Holiday

 The Smart Hotel Holiday is a comfy design hotel located few minutes far from Venice city centre. Easy to reach from Venezia-Mestre highway exit, this hotel is the ideal base for all those who are travelling by car and want to visit the wonderful Venice and the other cities in the surroundings.

Completely renewed, the Hotel Holiday offers 89 spacious rooms. All rooms in the hotel offer modern comforts such as wi-fi connection, air conditioning,TV Sat and LCD. En-suite facilities include marble bathrooms with hair dryer and towel warming heaters.

In restaurant you will taste the best Italian traditional cuisine surrounded by a welcoming and familiar atmosphere. It has a great capacity, so it is perfect for any kind of event or for business meetings.

title: Verona And Lake Garda Holiday With Breakfast
description:
During this twin centre holiday, start with Verona – made immortal by Shakespeare’s famed Romeo and Juliet – before finishing with time to relax at Italy’s largest lake. You can take in the beautiful architecture, visit Juliet’s Balcony, people watch on the Piazza Bra and see the Arena, one of the world’s best examples of a Roman amphitheatre.
Hotel Italia is a charming 3-star hotel located in the center of Verona, very close to the Old Town with its magnificent sights. The Cathedral and the Roman Theatre are just 800 meters away. Nearby are also Piazza delle Erbe, and Juliet’s House and you can reach the Arena with a nice walk through the romantic streets of the Old Town.
After enjoying an overnight stay in Verona we'll whisk you off by train to Lake Garda. Stunning rustic scenery and enchanting towns embody the timeless appeal of this majestic lake. Whether you’re looking to unwind as you get to know the café culture, or get active on the water, a Lake Garda holiday is the ideal choice for an idyllic Italian escape. Lake Garda holidays aren’t just about relaxing and enjoying beautiful views but also offer the opportunities for a little fun and excitement.
Hotel Riel (Travellers' Choice 2022) is only 100 metres from the shores of Lake Garda, near Lugana Beach. The Riel Hotel is 400 metres from the Virgilio Thermal Centre and Sirmione is a short drive away. Rooms at the Riel are large and each has a unique view of the lake, the garden, the swimming pool or the canal.
whats_included: Return flight from UK
1 night stay at Hotel Italia, Verona
Train transfer from Verona to Lake Garda and vice versa
Spend 4 nights' at Hotel Riel, Lake Garda (Travellers' Choice 2022)
Breakfast included throughout your holiday
highlights: Opera lovers head to the stone benches of Verona’s vast amphitheatre
Join the local gossip at a bar with a pot of tea and Romeo and Juliet kisses
Stroll with your beloved across the historic centre – UNESCO Word Heritage Site
Wander through its lemon and olive groves.
Mingle with other shoppers at one of the local street markets or in the elegant boutiques.
Eat and drink at one of the many bars, cafes and restaurants.
Go swimming, windsurfing or simply soak up the sun from one of the beaches.
Enjoy the lakeside amusement parks.
Take the cable car up into the surrounding mountains to marvel at the stupendous views, go hiking or, in winter, skiing.

accommodations.name: Hotel Italia, Verona
accommodations.description:
Hotel Italia, Verona

 Hotel Italia is just a 5-minute walk from Borgo Trento Hospital, in a quiet neighbourhood of Verona. The popular Piazza delle Erbe square and Juliet's balcony are just a 15-minute walk away. You have excellent public transport connections to all the city's attractions.
Each guest room comes with satellite TV. Air conditioning is available throughout the summer. The rooms are a mix of styles from classic to modern. Some come with four-poster beds, others with a small terrace.
A highlight of the hotel is its professional and helpful team of staff. They are always on hand to share local knowledge and recommend the best places to eat and drink.
Your day at the Italia Hotel begins with an italian breakfast. You can relax or enjoy a cocktail in the new terrace-garden bar.

accommodations.name: Hotel Riel, Lake Garda
accommodations.description:
Hotel Riel, Lake Garda

 Hotel Riel is only 100 m from the shores of Lake Garda, near Lugana Beach. The Riel Hotel is 400 m from the Virgilio Thermal Centre and Sirmione is a short drive away. Motorboat excursions can be booked on request, and depart from the hotel's private port.

Rooms at the Riel are large and each has a unique view of the lake, the garden, the swimming pool or the canal. Some come with a balcony.

The restaurant and pizzeria Al Braciere serves local cuisine. During pleasant weather meals are served in the veranda.

title: Vienna and Venice A Twin City European Escape By Sleeper Train
description:
From imperial elegance to canal-side charm – discover two unforgettable cities in one magical journey - Save £200 per person!

Embark on a 5-night European escape that combines the cultural richness of Vienna with the romantic allure of Venice. Begin in Austria’s capital, where grand architecture, classical music, and traditional cafés create a refined and welcoming atmosphere.

The highlight of your journey is the Nightjet sleeper train – a relaxing and atmospheric way to travel overnight from Vienna to Venice. As you glide through alpine landscapes in a cosy sleeper cabin, the train experience becomes a memorable part of the adventure.

Arrive in Venice as the sun rises, ready to discover the beauty of its canals, hidden alleyways, and stunning architecture. With two nights in this iconic city, you’ll have plenty of time to explore its treasures, from St. Mark’s Square to gondola rides along the Grand Canal.

A holiday that blends comfort, culture and classic European charm – ideal for those seeking both elegance and exploration.
whats_included: Flights from UK to Vienna
2 nights at 4* Hotel PLAZA INN Amedia Wien, Vienna with daily breakfast
Overnight Sleeper Train journey from Vienna to Venice

Flights back from Venice to UK
highlights: Explore the grand palaces, art, and café culture of Vienna
Travel overnight in comfort on the Nightjet sleeper train through the Alps
Wake up in Venice and experience its unique canals, bridges and piazzas
Enjoy two iconic European cities with minimal travel hassle
A perfect mix of culture, romance, and scenic adventure

accommodations.name: PLAZA INN Amedia Wien
accommodations.description:
The Hotel PLAZA INN Amedia Wien also presents itself in a modern and comfortable way. In 2012 it was redesigned, 4 stars were awarded for the service. The hotel building is located in Vienna's third district, directly between the airport and the city centre. Not far from the hotel is the Viennese business district "Landstraße", with many exclusive shopping facilities and restaurants. Our house is therefore an excellent starting point for extensive tours through the city or as a starting point for trips into the beautiful landscape of Vienna's surroundings.



Typically Vienna
Whether city trip or holiday for families with children - book the PLAZA INN Amedia Wienfor a comfortable stay, which earns itself 4 stars. The modern furnishing of the hotel's rooms and suites leaves nothing to be desired. Free WLAN is available everywhere. In the morning, a breakfast typical of the country ensures a good start to the day. In the evening, restaurants and a bar nearby invite you to linger with tasty dishes and drinks from Austria. You can book a parking space for your vehicle in the hotel's underground car park. If you travel by train to the main station or by plane to the airport, Vienna's public transport system will take you quickly to your hotel. The sights in the centre of the city can be easily reached by tram or bus. We recommend that visitors book an "EASY City Pass Vienna", which can be used throughout their entire stay. Any number of trips with all public transport and discounts in various shops, restaurants and museums.

accommodations.name: Russo Palace, Venice
accommodations.description:
Russo Palace, Venice

 Located in Venice, Russo Palace Hotel is by the sea, a 1-minute drive from Lido of Venice and 7 minutes from Venice Convention Center.

The romantic rooms at the Russo Palace Hotel at the Lido of Venice are near historic downtown Venice, perfect for a weekend for two, with all the most modern comforts and an intimate and relaxing atmosphere.

Satisfy your appetite at the hotel's coffee shop/café, or stay in and take advantage of the room service. Wrap up your day with a drink at the bar/lounge. Sound-proof and available in different categories, the rooms have adjustable air conditioning, satellite TV & Sky, wireless internet and a direct line.

title: Whisk Yourself Away To The Floating Paradise Of Venice
description:
Venice is a romantic and enchanting city of turquoise canals, ancient palaces and serenading gondoliers. It is a whimsical wonderland binded together by the ethos of its surreal charm, and the magnetism of its fairy tale allure. The quintessential ‘City of Romance’ has the power to alter your thought process and take you into a different world; into the world of fantasies, mystery and awe.

Epic grandeurs, breathtaking scenery, fabulous feasts – When you are in Venice, you are in for the Sojourn of your lifetime. Get mesmerized by the Piazza San Marco, described by Napolean as ‘the drawing room of Europe’, or why not just walk down to the eastern end and feel the grandeur of the St Mark’s Basilica. The most famous of the city’s churches, it has always been revered to as the symbol of Venetian wealth and power..

But the best way to discover the real essence of Venice lies in exploring its beautiful lagoons, the criss crossing canals and some of the most beautiful stone bridges. Get lost in the numerous alleys, or hop on to a Gondola, and let Venice cast a captivating spell on you, which may seem too hard to break in your lifetime….

Facing the magical Grand Canal, the Hotel Carlton on the Grand Canal is the ideal destination for an unforgettable holiday in Venice. The pastel colours, Venetian plaster-work and wall-lamps in Murano glass, the Hotel Carlton on the Grand Canal will enchant you with its refined elegance. The hotel is the ideal place for a romantic evening with a splendid view of the Grand Canal and the Venice skyline.

title: Discover Japan Cities Culture and Cuisine
description:
This 10-day Japan tour offers a perfect introduction to the country’s rich culture, modern cities, and iconic landscapes. Begin your journey in Tokyo, where cutting-edge technology blends seamlessly with traditional temples and vibrant neighborhoods. Enjoy a full-day excursion to Mount Fuji and Hakone, showcasing Japan’s natural beauty and scenic views.

Travel by the world-famous Shinkansen bullet train to Kyoto, the cultural heart of Japan, where historic temples, imperial landmarks, and traditional streets reflect centuries of heritage. With guided sightseeing and leisure time, you can explore the city at your own pace before continuing to Osaka, Japan’s lively culinary capital.

In Osaka, experience the city’s famous street food culture and energetic atmosphere, along with free days for shopping and sightseeing. With comfortable hotel stays, efficient transportation, guided tours, and ample leisure time, this itinerary is ideal for first-time visitors seeking a well-balanced and memorable Japan experience.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Tokyo
itinerary.description: Arrive in Tokyo, where you will be welcomed by our representative at the airport and transferred to your hotel. After check-in, the rest of the day is free for you to relax and recover from your journey or take a short walk around the neighborhood to get a first glimpse of the vibrant city.

itinerary.title: Mount Fuji and Hakone Full-Day Tour
itinerary.description: After breakfast, depart on a full-day excursion to Mount Fuji and Hakone. Visit the Mount Fuji 5th Station (weather permitting) to enjoy breathtaking views of Japan’s most iconic peak. Continue to the Hakone region, where you will experience a scenic Lake Ashi cruise and enjoy panoramic views from the Hakone Ropeway. In the evening, return to Tokyo by motorcoach.

itinerary.title: Tokyo at Leisure
itinerary.description: Today is free for you to explore Tokyo at your own pace. You may choose to visit famous areas such as Asakusa, Shibuya, Akihabara, or Ginza, explore local markets, enjoy shopping, or experience the city’s unique food culture.

itinerary.title: Tokyo to Kyoto by Bullet Train
itinerary.description: After breakfast, transfer to the station to board the high-speed Shinkansen (bullet train) to Kyoto. Enjoy the smooth and efficient journey through the Japanese countryside. Upon arrival, transfer to your hotel and spend the rest of the day at leisure, discovering Kyoto’s traditional atmosphere.

itinerary.title: Kyoto Morning City Tour
itinerary.description: This morning, enjoy a guided city tour of Kyoto’s most famous landmarks. Visit Nijo Castle, known for its historic architecture, followed by the stunning Kinkaku-ji Temple, also called the Golden Pavilion. The tour concludes with a visit to the Kyoto Imperial Palace, offering insight into Japan’s imperial history. The afternoon is free for personal exploration.

itinerary.title: Kyoto at Leisure
itinerary.description: Spend the day at your own pace exploring Kyoto. You may visit Fushimi Inari Shrine with its thousands of red torii gates, stroll through the Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, or wander through the historic Gion district, known for its traditional streets and geisha culture.

itinerary.title: Kyoto to Osaka by Train
itinerary.description: After breakfast, travel by train to Osaka. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel. The rest of the day is free to explore the city, enjoy shopping, or experience Osaka’s lively entertainment and dining scenes.

itinerary.title: Osaka Street Food Tour
itinerary.description: Today, enjoy a guided street food tour in Osaka, often referred to as Japan’s kitchen. Visit Kuromon Market and the lively Sennichimae area, sampling a variety of local specialties such as takoyaki and okonomiyaki while learning about Osaka’s rich food culture.

itinerary.title: Osaka at Leisure
itinerary.description: This day is free for you to explore Osaka independently. You may visit Osaka Castle, stroll through the famous Dotonbori area, enjoy shopping, or opt for an optional visit to Universal Studios Japan.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, check out from the hotel and transfer to the airport for your departure, bringing your memorable Japan journey to an end.

Discover a cozy space with a balance of comfort and functionality to ensure a comfortable stay. [Make your reservations via our official website to find the best deal]

accommodations.description: Ibis Styles Kyoto Station is an economy hotel conveniently located in front of the Hachijo West Exit of JR Kyoto Station, just across the pedestrian crossing. The limousine bus stop from the domestic and international airports is also approximately 3 minutes' walk away.

accommodations.description: The Ibis Osaka Umeda is a well-located, bright, economy hotel in Osaka's bustling Umeda district, perfect for exploring business and entertainment areas, just a 4-minute walk from JR Osaka & Umeda Stations, offering modern decor, comfy beds, good breakfast, and easy access to shopping like HEP Five and Grand Front Osaka. It provides a lively, budget-friendly base with reliable Accor service, great for accessing city transport and attractions like Universal Studios and Osaka Castle.

title: Journey through Riga and Tallinn
description:
Since regaining independence in 1991 and joining the EU, beguiling Latvia and beautiful Estonia are true rising stars on the travel to-do list. These wonderful countries burst with a rich and colorful history; from their capital cities to the surrounding forests, dotted with manor houses and medieval castles; to acre upon acre of Rococo churches; from emerald lakes to the pristine rivers, there’s a visual and untouchable vibrancy throughout the two Baltic cities of Riga and Tallinn.  

The setting for Riga really has to be seen to be believed. Here, the architecture is so beautiful it dazzles and the river is the deepest shade of blue. Sitting on the glorious south coast of the Gulf of Riga, at the mouth of the Daugava, the blissful river views are accompanied by a jigsaw puzzle of Hansel and Gretel houses, with its strip of Mr Kipling iced roofs and yellow frescos.

Travelling by luxury coach to Tallinn, Estonia’s mesmerizing beauty lies in the leafy green plains, where the sun lights up the pastel-painted villages, mansions adorn the horizon, vast natural areas present a David Attenborough-worthy roll call of waterfalls and national parks with the UNSECO Old Town of Tallinn rising up in all its historical and cultural glory. Our favorite city hot spot is the 1404 Gothic town hall. The lovely Freedom Square lies at its heart, featuring St. John’s Church, an underground shopping centre and a Victory Column honouring the Estonian War of Independence.

accommodations.name: The Rixwell Gertrude Hotel
accommodations.description:
The Rixwell Gertrude Hotel

 The Rixwell Gertrude Hotel is a family-friendly, boutique hotel located in a 19th century Art Nouveau style building. This 4-star hotel is a 15-minute walk from the St. Gertrude Church, the Vermane Central Park, the Latvian National Opera, and the Old Town. 

The spacious rooms of the Rixwell Gertrude Hotel feature a classic interior décor, with dark wooden elements. Each has a work desk and private bathroom with a bath or shower, free toiletries and a hairdryer. All rooms are soundproofed and feature satellite TV, safety deposit box, tea and coffee making facilities and ironing facilities, as well as a direct dial telephone.

A breakfast buffet is available in the mornings, included in the room rate. Lobby bar offers a choice of alcoholic and non alcoholic dri

accommodations.name: ibis Styles Tallinn
accommodations.description:
ibis Styles Tallinn is inspired by photography where every room becomes a kind of photo studio that speaks of Tallinn. Ideally located, the hotel offers stylish rooms, playful spaces and extraordinary experiences that will keep your creativity alive throughout your stay. Ideal for business travellers, families and city explorers alike, ibis Styles Tallinn combines modern comfort with the quaint charm of Estonia. Here, you're not just staying in a hotel, you're stepping into a creative mood.

Nestled in the bustling core of Tallinn's city center and close to the magnificent Old Town, a location that is truly great, our hotel guarantees a pleasant stay in our carefully designed rooms across various categories.

title: Malaysia Discovery City, Heritage and Island Escape
description:
Save £500 per person!

Discover the best of Malaysia on this 8-day journey that perfectly blends city excitement and island relaxation. Begin your adventure in Kuala Lumpur, the nation’s vibrant capital, where modern skyscrapers meet rich cultural heritage. Visit iconic landmarks like the Batu Caves, explore local art and craft traditions, and enjoy time at leisure to shop, dine, and experience the city’s cosmopolitan charm.

Then, fly to Langkawi, Malaysia’s island paradise known for its crystal-clear waters, lush rainforests, and white sandy beaches. Spend your days unwinding by the sea, exploring scenic spots such as the Langkawi Sky Bridge, or joining optional tours like island hopping and sunset cruises.

Perfect for couples, families, and leisure travelers alike, this tour offers a seamless blend of culture, adventure, and relaxation, giving you a true taste of Malaysia’s diversity and beauty.
excerpt: Discover the best of Malaysia on this 8-day journey that perfectly blends city excitement and island relaxation. Begin your adventure in Kuala Lumpur, the nation’s vibrant capital, where modern skyscrapers meet rich cultural heritage. Visit iconic landmarks like the Batu Caves, explore local art and craft traditions, and enjoy time at leisure to shop, dine, and experience the city’s cosmopolitan charm.

3 nights stay at 5 star MiCasa All Suite Hotel (Studio superior) in Kuala Lumpur
Half-day Batu Caves Tour
Domestic flight from Kuala Lumpur to Langkawi

English-speaking local guide during tours
Entrance fees included at mentioned sightseeing attractions
Daily breakfast throughout your trip
All airport and hotel transfers by private air-conditioned vehicle
highlights: Batu Caves: Explore Malaysia’s iconic limestone cave temple dedicated to Lord Murugan.
Kuala Lumpur City Sights: Discover the Petronas Twin Towers and vibrant local culture.
Cultural Craft Stops: Visit the Royal Selangor Pewter Factory and traditional batik workshop.
Langkawi Beaches: Relax on pristine shores surrounded by tropical beauty.
Island Adventures: Enjoy optional island hopping, sunset cruises, and panoramic views from the Sky Bridge.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Kuala Lumpur
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Kuala Lumpur International Airport, you will be greeted by a local representative and transferred to your downtown hotel for check-in. The rest of the day is free for you to relax after your flight or begin exploring the lively capital city at your own pace. Overnight in Kuala Lumpur.

itinerary.title: Batu Caves Half-Day Tour
itinerary.description: After breakfast, set out for a guided half-day tour to the iconic Batu Caves, a remarkable limestone hill housing temples and Hindu shrines. Climb the colorful 272 steps to the main cave temple dedicated to Lord Murugan, and admire the world’s tallest golden statue of the deity standing at the entrance. The tour also includes visits to the Royal Selangor Pewter Factory, where you’ll witness the craftsmanship of pewter making, and an opportunity to observe traditional batik painting demonstrations. End the tour with a stop at the Beryl’s Chocolate Gallery, a paradise for chocolate lovers. Return to the hotel for leisure.

itinerary.title: Kuala Lumpur: Free at Leisure
itinerary.description: Enjoy breakfast at the hotel and spend the day at your own leisure. You may choose to explore Kuala Lumpur further, indulge in shopping at world-class malls, or join an optional excursion. Overnight in Kuala Lumpur.

itinerary.title: Kuala Lumpur to Langkawi
itinerary.description: After breakfast, check out and transfer to Kuala Lumpur International Airport for your flight to Langkawi, Malaysia’s island paradise. Upon arrival, meet your local representative and transfer to your hotel for check-in. The rest of the day is free to relax by the beach or explore the island’s scenic surroundings. Overnight in Langkawi.

itinerary.title: Langkawi: Free at Leisure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, enjoy a full day at leisure to relax by the beach, explore Langkawi’s charming towns, or join optional tours such as island hopping or a sunset dinner cruise. Overnight in Langkawi.

itinerary.title: Langkawi: Free at Leisure
itinerary.description: Spend your final full day in paradise at your own pace. Relax by the pool, pamper yourself with a spa treatment, or do some last-minute shopping at the Langkawi Duty-Free Outlets.

itinerary.title: Langkawi: Free at Leisure
itinerary.description: Another full day to enjoy Langkawi’s charms at your own. Discover the island’s highlights such as the Langkawi Sky Bridge, Eagle Square, and the Langkawi Cable Car, or simply soak up the sun on its white sandy beaches.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Enjoy breakfast at your hotel before check-out. Spend some free time at leisure until your transfer to Langkawi Airport for your onward departure flight, marking the end of your memorable Malaysian holiday.

accommodations.name: MiCasa All Suite Hotel
accommodations.description:
MiCasa All Suite Hotel

 Nestled in the vibrant heart of Kuala Lumpur, the 5-star MiCasa All Suite Hotel offers travellers a blend of comfort, luxury, and convenience. This upscale hotel features exquisitely designed suites with contemporary furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, plush bedding, and expansive living areas, ensuring a home-away-from-home experience.

Dining options include the renowned Cilantro Restaurant & Wine Bar, serving a fusion of French and Japanese cuisines, and The Deli, offering international dishes and pastries in a cozy atmosphere. Guests can relax in the tropical-inspired swimming pool, stay fit in the fully equipped gym, or enjoy the tennis court. Business travellers benefit from well-appointed meeting rooms and comprehensive services.

Located in the prestigiou

accommodations.name: Berjaya Langkawi Resort
accommodations.description:
Berjaya Langkawi Resort

 Set amidst lush tropical rainforest, yet just steps away from azure waters and a gorgeous beachfront, Berjaya Langkawi Resort is an invitation to a magical sojourn all wrapped up in nature’s gifts.

Set on the edge of tropical rainforest, Berjaya Langkawi Resort offers Malay-style chalets with private balconies and contemporary furnishings. Fitted with wooden decor, the spacious chalets are appointed with a flat-screen TV and a seating area. Certain units located within the rainforest while others are set above the water. 

You can relax with a pampering massage at the spa or workout at the gym while the children play at the kids' club. The staff at tour desk can help arrange island tours, yacht trips and water sports activities.

A buffet spread of local and inte

title: Malaysia Wonders Cities, Rainforests & Wildlife
description:
This 10-day journey offers the perfect blend of city excitement, cultural discovery, and natural wonders. Begin in Malaysia’s capital, Kuala Lumpur, where modern skyscrapers stand beside colonial landmarks, vibrant temples, and bustling street life. Fly onwards to Kuching in Sarawak, the gateway to Borneo, where you’ll explore charming riverside streets, experience a sunset wildlife cruise in search of dolphins, monkeys, crocodiles, and fireflies, and encounter endangered orangutans at the Semenggoh Wildlife Centre. Discover the unique traditions of Borneo’s diverse ethnic groups at the award-winning Sarawak Cultural Village.

Continue your adventure to Sabah’s Kota Kinabalu, with an excursion to the spectacular Kinabalu National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site home to Mount Kinabalu, exotic flora, and rich birdlife. Finally, unwind at the luxurious Shangri-La’s Rasa Ria Resort, where pristine beaches and lush landscapes provide the perfect setting for relaxation.

This well-balanced itinerary showcases Malaysia’s modern icons, cultural treasures, and natural wildlife—ideal for travelers seeking a mix of urban exploration, cultural immersion, and eco-adventure.
excerpt: This 10-day journey offers the perfect blend of city excitement, cultural discovery, and natural wonders. Begin in Malaysia’s capital, Kuala Lumpur, where modern skyscrapers stand beside colonial landmarks, vibrant temples, and bustling street life. Fly onwards to Kuching in Sarawak, the gateway to Borneo, where you’ll explore charming riverside streets, experience a sunset wildlife cruise in search of dolphins, monkeys, crocodiles, and fireflies, and encounter endangered orangutans at the Semenggoh Wildlife Centre. Discover the unique traditions of Borneo’s diverse ethnic groups at the award-winning Sarawak Cultural Village.

Kuala Lumpur Half-Day City Tour including Petronas Twin Towers Skybridge (or KL Tower), Independence Square, Jamek Mosque, River of Life, Chinatown, Sin Sze Ya Temple & Sri Mahamariamman Temple

Kuching City Tour featuring Fort Margherita (view), Tua Pek Kong Temple, colonial landmarks, Malay villages, India Street & Waterfront

Visit to Semenggoh Wildlife Centre – orangutan rehabilitation sanctuary
Entrance to the Sarawak Cultural Village – living museum with ethnic longhouses, cultural dances & crafts.
1 night stay at the Le Meridien Kota Kinabalu (Urban Room) in Kota Kinabalu (Sabah, Borneo – City Stay)
3 nights stay at the Shangri-La’s Rasa Ria Resort (Garden Wing in Kota Kinabalu (Sabah, Borneo – Beach Resort)
Excursion to Kinabalu National Park (UNESCO site) – guided rainforest walk, rare flora, Mountain Garden & views of Mount Kinabalu
Entrance fees to all places of visit as mentioned in the program
Boat transfers & boat tours on a joining/shared basis
Daily Breakfast throughout your trip
Lunch during Kinabalu National Park Excursion

highlights: Kuala Lumpur’s Petronas Twin Towers Skybridge and heritage landmarks (Independence Square, Sultan Abdul Samad Building, Jamek Mosque, Chinatown temples).
Kuching City Tour – colonial architecture, Tua Pek Kong Temple, India Street & vibrant waterfront.
Santubong Wildlife Cruise – chance to spot Irrawaddy dolphins, proboscis monkeys, crocodiles, and fireflies.
Semenggoh Wildlife Centre – see endangered orangutans in a natural rainforest setting.
Sarawak Cultural Village – award-winning “Living Museum” with longhouses, crafts, and cultural dances.a
Kinabalu National Park (UNESCO World Heritage Site) – rainforest trails, rare flora (orchids, pitcher plants, rhododendrons), and views of Mount Kinabalu.
Shangri-La’s Rasa Ria Resort Stay – luxury beachfront resort with leisure time to unwind.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Kuala Lumpur
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Kuala Lumpur International Airport, you will be greeted by a local representative and transferred to your downtown hotel. After check-in at the Concorde Hotel, the remainder of the day is free at leisure for you to relax and recover from your journey or begin exploring the vibrant city at your own pace. Overnight stay in Kuala Lumpur.

itinerary.title: Kuala Lumpur City Sightseeing
itinerary.description: After breakfast, set out for a guided half-day city tour of Kuala Lumpur. Begin with a visit to the iconic Petronas Twin Towers Skybridge (or KL Tower if closed) for sweeping views of the city skyline. Continue to Independence Square (Dataran Merdeka) to admire the Sultan Abdul Samad Building, Royal Selangor Club, and St. Mary’s Church. Stroll to the historic Jamek Mosque and see the River of Life project before heading into lively Chinatown. Here, visit the Sin Sze Ya Temple, the city’s oldest Taoist temple, followed by the richly decorated Sri Mahamariamman Temple, the oldest Hindu temple in Kuala Lumpur. After the tour, return to the hotel with the rest of the afternoon free at leisure. Overnight in Kuala Lumpur.

itinerary.title: Kuala Lumpur at Leisure
itinerary.description: Enjoy a leisurely breakfast at the hotel. The entire day is free for your own arrangements. You may wish to indulge in shopping at the world-class malls, explore street markets, or opt for an optional tour such as Batu Caves, Putrajaya, or a cultural evening. Overnight in Kuala Lumpur.

itinerary.title: Kuala Lumpur to Kuching
itinerary.description: This morning, check out after breakfast and transfer to the airport for your domestic flight to Kuching, the capital of Sarawak in Borneo. On arrival, meet your guide and transfer to the Sheraton Kuching Hotel. After check-in, the rest of the day is at leisure to relax or take a gentle walk along Kuching’s scenic waterfront. Overnight stay in Kuching.

itinerary.title: Kuching City & Santubong Wildlife Cruise
itinerary.description: After breakfast, embark on a guided sightseeing tour of Kuching. Drive past colonial landmarks such as Fort Margherita, the Square and Round Towers, the Colonial Courthouse, and the General Post Office. Visit the Tua Pek Kong Temple, the city’s oldest Chinese temple, and explore India Street, Malay villages, and traditional shop-houses. Take a walk along the lively waterfront and watch sampans ferrying locals across the Sarawak River. Later in the afternoon, head out for the Santubong Wildlife Cruise. Cruise through mangrove forests and estuaries in search of Irrawaddy dolphins, rare proboscis monkeys, crocodiles, and fireflies. Enjoy the magical sight of fireflies illuminating the mangroves at dusk before returning to Kuching. Overnight stay in Kuching.

itinerary.title: Semenggoh Orangutan Sanctuary & Sarawak Cultural Village
itinerary.description: Today begins with a visit to the Semenggoh Wildlife Centre, a renowned orangutan rehabilitation facility within the Semenggoh Nature Reserve. Here, you may witness orangutans emerging from the rainforest during feeding time, a truly unforgettable encounter with these endangered primates in their natural habitat. Continue to the Sarawak Cultural Village, an award-winning living museum nestled at the foothills of Mount Santubong. Explore authentic replicas of traditional homes representing seven major ethnic groups of Sarawak, interact with local artisans, and enjoy a vibrant multi-cultural dance performance. Return to Kuching for overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Kuching to Kota Kinabalu
itinerary.description: After breakfast, check out and transfer to the airport for your flight to Kota Kinabalu, the lively capital of Sabah. On arrival, transfer to Le Meridien Kota Kinabalu for check-in. The rest of the day is free to explore the city’s bustling markets, seafront promenade, or simply relax at the hotel. Overnight stay in Kota Kinabalu.

itinerary.title: Kinabalu National Park Excursion
itinerary.description: After breakfast, depart for a full-day excursion to Kinabalu National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Drive through picturesque mountain countryside to reach the park, home to Mount Kinabalu – Southeast Asia’s highest peak. Explore the lush rainforest with a guided walk along one of the park’s nature trails, where you’ll discover unique flora such as orchids, pitcher plants, and rhododendrons, as well as exotic birdlife. Visit the Mountain Garden, which showcases the park’s rare and endemic plants. Later, transfer to the luxurious Shangri-La’s Rasa Ria Resort, where you’ll check in and enjoy leisure time by the beach. Overnight stay at the resort.

itinerary.title: Kota Kinabalu at Leisure
itinerary.description: Today is free to relax and enjoy the facilities of Shangri-La’s Rasa Ria Resort. You may lounge by the beach, indulge in spa treatments, or join optional resort activities such as water sports, horse riding, or jungle trekking. Overnight stay at the resort in Kota Kinabalu.

itinerary.title: Departure from Kota Kinabalu
itinerary.description: After breakfast, enjoy leisure time until check-out. Later, transfer to Kota Kinabalu International Airport for your onward flight.

accommodations.name: Concorde Hotel Kuala Lumpur
accommodations.description:
Concorde Hotel Kuala Lumpur

 Concorde Hotel Kuala Lumpur is a 4-star hotel, ideally located in the heart of the vibrant Golden Triangle of Kuala Lumpur. Guests can enjoy the convenience of being just a short walk from Bukit Nanas Monorail Station and Dang Wangi LRT Station, as well as major attractions like MaTiC, Suria KLCC Shopping Centre, Petronas Twin Towers, KL Convention Centre, and KL Tower. Nestled between Bukit Bintang and KLCC, the hotel offers an excellent location for both business and leisure. With 15 well-equipped meeting rooms, Concorde is perfect for hosting seminars and events for up to 750 people. For dining, the hotel offers a variety of F&B outlets: Melting Pot Cafe serves Asian and Continental buffet and a la carte, while Xroads Xpress offers an all-day express deli a

accommodations.name: Sheraton Kuching Hotel
accommodations.description:
Sheraton Kuching Hotel

 Sheraton Kuching captivates guests with its iconic curved architecture and central location near the Sarawak River and Kuching Waterfront Esplanade. Located within Kuching's Golden Triangle, just a 15-minute drive from Kuching International Airport with nearby attractions including Sarawak River Cruises, and the striking Darul Hana Bridge. Gather with friends and colleagues in our bright, open lobby before a workout in Sheraton Fitness or infinity pool. Savor local and international specialties at Nomadic or host a private dinner at Gu Jin. If you are planning a wedding or conference at our 5-star hotel in Kuching, Sarawak, reserve our pillarless Grand Ballroom which features a built-in lighting system, LED screen, and space for 700 guests. Relax in a contemporary

accommodations.name: Le Meridien Kota Kinabalu
accommodations.description:
Le Meridien Kota Kinabalu

 Le Meridien Kota Kinabalu is a contemporary 5 star hotel, strategically located at the heart and city center of downtown Kota Kinabalu across the fresh and handicraft markets and Kota Kinabalu waterfront. Overlooking the sea, Le Meridien Kota Kinabalu features newly renovated rooms, leisure outdoor pool, a sunset rooftop bar and a pool side bar and cafe. Convenient within easy walking distance of other local attractions like Gaya Street in less than 10 minutes walk, retail and entertainment facilities in less than 20 minutes walk like Centrepoint, Suria and Imago Shopping Malls. Enjoy access to local art gallery, golf courses and other island excursions.

accommodations.name: Shangri-La Rasa Ria, Kota Kinabalu
accommodations.description:
Shangri-La Rasa Ria, Kota Kinabalu

 Escape from the city and journey into nature; where sea meets rainforest and a warm welcome awaits. Discover a hidden haven with over 400 acres of untouched natural beauty, from the wide sandy beach to the lush tropical forest, Shangri-La Rasa Ria, Kota Kinabalu is a spectacular sanctuary that makes each moment of your holiday matter. Relish imaginative cuisine showcasing the best of Borneo as well as international favorites from around the globe that will satisfy all palettes. Choose from over 60 immersive activities, from magical sunrise walks to bird watching and watersports, and keep the whole family excited, engaged and entertained. Be immersed into this enchanting paradise, bringing your Borneo dreams to life.

title: Spend 10 nights touring all of Nepal’s hot spots
description:
Located in Southern Asia, Nepal is a touristy bubble bursting with spectacular hiking routes and eight of the world’s highest peaks, including Everest. Lush, green mountains and sparkling lakes make for a concoction that’s irresistible to tourists and visitors, who find themselves drawn in by its cultural charm and religious heritage. Temples are ten a penny and the lifestyle is so unique and rich with flavor and age-old customs that it’s hard not to fall in love with the Nepalese way of life.  

Beginning in Kathmandu, Nepal’s capital, your trip will lead you deep into the very heart of Nepali culture. As you mingle with the monkeys at the Monkey Temple, one of the most sacred Buddhist sites, and pay a visit to the Boudha Stupa, one of the largest stupas in the world, you’ll come to understand why this country has such a rich history. You’ll step foot into the Hindu temple of Pashupatinath, the Lord of Animals, and then wander Durbar Square’s palaces and temples, including the Kasthamandap, also known as the “Wooden House.” The square, sprinkled with merchants and devotees during the early hours, is Nepal’s most popular UNESCO World Heritage Site (and rightly so!).

Then, swap the urban vibes of Kathmandu with Chitwan. you will visit Chitwan National Park, whose 932 square kilometers are a haven for Bengal tigers, leopards, crocodiles, monkeys and more. Keep your eyes peeled for these sensational beasts, as well as the endangered one horn rhinos, which roam the plump grassland. In addition to marveling at the colorful flora and fauna, you can also go on elephant rides, jungle canoeing, nature walks and tiger and rhino viewing. Here you will spend 2 nights at the Jungle Villa, a leafy location right in the thick of all the glorious flora and fauna.

When you finally tire of Chitwan National Park charm, you’ll head to Pokhara, the second largest city in Nepal. As the starting point for many of the treks to the Annapurna Mountain Range, Pokhara is a lively hub of culture vultures and hiking enthusiasts. It is also home to the Phewa Lake, one of Nepal’s most idyllic spots, perfect for rowing across and gazing at the mesmerizing views of the towering peaks and dense forests which surround it.

After Pokhara, you’ll head to Bandipur, another hill-top town offering an abundance of hiking routes and fabulous views from elevated viewpoints. The quaint Old Inn in Bandipur will also be your home for a night, before hitting the road to Nagarkot where you’ll settle for 2 nights.

Nagarkot, a town located 32 km from Kathmandu which offers maybe the best views of the Himalayas in all of the Bhaktapur District, as well as a panoramic view of the Kathmandu Valley. At sunrise, watch as Mount Everest and other peaks of the Himalayan range come into view, startlingly crystal clear, and then head off for a hike into the heart of all of this incredibly beautiful nature.

highlights: Head out and explore the wonderful Kathmandu Valley.

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL IN KATHMANDU
itinerary.description:
The adventure begins today as you fly to Kathmandu. One of our friendly representatives will greet you at the airport and transfer you to your hotel. Upon arrival, you’ll be formally introduced to your guide for the next few days, the person who will lead all of your activities and ensure you are all well-informed and having a great time. It’s a good idea to pay attention to the safety aspects of the briefing too, and then you’ll have the opportunity to ask any questions and clear up any doubts you may have. You’ll stay overnight at the hotel.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN KATHMANDU

itinerary.title: KATHMANDU
itinerary.description:
Kathmandu City:

Get ready to visit the temple of the Living Goddess, one of
the most important in Nepal. The Kumari Goddess dwells on her balcony
regularly, acknowledging the greetings of the devotees, after having moved into
the temple with her family. She leaves the temple only to attend a few
festivals each year and her reign lasts only until puberty, when she becomes a
regular girl again. From Durbar Square you can see the Hanuman Dhoka Palace,
the ancient residence of the Nepalese Royalty.

Swayambhunath:

Associated with the birth of the Kathmandu Valley
civilization, Swayambhunath is a supremely important site of culture and
religion in Nepal. Located on a serene hilltop in the southwest, this enormous
dome around 2000 years old is surrounded by other temples and religious sites
for both Hindus and Buddhists, displaying the peaceful co-existence between
people’s differing religious beliefs in Nepal. Swayambhunath is also known as
the “Monkey Temple,” since it is surrounded by troops of monkeys, as well as
indigenous plant species and forest. Its high elevation also makes it a perfect
viewpoint, so make sure you keep a look-out for the Himalayas sitting idly in
the east, as well as the Kathmandu Valley.

Patan or Yala:

One of the three main cities of the Kathmandu Valley is
Patan (otherwise known as Yala), located on the southern banks of the Bagmati
River. Famous for its temples, palace courtyards and public baths, Patan was
established by the Kirat Dynasty and was supposedly the first settlement in the
Valley. Its abundance of temples and monuments (there are more than 1000)
dedicated to a mixture of Buddhist and Hindu gods, are breathtakingly
elaborate. Definitely pay a visit to Patan Durbar Square, as it is one of the
seven Monument Zones which make up the UNESCO World Heritage Sites of Kathmandu
Valley. You’ll stay overnight at the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN KATHMANDU

itinerary.title: KATHMANDU–CHITWAN
itinerary.description:
Leave the beauty of Kathmandu behind and venture to Chitwan. Upon arrival, you’ll check into a wildlife lodge and be briefed about your stay in Chitwan. 

You will enjoy a guided village walk and a walk on the banks of the Rapti River to watch the charming sunset views in the sub-tropical jungle. 

In the evening, Dinner with cultural program by ethnic tribal people with their stick dance and tribal drums.

OVERNIGHT IN CHITWAN ON FULL BOARD BASIS

itinerary.title: ESCAPING TO THE JUNGLE OF CHITWAN
itinerary.description:
Today will be spent entirely within the park. You will also visit to the National Park Visitor's centre where you can learn more about the history of the National Park and about wildlife.

You will have a chance to see four different kinds of deer, rhinoceros, wild boar, monkey, leopard, sloth bear, the Royal Bengal Tiger (If you are lucky) and will encounter many other smaller mammals that have made Chitwan their home.

You will also have an excellent opportunity for Bird Watching and for seeing the 2 rare species of crocodiles; the Marsh Mugger and the fish eating Gharial. Experience Jungle Walk & on the way back one can enjoy the elephant bathing.

In the evening, enjoy Tharu cultural dance presentation (which you will also participate in) by the local villagers.

OVERNIGHT IN CHITWAN ON FULL BOARD BASIS

itinerary.title: CHITWAN—POKHARA
itinerary.description:
Enjoy a delicious breakfast. After, you’ll proceed to Pokhara. 

Pokhara does its best to leave all its visitors spellbound by the end of their trip. There are mountains, rivers and lakes, including the peaceful Phewa Lake and grandiose Machha puchhre which make for an impressive landscape. 9km northwest of Pokhara, you’ll find Sarangkot, one of Nepal’s primary tourist destinations. Its high elevation (1592m) means it is blessed with priceless views and terrific scenery, including a fabulous panorama of the entire Annapurna mountain range and the rustic settlements sitting peacefully below. At a 700m drop from the top of Sarangkot, you’ll find the Pokhara Valley and Phewa Lake. After taking an evening walk around the lakeside, you’ll stay overnight at the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN POKHARA

itinerary.title: POKHARA SIGHTS
itinerary.description:
Davis Falls:

Davis Falls is a rather spectacular waterfall lying 2km from the centre of Pokhara city. The water gushes directly into a deep, narrow canal which seemingly has no end, and which supposedly took the life of a tourist called David who fell in, and thus was named The Davis Waterfall (other names include Davy’s Fall, David’s Fall or Davis’ Fall).

Gupteswor Mahadev Cave:

Step foot into one of the longest caves in Nepal. On the way to the cave, you’ll marvel at an array of shining stones, as well as the temple stone which resembles a snake’s head and tortoise. This is said to represent the image of the Lord Shiva. Next, you’ll see more beautiful, sparkling gems and a plethora of different rocks shaped like beehives.

Bindebashini Temple:

The Bindebashini Temple is located in the area of the Bazaar and is one of Pokhara’s oldest temples. Legend has it that when Pokhara was part of the Kaski Kingdom, Goddess Durga, the temple’s main deity, was brought from India. On a clear day, you may even be able to catch sight of the Himalayas from this temple, before heading to the old bazaar.

The Fewa (or Phewa) Lake:

The Fewa Lake is the second largest lake in Nepal and is shrouded by mountains whose snowy caps reflect against the glistening ripples of the water. You’ll find an abundance of hotels, tour companies and places to unwind in front of a delicious meal while lapping up the unparalleled landscape. You’ll also get to head out on a boat ride for an hour and visit the Barahi Temple, situated in the middle of the lake. As your boat ride comes to an end, you’ll be dropped back at the Pokhara lake side and stay overnight at the hotel. 

OVERNIGHT IN POKHARA

itinerary.title: POKHARA – BANDIPUR
itinerary.description:
Bandipur offers plenty of hiking routes, elevated viewpoints and panoramic views of the Annapurna Mountain Range when the clouds have subsided. It was first established as an agricultural village by the Magar Kingdom, but was later colonized in the 18th century and began to achieve success as a centre for trading and textiles. Its new residents, the Newars of Bhaktapur, favored the architectural styles of the Newari heartlands in the Kathmandu Valley. The main square is surrounded by temples and shrines, including Khadga Devi Mandir, which supposedly contains a sword given to an early Magar King by Lord Shiva.

OVERNIGHT IN BANDIPUR

itinerary.title: BANDIPUR--–KATHMANDU---NAGARKOT
itinerary.description:
After an early morning breakfast make your way to Nagarkot via Kathmandu.  Nagarkot is situated 32 kilometers east of Kathmandu at an altitude of 2.175 meters. When you reach Nagarkot you’ll be blown away by a panoramic view of the major peaks including Mount Everest. It is an outstanding hilltop that offers great views of Himalayas, breathtaking mountain scenery and the most impressive views of sunrise and sunset.

OVERNIGHT IN NAGARKOT

itinerary.title: NAGARKOT—AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Today will be at leisure and you can enjoy the green hills, clean and fresh environment and the unobstructed views of Langtang range which are the main features of Nagarkot.

In the evening enjoy free farewell dinner at the hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN NAGARKOT

itinerary.title: DRIVE BACK TO KATHMANDU
itinerary.description:
Breakfast will be followed by a drive back to Kathmandu. Upon arrival, you’ll be taken to your hotel. In the evening you will visit Pashupatinath Temple & Boudhanath Stupa and then stay overnight OR extend your stay by choosing any of the optional hiking and trekking trip:

1)  CHANGU TO TELKOT

After breakfast we start leisurely from Nagarkot around 8 AM or 9 AM and drive for around an hour to the trail head  Changunarayen. Here you will visit the historical Changunarayen Temple built on 5th century. It is a lovely old temple with important historical writings on copper plates and stones here and there , and speaks well about Nepal's civilization that time. The temple is also a world heritage site declared by UNESCO.  After visiting temple  you will be taken on the relaxed walking trail to the village of Tilkot. Will stop on the way for lunch. The two hour walk takes you through forests and villages so that you can experience life in the more rural areas of Nepal. The views down to the Valley can be stunning, as paddy fields and brick-making chimneys mingle before snowy peaks of the distant Himalayas on clear days.

OVERNIGHT IN KATHMANDU

2)  PANAUTI - NAMOBUDDHA - DHULIKHEL  ( 1 NIGHT STAY)

DAY 1

After breakfast drive to Panauti which is 33 kms away (approx 1 hr) from Nagarkot. From there, hikers start the walk by following the dirt road along the river and passing the town’s Hindu temple. For the next two hours, the way leads more or less evenly through small villages and rice fields. Depending on the season, you can see the locals planting, harvesting rice and other crops and get a great insight into the village life in Nepal. Tea shops along the way invite to rest and have a refreshing cup of Chia ( Tea).

On the way experience breathtaking views of the monastery and the Stupa in the distance. And also the hundreds of thousands of prayer flags attached to a pole at the highest point are unmistakable.

After two hou

itinerary.title: DEPARTURE
itinerary.description:
It’s time to go! After breakfast you’ll make your way to the airport, ready to catch your flight back home. We hope you’ve enjoyed your holiday.

END OF TRIP

accommodations.name: Hotel Shambala , Kathmandu
accommodations.description: Hotel Shambala is a contemporary Tibetan themed boutique hotel in Kathmandu, Nepal that offers unparalleled comfort and luxury with the best of Tibetan and Nepalese hospitality. Each of the rooms have been beautifully designed and luxuriously furnished to make each stay a comfortable one. All rooms come equipped with en-suite bathrooms with complimentary herbal toiletries, individually controlled air-conditioners, in-room safe, free Wi-Fi, LED TV with cable channels and tea/coffee making facilitiesThe in house restaurant, Erma cafe offers a range of Continental and Asian cuisines prepared by our experienced chefs. The cafe offers both indoor and outdoor dining options by the garden and waterfall bar. For light snacks and beverages in an exclusive environment, guests are welcome to enjoy the serene ambiance of the roof top lounge- Cloud 9. Signature cocktails and alcoholic beverages are available through the bar.Health and wellness facilities include a beautiful roof-top swimming pool and a full spa offering range of body therapy sessions, massages and facials. Conference facilities and other business services are also available upon request.

accommodations.name: Jungle Villa Resort, Chitwan
accommodations.description:
Jungle Villa Resort, Chitwan

 Bestowed with nature's finest creations, the Jungle Villa Resort is one of the most popular luxury wildlife resorts in Nepal. This resort is located in the northern fringe of bio-diversity-rich Chitwan National Park, which is home to many endangered flora and fauna.

All rooms are air-conditioned with attached lavatories having facility of 24/7 running hot & cold water. Guests can overlook scenic view of Rapti river and adjoining CPN from the private balconies attached with their rooms. It is ideal place to catch a glimpse of fascinating wildlife including one-horned rhinoceros, Royal Bengal Tiger, deer as well as various species of birds and aquatic animals. 

Spacious and elegantly designed restaurants offer an opportunity to enjoy breathtaking views of Rap

accommodations.name: Atithi Resort and SPA, Pokhara
accommodations.description:
Atithi Resort and SPA, Pokhara

 Located just a 5-minute walk from Fewa Lake, Atithi Resort and SPA is a unique resort located at the heart of the city of lakes, Pokhara.

There are 51 luxury rooms at Atithi Resort & Spa, each offering all the essentials to ensure a comfortable stay. The resort also has several connecting rooms to accommodate families.

Atithi Resort & Spa's on-site restaurant offers Indian-inspired meals, which can be served on the al fresco terrace. In the evening, guests are welcome to relax in the comfortable lounge bar. A wide selection of dining options are also found in the surrounding area.

Guests can experience the bet quality services of different kind of spa and massage treatments at camellia spa & wellness center. The swimming pool is one of the most modern am

accommodations.name: The Old Inn, Bandipur
accommodations.description:
The Old Inn, Bandipur

 The Old Inn has been welcoming visitors to Bandipur since 2000. The Old Inn is a local Nepalese guesthouse situated in the charming town of Bandipur, mid-way between Kathmandu and Pokhara. It is perfect for those guests who want to escape the usual tourist trail of Kathmandu, Pokhara or Chitwan and is great to stop off when driving to the Annapurna Range from Kathmandu. It is located right in the main square and is a restored town-house in a town where time have stood still and is now a traffic-free zone.

The Old Inn has a New Wing with en-suite facilities. Guestrooms are fitted with all the amenities you need for a good night's sleep. Rooms are decorated with traditional art and use local, handmade bed covers.

To enhance guests' stay, the hotel offers recreationa

accommodations.name: Hotel Country Villa, Nagarkot
accommodations.description:
Hotel Country Villa, Nagarkot

 Hotel Country Villa – a beautiful paradise on earth is your ideal address in Nagarkot. It boasts of wonderful scenic canvas of Himalayas stretching form Mt. Kanchanjunga in the far east to the Annapurna massif in the far west and the revering sunrise that serenades the entire valley.

Hotel Country Villa oozes luxury in every sense and the lobby epitomizes the ultimate class. The lobby offers an out of the world panoramic landscape from all points through its floor to wall ceiling windows. Add into it the minute detailing for comfort, the visitors can just relax and savor the scenic splendor.

Hotel Country Villa offers elegant rooms that will make you feel comfortable and relaxed. The elegant ambience blended with all the modern amenities will make you stay

Early Check in / Late Check out

title: Royal Waterways
description:
Experience an extraordinary river cruise along the three most important waterways in Europe - the Rhine, Main and the Danube. Your journey begins in picturesque Amsterdam, famous for its canals, art museums and lively atmosphere. From there, your route takes you through fascinating landscapes and along important cultural centers.

In Koblenz, discover the Deutsches Eck, where the Rhine and Moselle rivers meet, and be amazed by the historic old town and Ehrenbreitstein Fortress. In Mainz, magnificent half-timbered houses, the imposing cathedral and the Gutenberg Museum await you. Continue to Würzburg, where the prince-bishop's residence, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the baroque old town will amaze you.

In Nuremberg, you will experience medieval flair, characterized by the imperial castle and traditional craftsmanship. Regensburg impresses with its well-preserved old town and the Stone Bridge, while Passau, the city of three rivers, enchants with its Italian charm and magnificent churches.

The journey continues along the majestic Danube to Vienna, the capital of music, where imperial elegance, coffee house culture and world-famous sights such as Schönbrunn Palace and St. Stephen's Cathedral await you. Bratislava, with its mixture of baroque heritage and modern lifestyle, offers impressive contrasts.

The crowning glory is the fascinating Budapest, where the parliament, the Chain Bridge and the spa culture provide unique insights into Hungarian history and the present. Enjoy an unforgettable journey, the culture, history and natural beauty in a wonderful way - a highlight of every river cruise through Europe.
excerpt: Experience an extraordinary river cruise along the three most important waterways in Europe - the Rhine, Main and the Danube. Your journey begins in picturesque Amsterdam, famous for its canals, art museums and lively atmosphere. From there, your route takes you through fascinating landscapes and along important cultural centers.

Explore the cathedral and Gutenberg heritage of Mainz
Visit Würzburg and its UNESCO-listed Residence

Wander the preserved old town of Regensburg and admire the Stone Bridge
Discover Passau, the “City of Three Rivers”
Enjoy imperial grandeur in Vienna, home to Schönbrunn Palace and St. Stephen’s Cathedral
Explore the baroque-meets-modern charm of Bratislava
Conclude in spectacular Budapest, with Parliament, Chain Bridge and thermal spa culture
Scenic cruising along the legendary Rhine, Main and Danube rivers
A perfect blend of culture, UNESCO heritage, architecture and natural beauty

accommodations.name: VIVA ENJOY
accommodations.description: Düsseldorf-based river tour operator VIVA Cruises is continuing to grow: after the VIVA ONE (2022) and the VIVA TWO (2023), the third newbuild VIVA ENJOY will join the fleet next year. The ship was previously announced with the working title VIVA THREE, but in keeping with the company motto "Enjoy the Moment", the final name has now been decided. The maiden voyage of the VIVA ENJOY will start on September 22, 2024 from Frankfurt in the direction of Vienna. The tour operator is responding to the increasing demand for river cruises by expanding its fleet. "We have grown strongly over the last five years, as has our customer base and the demand from international guests, especially on the Danube. The third new building in combination with our remaining fleet gives us new scope again, not only with regard to our routings, but also with regard to our international partners and guests," explains Andrea Kruse, Managing Director of VIVA Cruises. The VIVA ENJOY will be the sister ship of the VIVA TWO and therefore identical in design. The ship has a total of 95 outside cabins with space for a maximum of 190 passengers. With a total of three restaurants, an open-plan lounge, a spacious sun deck and a fitness and wellness area, the ship is a floating premium hotel with a new, modern look and a pure feel-good ambience.

title: 13 Days Classic Peru Holiday
description:
Explore Peru’s enchanting heritage and step back in time to a place with vast Inca ruins, archaeological gems and postcard landscapes. Tour around Lima, Peru’s colourful capital that’s brimming with World Heritage Sites, before visiting Arequipa, famous for its volcanoes and Spanish cuisine. 

You’ll rub shoulders with indigenous communities, visit the majestic Lake Titicaca and travel to the Cusco - a World Heritage Site and haven for striking architecture. Here you’ll take a trek into history with a visit to Machu Picchu, an awe-inspiring, almost-forgotten city that dates back to the 15th century.
excerpt: Explore Peru’s enchanting heritage and step back in time to a place with vast Inca ruins, archaeological gems and postcard landscapes. Tour around Lima, Peru’s colourful capital that’s brimming with World Heritage Sites, before visiting Arequipa, famous for its volcanoes and Spanish cuisine.
whats_included: Return flights from the UK
Meals as indicated in the itinerary, 12 breakfasts and 7 Lunches
2 nts at Tierra Viva in Lima
1 nt at Tierra Viva Arequipa in Arequipa

2 nts at Hacienda Puno in Puno
3 nts at Tierra Viva Cusco in Cusco
1 nt at AVA Spots in Sacred Valley
1 nt at Hatun Inti Classic Standard in Aguas Calientes
Guided city tours: Lima, Arequipa, Cusco
Colca Valley & Canyon experience (Chivay, Condor Cross Lookout, Uyo Uyo site, local villages)
Lake Titicaca tour: Uros & Taquile Islands (speedboat)
Cultural & archaeological visits: Pucara Museum, La Raya, Racchi, Andahuaylillas
Sacred Valley tours: Chinchero, Living Museum of Yucay, Ollantaytambo
Machu Picchu tour: Roundtrip train, bus transfers, entrance, guided visit
Shared transfer from/to bus station in Puno / Cusco
Shared transfer to/from the train station in Ollantaytambo

Roundtrip train to Machu Picchu
Tours / entrance fees as indicated in the program
