itinerary.title: ARRIVAL SIGIRIYA
itinerary.description:
Arrive in Sri Lanka,  Bandaranayke International Airport. There you will meet one of our representative and will be transfered to Sigiriya. 

COMPLIMENTARY MEAL ON ARRIVAL & OVERNIGHT IN SIGIRIYA

itinerary.title: SIGIRIYA / ROCK / VILLAGE HOUSE / SIGIRIYA
itinerary.description:
After breakfast leave for Rock Fortress which is is one of the most valuable historical monuments of Sri Lanka. Referred by locals as the Eighth Wonder of the World this ancient palace and fortress complex has significant archaeological importance and attracts thousands of tourists every year. It is probably the most visited tourist destination of Sri Lanka. Sigiriya rock plateau, formed from magma of an extinct volcano, is 200 meters higher than the surrounding jungles.

Later do you want to try something traditional and completely different? A delightful evening bullock cart ride is just the ticket, which will take you to some of the nicest places on the island. It’s pretty unconventional and definitely an adventure. Riding in the shade of the forests, you will see the country’s historic sites and some of the lovely scenery as well. What makes this ride exceptional is that you will visit a Sri Lankan village and enjoy a cookery demonstration and home-cooked meal from the local women and then top it off with a canoe ride across a beautiful lake! This tour will make your holiday that much more unforgettable! 

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN SIGIRIYA

itinerary.title: SIGIRIYA / DAMBULLA / MINNERIYA / SIGIRIYA
itinerary.description:
After breakfast proceed to visit the Dambulla Rock Temple. Dambulla is a popular destination in the cultural triangle of Sri Lanka. With a history that dates back to the 1st century, Dambulla has historic statues and paintings in its caves that has made it a popular destination for archeological studies. This temple was built by King Walagambahu during the 1st Century B.C. Dambulla is a UNESCO world heritage site. There are more than 80 documented caves in the surrounding and the paintings are done on the ceiling. There are a total of 153 Buddha statues 3 statues of Sri Lankan kings and 4 statues of god and goddess.

Thereafter proceed on a jeep safari to visit the Minneriya National Park. The park is famous for being the best place in Sri Lanka to get close to free-ranging elephants. Bordering the Minneriya tank and the Giritale tank, the 7,529 hectare park attracts hundreds of elephants during the dry season, as surrounding water source dries up. Watching them travel in herds of 10 to 20, it is impossible not to be impressed with the sheer bulk of a fully grown adult, but equally spectacular is seeing how they interact. There is also a host of water birds, both large and small, so keep your eyes open for delicately hued storks, slender and elegant grey herons, and great white pelicans.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN SIGIRIYA

itinerary.title: SIGIRIYA / MATALE/ KANDY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast you will leave for Kandy. Just 25 km from the hill capital of Kandy, the Matale spice gardens are among the best on the island where you will be introduced to different types of spices. Here you will also be shown how some of these spices are grown and processed. Visitors may buy spices, traditional remedies and cosmetics, taste a herbal brew and in some venues.

In Matale, you will also enjoy the full flavors of Sri Lankan spices over lunch or an Ayurveda massage based on natural products from the garden.

Evening at leisure.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN KANDY

itinerary.title: KANDY (SIGHTSEEING)
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, proceed on a sightseeing tour of Kandy - which was the last capital of the Sri Lankan kings, and is a World Heritage Site. The name Kandy conjures visions of splendor and magnificence. Many of the legends, traditions and folklore are still lovingly kept alive. Drive around the Kandy Lake built by the last Sinhala King Sri Wickrama Rajasinghe in 1798. Visit the Kandy town and Bazaar, the arts & crafts centre and a gem museum and a lapidary.  

In the evening visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic and thereafter witness a cultural show featuring traditional Sri Lankan dancing.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN KANDY

itinerary.title: KANDY TO NUWARA ELIYA ( TRAIN RIDE )
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, proceed to the Kandy Train Station to board the Train leaving for Nanu Oya – It is an unforgettable train ride to Nuwara Eliya. Sit back in the comfort where from you can catch jaw-dropping views as the train powers up through the tea plantations, into the clouds and then weaves in and out of the many mountain tunnels. The railway in Sri Lanka was first introduced by the British Colonial government in 1864, to transport tea from places like Nuwara Eliya to the capital, Colombo.

Nuwara Eliya which is at an elevation of 1890 meters above the sea level is the highest city in Sri Lanka and it lies beneath the tallest peak of the island. Nuwara Eliya (City of Lights) is a resort city blessed with salubrious climate, breathtaking views of valleys, meadows, mountains and greenery. Nuwara Eliya, also known as ‘Little’ England’, was the favorite hill station of the British who tried to create the resort into a typical English Village.  

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN NUWARA ELIYA

itinerary.title: NUWARA ELIYA – KOGGALA
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, travel through the twisting roads. It is then onto the beautiful village of Kitulgala; with forest-covered hills plunging down to the wild waters of the Kelani Ganga, astounding waterfalls and towering trees, the scenery is particularly dramatic. Kitulgala’s original claim to fame is as the location for David Lean’s 1957 film “Bridge on the River Kwai”. Nowadays, Kitulgala is best known as the site of Sri Lanka’s best white water rafting with grade two and grade three rapids some 5km upriver from the village.

After lunch we then head for the paradise beaches of Koggala. En route we visit the town’s Turtle Hatchery. Koggala is one of Sri Lanka’s most import ant breeding spots for the endangered Sea Turtles.

The sandy beaches serve as sanctuary for several types of marine turtles including Loggerheads and the Leatherback Turtle. The small turtle hatchery first opened in 1996, in an effort to promote responsible tourism and conservation. The hatchery buys turtle eggs off fishermen, in an attempt to discourage them from poaching and selling the eggs.

You will be educated on the importance of protecting turtle life, via the hatchery’s facilities that care for new born turtles until they are ready to be released back into the ocean, an experience that you may even have the opportunity to do yourself first-hand! Continue to hotel.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN KOGGALA

itinerary.title: KOGGALA
itinerary.description:
A relatively unknown when it comes to beach escapes, Koggala is authentic, captivating and entirely without guile and location-wise it doesn’t get much better than this. The small coastal town stretches across the south coast of Sri Lanka and has one of the island nation’s longest beaches, where you can explore the reef straight from the beach.

A stay here takes your Sri Lanka adventure to an altogether different level, bringing together the exceptional and beautiful in one special paradise.

After breakfast, enjoy a relaxing day at leisure. Your beautiful hotel is set beside the sublime sands and coconut palms of Koggala Beach.Take a walk along the coast to look out for turtles wading in the shallows.

Retire to the serenity of the spa for a soothing treatment or lounge beside the outdoor pool and be pampered. Nearby to the historic city of Galle, its famous fort is a site you will not once to miss and between November and April feel free to join a whale watching excursion at nearby Mirissa.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN KOGGALA

itinerary.title: KOGGALA
itinerary.description:
End your holiday in this very beautiful and relaxing part of Sri Lanka by reclining on the golden sandy beaches or sipping cocktails overlooking the sunset.

If you keep your eyes open you might even see the Green Flash! A sudden short pinprick of green that happens as the sun sinks towards the horizon.

DINNER & OVERNIGHT IN KOGGALA

itinerary.title: KOGGALA – COLOMBO--UK
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, check out from the hotel. If there’s time discover the meaning behind Sri Lanka’s affectionate nick name “Ratna-Dweepa” which means Gem Island.

At the local bazaar you will find the world’s finest sapphires, topazes and many other beautiful jewels. It is time to head home via private transfer to the international airport to connect with the departure flight.

END OF TOUR

accommodations.description:

 Situated in Koggala Beach, The Beach Cabanas is the perfect place to experience Unawatuna and its surroundings. Get a closer insight into Sri Lanka with our below optional tours!

Discover the Jewels of Polonnaruwa  

Roam the grand temples built by the kings of the 11th to 13th centuries. Savour the beauty of the Royal Palace and at Gal Vihare and the Temples of Shiva gaze at the exquisite paintings and superb statues. 

Mountains & Lakes of Horton Plains 

Lying in the lap of the central highlands, the beauty of the Horton Plains is unsurpassable – dotted with lakes of ice-cold waters, montane forests, rocky outcrops and tumbling waterfalls. The experience is enhanced with a visit to “World’s End” for mind-blowing views.

Wilderness of Yala National Park 

This tour is a fantastic opportunity to explore the country’s oldest and most popular nature reserve – Yala National Park, a prime place for spotting leopards. Feel the thrill of being up close to some of nature’s most stunning and elusive creatures - home to the sloth bear, elephants and prolific bird life.                                                                                          

Cruise the Beautiful Madhu River 

Immensely beautiful and deeply vital to those who call its banks home, the Madhu River is a must-see destination. Take this rare opportunity to sail pass dozens of islets and get a closer insight into local life.

title: 10 Days Getaway To The Exotic Islands of Thailand
description:
First, you’ll touch down on Phuket Island, nestled on Thailand’s Indian Ocean coastline and boasting world-class beaches and an exotic climate which will have you donning swimwear every chance you get. A hotspot for travellers and tourists, Phuket’s beauty not only lies in its crystal-clear waters but also in its colourful history and plump, karst islands sprouting from the sea.

With waters as transparently turquoise as these, swimming, sunbathing and snorkelling are firm features on most visitors’ to-do lists when they come to Phuket. Lounge about on radiant beaches, try your hand at surfing or get up close and personal with mesmerizing marine life in the shallow reefs of Patong, Karon and Kata beaches. Phuket is also one of the most popular spa destinations in the world, so get ready to wave goodbye to those aches and pains!

One of your evenings in Phuket will be spent watching Siam Niramit, a spectacular performance of Thailand’s artistic and cultural heritage. With more than 100 performers dressed in lavish costumes against a backdrop of fabulous set designs and special effects, this promises to be a night to remember.

Next, you’ll spend 2 nights in Ko Phi Phi. This is the name given to a small archipelago of six islands in Southern Thailand whose beauty and idyllic scenery are world-renowned. Phi Phi Bayview Resort is a paradise holiday destination on a remote tropical island where the turquoise waters and powder soft white sands are just steps away from the charming accommodation. Situated on a gently sloping hillside of lush jungle overlooking the sparkling shores of Laem Hin Beach, the Phi Phi Island resort is a tranquil hideaway for nature lovers and romantics.

With a plethora of beaches to choose from, you’ll leave Phi Phi feeling well-rested and spoiled. Yao Beach boasts stunning views with plenty of scuba-worthy coral reefs and Laem Tong, located in the north of the island, gives visitors a chance to unwind on a quaint beach against a backdrop of remarkable vistas.

After spending some time in Phi Phi Don, the coastal province of Krabi, bursting with dazzling beaches and intricate caves and waterfalls, awaits you. Settle in at the Holiday Inn Express on Ao Nang Beach for 4 nights, partake in a spot of beach-hopping and soak up the sun alongside turquoise waters. Start at Fossil Shell Beach, one of only 3 fossil shell beaches on the planet, and then proceed to the bustling Rai Leh Beach, the heart of Thailand’s rock climbing scene.

 For thrill-seekers, venture off to do some hiking at Khao Ngon Nak, the highest mountain in the Krabi region, or whizz off on a 1-day rip-roaring safari by speedboat to Hin Daeng and Hin Muang if gazing at whale sharks and manta rays floats your boat. Plus, with a range of boat trips on offer which will take you to all of Krabi’s secluded beaches and mini islets, the choices are endless!

itinerary.title: ARRIVE IN PHUKET
itinerary.description:
Upon arrival at Phuket International Airport, one of our trusty representatives will transfer you to your hotel. The rest of the day can be spent however you please, whether it be spoiling yourself with a traditional Thai massage, sampling the local cuisine or watching the sunset at the hotel’s stylish beach club.

OVERNIGHT IN PHUKET

itinerary.title: PHUKET
itinerary.description:
Spend your day soaking up the sun at the pool area or taking a refreshing dip in the sea. Phuket is both colourful and cosmopolitan, so make sure you pay a visit to its glittering temples and colonial mansions too! Your rep will be able to advise you on activities and make the necessary arrangements.

OVERNIGHT IN PHUKET

itinerary.title: PHUKET
itinerary.description:
After breakfast day will be at leisure. Today For one night, we’ll treat you to an evening of Siam Niramit, a world-class performance of Thailand’s artistic and cultural heritage with over 100 performers sporting lavish costumes against a backdrop of stunning set designs. Enhanced special effects and cutting-edge technology are responsible for such an exciting show. The show starts at 20.30 but gates open at 17.30 for access to the restaurant and to give visitors a chance to visit the other on-site attractions. 

OVERNIGHT IN PHUKET

itinerary.title: PHUKET – KO PHI PHI
itinerary.description:
Meet and greet your driver at 12.00 and head to Phuket pier to catch the ferry boat to your hotel in Ko Phi Phi. Settle in to your hotel and spend the rest of the day however you please. Overnight at hotel in Ko Phi Phi.

OVERNIGHT IN KO PHI PHI

itinerary.title: KO PHI PHI
itinerary.description:
Full day of leisure. Take some time to relax poolside or head to the beach. Overnight at hotel in Ko Phi Phi with breakfast included.

OVERNIGHT IN KO PHI PHI

itinerary.title: KO PHI PHI – KRABI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, the hotel staff will be on hand to help you transfer to Ton Sai pier. From here you’ll catch the ferry to Krabi and then make your way to your hotel to check-in. There is no set itinerary, so you could spend your evening exploring Krabi’s streets and stocking up on handicrafts and delicious food.

OVERNIGHT IN KRABI

itinerary.title: KRABI
itinerary.description:
Krabi is a veritable hub of diversity, boasting limestone cliffs and swaying palm shores. Today, visit the extraordinary temples or do some island-hopping around the shores of Krabi. There are so many islets and secluded beaches to explore!

OVERNIGHT IN KRABI

itinerary.title: KRABI
itinerary.description:
Today, you could get in touch with your wild side and try rock-climbing and jungle trekking around Krabi. Alternatively, if basking in the sun at the beach with a mojito in hand sounds more like your cup of tea, feel free to spend the day unwinding.

OVERNIGHT IN KRABI

itinerary.title: KRABI
itinerary.description:
Today, you might decide to take a traditional long tail boat ride to the uninhabited offshore islands and try some snorkelling. The multicolored species and vibrant coral reefs beneath the surface are definitely not to be missed! Your rep will be able to help you make arrangements for any of these excursions.

OVERNIGHT IN KRABI

itinerary.title: KRABI – DEPARTURE
itinerary.description:
After loading up on a delicious breakfast you’ll have some free time before your transfer to Krabi Airport for your flight home. We hope you’ve enjoyed your stay!

END OF TOUR

title: 12 Days Island Hopping Thailand Holiday
description:
Save £600 per person!

This 12-day Thailand island-hopping adventure takes you through some of the country's most stunning tropical destinations, blending relaxation, adventure, and cultural exploration. From the towering limestone cliffs of Krabi to the unspoiled beaches of Koh Lanta, the turquoise waters of Koh Phi Phi, and the vibrant charm of Phuket, this journey offers a perfect balance of leisure and excitement. 

The trip begins in Krabi, where you’ll be greeted and transferred to your hotel for a relaxed start. From there, you'll travel to Koh Lanta, a peaceful island known for its serene beaches and laid-back atmosphere. With ample free time, you can explore at your own pace, whether it’s sunbathing on the beach, snorkeling in crystal-clear waters, or discovering local villages. 

Next, you'll head to the world-famous Koh Phi Phi, an island paradise renowned for its breathtaking scenery, hidden lagoons, and vibrant marine life. Here, you can enjoy thrilling water activities like snorkeling and diving, or simply relax and take in the island’s postcard-perfect beauty. The journey continues to Phuket, Thailand’s largest island, where you can choose to explore the cultural charms of Phuket Old Town, visit an ethical Elephant Jungle Sanctuary, or simply enjoy the island’s renowned nightlife and pristine beaches. 

Throughout the trip, you'll experience seamless travel with scenic ferry rides, comfortable accommodations, and optional excursions that allow for customization based on your interests. Whether you seek adventure, cultural immersion, or pure relaxation, this itinerary offers the ultimate tropical escape, with unforgettable moments at every turn. From exploring lush jungles and limestone caves to dining by the ocean under breathtaking sunsets, this trip is designed to showcase the very best of Thailand’s island life.

itinerary.title: PHUKET
itinerary.description:
Free day at leisure 

Or take our Optional tour ( Half day Elephant Jungle Sanctuary (Join transfer and join activities at the Camp) Time: 730 - 1230 Hrs. Pick up time: 6:30-7:30 am at hotel lobby )

A half-day package to the Elephant Jungle Sanctuary offers visitors an enjoyable opportunity to spend quality time with elephants in their natural home, as well as gain an insight into their history and behavior, and create cherished lifelong memories of their time spent with these amazing animals. Visitors to the Sanctuary will witness stunning views of the surrounding jungle while interacting personally with friendly, happy elephants in a safe and sustainable environment. On the morning of your visit, you will arrive at the Sanctuary, meet your English-speaking guide, and learn a little about the elephants. After being allocated bananas and sugar cane to feed to the elephants, you will walk with your guide through the Sanctuary, where you will find free-roaming elephants and hear their individual stories. You can touch, feed, photograph, and interact with the elephants at your leisure while you learn about their lifestyle and behaviors. After you have met all the elephants, you will accompany them to a nearby mud spa. Get muddy with the elephants as you apply a healthy cosmetic mud treatment to their skin. Next, you will walk with the elephants to a nearby pool and join them in the water for a refreshing bath. Take a swim and help bathe and brush the elephants while they wade through and play in the water. Once the elephants are clean, you can take some final photos with them, and then it will be time to part ways with your new friend and change into some dry clothes. Finally, enjoy a delightful Thai buffet before saying one last goodbye to your new friends before departing from the camp.

OVERNIGHT IN PHUKET

itinerary.title: PHUKET AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Free day at leisure

Or take our Optional tour ( Half day-Old Town Heritage Trails Tour / Private )

Discover Phuket old town. Phuket old town deserves more attention. You will find the historic quarter even more charming these days. The historical part of Phuket is not huge but is rich and exciting enough to explore in half a day. The wonderful mixture of historical insights, Old Phuket Town invites visitors to experience its architecture, commerce, dress, and way of life. Surrounded by Sino-Portuguese buildings on both the sides, it is one of the best places in Thailand to discover and experience the history of Phuket. It offers an illusion to be thrown back in the ancient time and you will enjoy it. Roam in the mysterious streets of Old Phuket Town which serves as an easy way for pedestrians to walk around. Get ready to explore the architectural wonders along with delicious Thai foods and walk along the streets full of styled houses. We will start by picking you up directly from your hotel with our awesome local tour guide. You’ll be taken to see Phuket Old Town. APPROXIMATE TIMELINE: 9.00 Leave hotel 10.00 Phuket town exploration 13.00 Leave the town 14.00 Arrival at your hotel.

OVERNIGHT IN PHUKET

itinerary.description:
After breakfast, day will be at leisure and you can spend it the way you want to.

OVERNIGHT IN PHUKET

itinerary.description:

title: 13 Night Solo Trip Southeast Asia: Thailand, Cambodia And Vietnam
description:
In Thailand you can discover how the Grand Palace and the temples effortlessly entwine with the frenetic streets. Visit Bangkok and be swept along in the crowds and weave through traffic in a tuk tuk before venturing to Wat Phra Kaew or the Temple of the Emerald Buddha which is regarded as the most important Buddhist temple in Thailand.

Located in the historic centre of Bangkok, within the grounds of the Grand Palace, it enshrines Phra Kaew Morakot (the Emerald Buddha), the highly revered Buddha image meticulously carved from a single block of jade. End this thrilling adventure by crossing over the waters of the Chao Phraya River where in 1782 King Rama I built some of the world’s most beautiful temples with the canals earning Bangkok its nickname, “Venice of the East”.

During this fantastic trip, undergo an exhilarating renovation of the senses. Venture into the jungle’s vines and creepers towards Angkor Wat, the 12th century temple and shrine of Khmer art and architecture, a Cambodian mascot with its sculpted towers of lotus blossom and stately surroundings worshiped by Buddhists monks.

Next up is Vietnam with are breathtaking destinations of diverse delights. Travelers are gripped by the sights and sounds, bewitched by their geniality and divine cuisine, captivated by fast-moving cities like Ho Chi Minh, the Mecca in which Vietnam flourishes, and enlightened by  the palaces, cathedrals and the thousands of stalagmites of Sung Sot cave.

Such a concoction of history, French colonial influences, and tales of past upheaval ensures a majestic holiday. This trip also includes Cruise in the Halong Bay, a UNESCO world heritage Site. Lunch on Board with a Free Bottle of Wine, with a lot of Sights & activities along the way.

itinerary.title: ARRIVE BANGKOK--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
This day will be at leisure in Bangkok. You can marvel at the gleaming temples, catch a tuk tuk along the bustling Chinatown or take a longtail boat through floating markets. Food is another Bangkok highlight, from local dishes served at humble street stalls to haute cuisine at romantic rooftop restaurants.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGKOK

itinerary.title: GRAND PALACE AND EMERALD BUDDHA TOUR
itinerary.description:
The Grand Palace was established at the same time as Bangkok in 1782, by the first king of the ruling Chakri dynasty. Each subsequent monarch has added their own contribution to the complex, so that it represents the full range of royal architecture of the Rattanakosin (Bangkok) period. Some of the buildings represent traditional Thai themes, whilst others have distinct European features. Within the palace grounds one can find the temple commonly called Wat Phra Kaew, which houses the small but holy figure of the Emerald Buddha (actually fashioned from a type of jadeite). The figure was discovered in a Chiang Rai temple in 1436 and has traveled since to Lampang, Chiang Mai, Luang Prabang and Vientiane. The tour concludes with a visit to a jewelry retailer.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGKOK

itinerary.title: BRIDGE OVER THE RIVER KWAI TOUR
itinerary.description:
Kanchanaburi Province, one of the largest and hottest of all Thai provinces, lying to the west of Bangkok and adjacent to Myanmar separated mostly by a mountainous border broken by the Three Pagodas Pass. Kanchanaburi sits on the banks of the Kwae Yai and Kwae Noi rivers, surrounded by limestone outcrops and fields of sugar cane. One less pleasant aspect of its history was the prisoner of war camp during the Second World War, when this area was the site of a railway line intended to stretch throughout British Burma to India. 

Visit the Jeath war museum, which is housed in the same sort of huts the POW's used and is filled with exhibits telling their story. Visit the War Cemetery as well as the Bridge on the River Kwai, restored after the war and still carries the railway today.Take a ride along a pleasant section of the unpleasantly named Death Railway.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGKOK

itinerary.title: ARRIVE SIEM REAP--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
You will be received with a warm welcome at Siem Reap International airport and transferred to the hotel. The evening is free for you to relax at the hotel. In case you wish to explore the city, you can choose to visit Pub Street which is famous for its quality yet economical restaurants and bars. Enjoy Khmer dinner on the day of arrival.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: FULL DAY ANGKOR TOUR
itinerary.description:
After a sumptuous breakfast at the hotel, you’ll enjoy a full-day tour of the Angkor complex. You must not miss the antique capital of Angkor Thom. With sites like the South Gate with its huge statues depicting the churning of the ocean of milk; the Bayon Temple, unique for its 54 towers decorated with over 200 smiling faces of Avolokitesvara; the Phimeanakas; the Royal Enclosure; the Elephants Terrace and the Terrace of the Leper King; 

Angkor Thom has so much to offer.

The most famous of all the temples on the plain of Angkor is Angkor Wats. The temple complex covers 81 hectares and is comparable in size to the Imperial Palace in Beijing. Its distinctive five towers are emblazoned on the Cambodian flag and the 12th century masterpiece is considered by art historians to be the prime example of classical Khmer art and architecture.

Other sites that deserve a mention are: Prasat Kravan, also known as ‘The Royal Baths’, the site was once used for ritual bathing; Banteay Kdei, surrounded by 4 concentric walls; Neak Pean, a fountain built in the middle of a pool; Preah Khan temple, built by the King Jayavarman VII.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP - --DAY AT LEISURE OR OPTIONAL BOAT TRIP
itinerary.description:
After eating breakfast at the hotel, your day will be at leisure or you may head out to enjoy an optional boat ride on the Tonle Sap Lake. This beautiful fresh-water lake is the largest in South-east Asia and flows into Tonle Sap River, joining the Mekong in Phnom Penh. Built on the lake, is a wondrous ‘floating village’ with floating schools, floating police station, et al. The ‘village’ moves from place to place following water levels and current.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP - PHNOM PENH
itinerary.description:
After breakfast get ready for a coach journey from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh. On arrival, you will meet one of our representative and transferred to your hotel in Phnom Penh.

Continue your journey in the Pearl of the Orient, Phnom Penh, visiting by yourself the dazzling white and gold complex of the Royal Palace and the Silver Pagoda. Feel free to visit the Genocide Museum or venture to the former Tuol Svay Prey High School, which the Khmer Rouge set up as prison in May 1976, to detain those accused of opposing the Pol Pot regime. Be prepared to be shaken by this moving reminder of the genocide that occurred during Cambodia’s Khmer Rouge regime.

OVERNIGHT IN PHNOM PENH

itinerary.title: PHNOM PENH - HO CHI MINH CITY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, we depart to Ho Chi Minh City by morning bus (Giant Ibis). Upon arrival at Ho Chi Minh City, you are transferred to the hotel. If there’s time, you must stroll around the downtown part of the bustling Ho Chi Minh City to explore the different local ways of life. Ho Chi Minh City, still called Saigon by many locals, is a centre of commerce, finance, culture and tourism in Vietnam. As opposed to its northern counterpart, this metropolis is filled with automobiles, numerous shopping areas and sidewalk cafes.

OVERNIGHT IN HO CHI MINH CITY

itinerary.title: HO CHI MINH CITY SIGHTS & CU CHI TUNNELS EXCURSION
itinerary.description:
The plan is to devote half of today to exploring captivating Ho Chi Minh City. We pass by the now-demolished wartime American Embassy on our way to the Reunification Palace. This famous edifice was a former residence of the President of Southern Vietnam until end of April 1975. We also visit some striking French colonial structures including the Notre Dame Cathedral and the Old Saigon Post Office.

Post this, we head to the Jade Emperor Pagoda and the War Remnant Museum. We conclude our sightseeing session with a walk around the retail Ben Thanh Market where anything and everything is for sale!

 In the afternoon, we travel about 30 km North-West of Ho Chi Minh City to Cu Chi town.

Cu Chi tunnels were used as the base from which the Vietnamese mounted their operations of the Tet Offensive in 1968. The incredible underground network in Cu Chi stretched over 200 kilometres and became legendary during the American war. The extensive tunnels have been specially constructed with living areas, weapon factories, filed hospitals, command centres, plus accommodation. We visit an exposition hall and a widened section of the tunnels to get a feel of the underground life.

There are also possibilities to fire off rounds from an AK47 or MK16 at the nearby rifle range.

OVERNIGHT IN HO CHI MINH CITY

itinerary.title: SAIGON - HANOI--EVENING AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Step back in time as you fly from the buzzing, modern city of Ho Chi Minh to Hanoi. Upon arrival at Noibai Hanoi Airport, you’ll be met by our tour guide & transferred to your hotel in Hanoi. Afternoon / Evening at leisure to explore the evening sights on your own.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI - HALONG PIER - FLAMINGO CRUISE
itinerary.description:
An Early morning Start today to get you to the Halong Pier,  you will be picked up from your hotel with a packed breakfast. We arrive at Halong tourist wharf. Halong Bay has been recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site since 1994. The islands in Halong Bay are mainly limestone and schist islands. Karst, the result of repeated rising and lowering of the continent, underwent full erosion and weathering to create the unique Halong Bay in the world. 

We travel to Flamingo Cruise where you are welcomed by a fresh drink, cruise briefing & safety instructions before setting you free on the deck to explore this Natural wonder. After Sightseeing you will be served with a Vietnamese sea-food lunch & Wine, while cruising towards Surprising Cave. In the evening you will disembark the Cruise for your road trip back to Hanoi. 

 Evening is at leisure to stroll past the Hoan Kiem Lake or enjoy drink and dinner at one of the specialized Singles Pub.        

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you will enjoy a walking tour of the Ho Chi Minh complex, the Ho Chi Minh’s Mausoleum - the final resting place of Vietnamese great father; President Ho Chi Minh’s stilt house - where Ho lived on and off from 1958 to 1969.

The next place is One Pillar Pagoda, a group of structures consisting of a pagoda and a tower built in the middle of a square pond. From there, leave for the Temple of Literature, the first college of Vietnam. While heading back to Hoan Kiem Lake, have a brief stopover at Ngoc Son Temple. Thereafter, you can take a Cyclo tour around the Old Quarter Streets.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Have a leisurely breakfast at the hotel. The day is at leisure to walk around the lake. You can choose to visit the Church, the famous red bridge. In the evening, you will enjoy the Water Puppet Show near the lake (Tickets will be provided).

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI--BACK TO UK OR TAKE OUR OPTIONAL HOI AN TRIP
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you can devote the entire day to shopping and buying souvenirs for friends. As the hotel is located in central Hanoi, all the shopping areas are not too far. Then, according to your flight schedule, you shall be driven to the airport.

FLIGHT BACK TO UK

 Hotel De Bangkok is a boutique hotel in Bangkok city center conveniently located near Pratunam market surrounded by shops, restaurants, Airport Rail Link train at Ratchaprarop Station, 24 hr convenience store & Duty Free. It is a perfect accommodation for exploring Bangkok attractions or place-to-visit. The unique design of De Bangkok Hotel appoints Thai contemporary adjoining with the atmosphere of Western blended well together giving Hotel de Bangkok a unique warmth and friendly ambience.

All air-conditioned rooms come with a seating areas, a flat-screen cable TV and are furnished with modern Thai décor. A minibar and a safety deposit boxes are available.

Relax in the tranquil atmosphere located at the ground floor where you can sit back and relax over the extensive

accommodations.description:
Angkor Panoramic Boutique Hotel, Siem Reap

 Angkor Panoramic Boutique Hotel is a charming boutique located in central Siem Reap, 6 km from Angkor Wat and about 50 m from the riverfront. Enjoying a convenient location, Angkor Panoramic Boutique is only a 15-minute drive from Siem Reap International Airport and a 10-minute walk from the Night Market and Pub Street.

The Frangipani Royal Palace Hotel, Phnom Penh

 The Frangipani Royal Palace Hotel is situated in the heart of Phnom Penh on the edge of the citys art district and couple of minutes walk from the bustling river front area and the historic Royal Palace. With a roof top pool and restaurant, own spa, and spacious, comfortable rooms, the hotel is the perfect base from which to explore Cambodia’s capital.

All the rooms in this hotel feature elegant design and offer highest levels of accommodation. The Royal Palace View Balcony Suites provide spacious suite accommodation featuring elegant contemporary Cambodia design. The suites have views over the Royal Palace compound and across Phnom Penh’s cityscape beyond.

Guests can make arrangements for a relaxing massage or rejuvenating spa or organise

accommodations.name: Ha Hien Signature Hotel, Ho Chi Minh City
accommodations.description:
Ha Hien Signature Hotel, Ho Chi Minh City

 Being an international deluxe hotel, Ha Hien Signature Hotel located in the downtown with historical relic spots. Ha Hien is a 3-star hotel near Ben Thanh market – just a minute to the northern gate, at the corner of Ly Tu Trong street & Thu Khoa Huan street. A convenient place for businessmen, travelers, for those making excursion and shopping. The newly opened Ha Hien Signature saigon offers first class hotel's amenities of international standard. Besides great location and elegant rooms, staffs are our biggest asset. The staffs at the 3 star Ha Hien Signature Saigon Hotel are well known for their enthusiasm, problem solving skills and friendliness.

This Saigon boutique hotel has 10 floors and consists of 62 elegantly furnished and tastefully

accommodations.name: Flamingo Luxury Cruise, Halong
accommodations.description:
Flamingo Luxury Cruise, Halong

 Flamingo cruise 1 was launched in 2014, soon followed by Flamingo cruise 2 and Flamingo cruise 3 in end of 2014. Accommodate 38 cabins with fleet of 3 cruises, Flamingo Cruises Halong could carry and serve nearly 100 guests with the highest standards of hospitality worthy of world class.

Flamingo cruises are one of few cruises line in Halong Bay with fully equipment, the highest safety standards with sonar, radars. Each cabin is provided modern alarm devices linked to a central monitoring system, smoke sensor, fire extinguishers, hydrant and hoses, life jacket and clear emergency instructions.

Guests can join the Tai Chi sessions, cooking lessons, or enjoy some alone-time on the sun deck. Water activities include swimming and kayaking. Besides the outstan

accommodations.name: Hanoi Boutique Hotel
accommodations.description:
Hanoi Boutique Hotel

 Introducing Hanoi's new Boutique Hotel, right in the Heart of Old Quarter! Hanoi Boutique Hotel promises unbeatable service combined with unparalleled accommodations. Designed with western guests in mind, hotel has spared no expense in creating a luxurious atmosphere for guests who seek high quality. 

Installed in each and every room are the most modern air conditioners available anywhere, plus safes programmed by each guest with their own password, laptop computers with free internet, and of course comfortable beds, roomy baths, and minibars.

Olive Restaurant serves up an array of international dishes, while The Lounge & Café Terrace offers a variety of snacks and beverages. Room service is provided round the clock.

accommodations.name: Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa
accommodations.description:
Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa

 Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa is an ideal choice for an unforgettable, once-in-a-lifetime romantic getaway. This enchanting resort is ideally located alongside the romantic and tranquil Thu Bon River of Hoi An and surrounded by beautiful Nipa palm trees. 

CD TVs with satellite channels are featured in all 36 soundproofed rooms, and you can enjoy free WiFi, free wired Internet, and private yards. Minibars provide free refreshments, and room service makes dining in an option. Nice touches to enhance your stay include rainfall showerheads and bathrobes, while memory foam beds and premium bedding invite a good night's rest. 

At Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa, guests may take part in a cooking class or enjoy fishing at the private pond. The friendly

title: 14 Nights Trip To Thailand Cambodia and Vietnam
description:
An amazing trip split between Thailand, Cambodia & Vietnam - Save £500 per person!

Experience the cultural richness and unique market scenes of Thailand with this comprehensive tour. Begin in Bangkok with visits to three significant temples: Wat Trimit, housing a 3-meter tall Buddha statue made of 5.5 tons of solid gold; Wat Pho, featuring Bangkok's largest reclining Buddha statue at 46 meters long and home to the birthplace of traditional Thai massage; and Wat Benchamabophit, the Marble Temple, built from white Italian marble and housing one of the country's most beautiful Buddha images.

 After exploring these spiritual sites, head to the Maeklong Railway Market, also known as Talad Rom Hup ("Umbrella Pulldown Market"), where you'll witness the unique sight of market stalls being pulled back to let trains pass through. Finally, visit the Damnoen Saduak Floating Market, reached by a short paddleboat ride, where vendors sell goods directly from their boats. Enjoy the vibrant atmosphere and sample local specialties like mango sticky rice. This tour offers a memorable glimpse into Thailand's Buddhist heritage and bustling market culture.

During this fantastic trip, undergo an exhilarating renovation of the senses. Venture into the jungle’s vines and creepers towards Angkor Wat, the 12th century temple and shrine of Khmer art and architecture, a Cambodian mascot with its sculpted towers of lotus blossom and stately surroundings worshiped by Buddhists monks.

Next up is Vietnam with are breathtaking destinations of diverse delights. Travelers are gripped by the sights and sounds, bewitched by their geniality and divine cuisine, captivated by fast-moving cities like Ho Chi Minh, the Mecca in which Vietnam flourishes, and enlightened by  the palaces, cathedrals and the thousands of stalagmites of Sung Sot cave.

Such a concoction of history, French colonial influences, and tales of past upheaval ensures a majestic holiday. This trip also includes an Overnight Cruise in the Halong bay, a UNESCO world heritage Site. Full Board and with a lot of Sights & activities along the way.

itinerary.title: ARRIVE BANGKOK--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
This day will be at leisure in Bangkok. You can marvel at the gleaming temples, catch a
tuk tuk along the bustling Chinatown or take a longtail boat through floating markets.
Food is another Bangkok highlight, from local dishes served at humble street stalls to haute cuisine at romantic rooftop restaurants.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGKOK

itinerary.title: Bangkok Temples Half Day Tour
itinerary.description:
This half-day tour in Bangkok offers a chance to visit three of the city's most significant temples, each renowned for their cultural value and Buddhist sacredness. The first stop is Wat Trimit, the Temple of the Golden Buddha, a modern temple built to house a 3-meter tall Buddha statue crafted from 5.5 tons of solid gold. This gleaming Sukhothai style statue was accidentally discovered after a painted and decorated statue was dropped during relocation, revealing the beautiful solid gold Buddha inside. As you pass through Chinatown, you'll visit Wat Pho, the Temple of the Reclining Buddha. This temple, one of Bangkok's oldest, features the city's largest Buddha statue, measuring 46 meters in length and 15 meters in height, illustrating the Buddha's passing into nirvana. Wat Pho is also home to Thailand's largest collection of Buddha images, the country's first university, and is the birthplace of traditional Thai massage. The final stop is Wat Benchamabophit, the Marble Temple, built in the 19th century from white Italian marble from Carrara. This monastery houses one of the country's most beautiful Buddha images. This tour provides a glimpse into Thailand's rich Buddhist heritage and showcases some of Bangkok's most iconic and architecturally stunning temples.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGKOK

itinerary.title: Maeklong Railway Market and Damnoen Saduak Floating Market Tour
itinerary.description:
This tour takes you to two of Thailand's most unique and vibrant markets: the Maeklong Railway Market and the Damnoen Saduak Floating Market.

 The first stop is the Maeklong Railway Market, also known as Talad Rom Hup, or the “Umbrella Pulldown Market.” This market is one of the largest fresh seafood markets in Thailand and is situated right on the tracks of the Maeklong Railway. With your local guide, gain fascinating insights into the market's operations and the variety of products sold. Marvel at how the market stalls and awnings are pulled back from the tracks every time a train approaches, allowing the train to pass through.

 Next, you'll visit the Damnoen Saduak Floating Market, one of the most famous floating markets in Thailand. To reach the market, take a short ride in a paddleboat. Enjoy plenty of time to explore this colorful market, where friendly vendors sell their goods directly from boats. Take in the sights and sounds, and sample local specialties like mango sticky rice. Your guide will provide recommendations on what not to miss. The tour returns to Bangkok in time for the early afternoon.

OVERNIGHT IN BANGKOK

itinerary.title: ARRIVE SIEM REAP--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
You will be received with a warm welcome at Siem Reap International airport and transferred to the hotel.

The evening is free for you to relax at the hotel. In case you wish to explore the
city, you can choose to visit Pub Street which is famous for its quality yet economical restaurants and bars. Enjoy Khmer dinner on the day of arrival.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: FULL DAY ANGKOR TOUR
itinerary.description:
After a sumptuous breakfast at the hotel, you’ll enjoy a full-day tour of the Angkor complex.

The most famous of all the temples on the plain of Angkor is Angkor Wats. The temple complex covers 81 hectares and is comparable in size to the Imperial Palace in Beijing.

Its distinctive five towers are emblazoned on the Cambodian flag and the 12th century masterpiece is considered by art historians to be the prime example of classical Khmer art and architecture.

You must not miss the antique capital of Angkor Thom. 

With sites like the South Gate with its huge statues depicting the churning of the ocean of milk; the Bayon Temple, unique for its 54 towers decorated with over 200 smiling faces of Avolokitesvara; the Phimeanakas; the Royal Enclosure; the Elephants Terrace and the Terrace of the Leper King; Angkor Thom has so much to offer.

Other sites that deserve a mention are: Prasat Kravan, also known as ‘The Royal Baths’, the site was once used for ritual bathing; Banteay Kdei, surrounded by 4 concentric walls; Neak Pean, a fountain built
in the middle of a pool; Preah Khan temple, built by the King Jayavarman VII.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP - DAY AT LEISURE OR OPTIONAL BOAT TRIP
itinerary.description:
After enjoying breakfast at the hotel, your day will be at leisure or you may head out to enjoy an optional boat ride on the Tonle Sap Lake.

This beautiful fresh-water lake is the largest in South-east Asia and flows into Tonle Sap River, joining the Mekong in Phnom Penh. Built on the lake, is a wondrous ‘floating village’ with floating schools, floating police station, et al. The ‘village’ moves from place to place following water levels and current.

Be prepared to be shaken by this moving reminder of the genocide that occurred during Cambodia’s Khmer Rouge regime.

itinerary.title: HANOI - HALONG PIER - LA REGINA ROYAL CRUISE
itinerary.description:
You will get picked up at the hotel lobby for a road journey to Halong by a shuttle bus.Upon arrival in Halong, check in the overnight cruise to discover the magnificent beauty of this “Descending-dragon” bay, created by thousands odd-shaped limestone rocks and karst grottoes. After a lunch on board, we can visit the Grottos, stop for swimming or kayaking, passing floating houses and approach islets to enjoy their beauty in close distance. Back to the cruise, enjoy cooking demonstration before having dinner. Evening attend activities on the boat or relax at your own leisure

OVERNIGHT ON CRUISE

itinerary.title: LA REGINA ROYAL CRUISE - HALONG PIER
itinerary.description:
The day starts early and has a lot of adventures to offer.
06:15: Tai Chi class on the Sun-Deck
07:00: Have light breakfast with Coffee, tea, and bakeries on board.
 You should wake up early to enjoy the dawn on the sea in the early morning. The Vietnamese call the "East Sea" by a point-oriented name "Eastern Sea", so you may guess how miraculous seeing the sunrise over the islets of the Bay. After breakfast our boat will continue to cruise in marvelous areas, where almost each islet has its own name, adopting its shape or reminiscent like Sail Island, Fisherman’s head rock, Butterfly rock or Turtle islet etc. Check out your room and have brunch on board while the cruise coming back the pier. Disembark from the cruise, transfer back to hotel in Hanoi.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you will enjoy a walking tour of the Ho Chi Minh complex, the Ho Chi Minh’s Mausoleum - the final resting place of Vietnamese great father; President Ho Chi Minh’s stilt house - where Ho lived on and off from 1958 to 1969.

In the evening, experience the Water Puppet Show near the lake and later you can enjoy a relaxing 30 minutes spa session.

END THE TOUR

accommodations.name: Le Petite Salil Sukhumvit 11, Bangkok
accommodations.description:
Le Petite Salil Sukhumvit 11, Bangkok

 Nestled down a small, quiet street off the Sukhumvit road, La Petite Salil Sukhumvit 11 awaits to welcome guests with its unique charm and character. World-class shopping malls, fine dining restaurants, night markets, and nightlife that are in the vicinity make this hotel’s location superb. Just a 5-minute walk to Nana BTS skytrain station, the hotel has a relaxed ambience, perfect for unplugging and recharging before the next day's adventures. Besides the dedicated guest rooms. the hotel also has facilities that a decent urban hotel has to offer –elevator, shuttle service from and to Nana BTS skytrain station, and free high speed Wi-Fi.

accommodations.name: Tara Angkor Hotel, Siem Reap
accommodations.description:
Tara Angkor Hotel, Siem Reap

 Tara Angkor Hotel is the 4-Star Luxury Hotel built in the mystical land of Angkor. Ideally and conveniently located. Tara Angkor Hotel is situated only 6 km from the World Heritage site of Angkor Wat Temples, 15 min drive from the Siem Reap International Airport, a few minutes stroll to the Angkor National Museum and a short ride to the city town center with an array of Cambodian souvenirs, shopping and culture.

Tara Angkor’s rooms feature wooden flooring and garden or city views. Each room has cable TV, a safety deposit box, and tea/coffee making facilities. 

Guests can approach the 24-hour front desk for currency exchange, tour arrangements, ticketing and concierge services. Alternatively, guests can exercise in the fitness centre or relax in the sauna. T

accommodations.name: Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel
accommodations.description:
Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel

 Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel is within a 5-minute walk from the Royal Palace. The property is just 500 metres from the Riverfront Park, while Wat Phnom and Chaktomouk Conference Hall 2 are 1 km away. It is 3.8 km to Aeon Mall. Phnom Penh International Airport is 9 km from Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel.
Fitted with parquet flooring, air-conditioned rooms open up to views of the city. The seating area is fitted with a flat-screen TV, minibar and a desk. En suite bathrooms include shower facilities, a hairdryer and free toiletries.
Guests can approach the 24-hour front desk for currency exchange, business facilities and concierge services. A fitness centre is available for working out or guests can indulge in in-house spa treatments. Guests may also rent a car to explore the

accommodations.name: Emm Hotel Saigon
accommodations.description:
Emm Hotel Saigon

 Centrally located between Saigon’s glittery downtown of District 1 and its historic homes of District 3, ÊMM Hotel Saigon is an easy walk to such city icons as Notre Dame Cathedral and Unification Palace as well as a variety of restaurants, shopping centers and local markets. For guests traveling by air, ÊMM Hotel Saigon is just a 15-minute taxi ride from Tan Son Nhat International Airport.

Grand Citidel Hotel, Hanoi

 Hanoi is a charming and enchanting city. Our hotel boasts 97 rooms, a restaurant, a gym, a welcoming lobby, a stylish bar, and a stress-relieving spa, providing a haven for relaxation and comfort. With three luxurious room categories and naturally illuminated event spaces, our hotel offers a space that is sophisticated, practical, and more inviting than ever.

Each room is equipped with a TV, safety deposit box and tea/coffee making facilities. Local cooking classes begin with a guided boat trip to Hoi An Market. Waterlily Spa offers Vietnamese massage therapies. Other recreational activities include a game of billiards or a workout in the fitness centre. At River Breeze Restaurant, guests can eat indoors or on the balcony overlooking the river. Snacks and refreshments can be enjoyed at the Sunshine Bar and the beachfront Sands Bar.

Spend your days soaking up the sun on pristine beaches, enjoying resort amenities, or exploring Phuket’s vibrant culture, island landscapes, and local attractions at your own pace. Whether you are planning a romantic escape, a family holiday, or a peaceful beach retreat, this Phuket tour promises a memorable and stress-free island experience.

title: 7 nights Split Between Bangkok And Phuket With City Sightseeing Tour
description:
Nestled in Southeast Asia, Thailand is a country bursting at the seams with dream-like beaches and jaw-dropping temples and monuments. To ensure your holiday here is as diverse and interesting as possible, we’re offering a getaway that’s equal parts sightseeing and sun-chasing – split between the bright lights of Bangkok and sun-drenched Phuket. 

Stopping off first in Bangkok, you’ll spend three nights at the W22 By Burasari Hotel. Close to all the major attractions, it’s a great springboard for exploring during your time in the capital. Included in your package is a sightseeing tour of the city, where you’ll stop in at historical treasures like the Grand Palace and Emerald Buddha, accompanied by an expert English-speaking guide. 

After three nights in the hustle and bustle of Bangkok, you’ll head to Phuket for a four-night stay at the 4-star Dinso Resort. The island oozes colour – the emerald hue of the limestone karsts collides with azure waters and golden sands, creating an idyllic, relaxing setting. 

Bed down on a lounger by the pool or head to the beach to do some snorkelling. Whatever you spend your time doing, we doubt you’ll be disappointed with Phuket.

Private airport transfers

title: 9 Nights Stay In Bangkok and Hua Hin With Breakfast
description:
Lonely Planet describes Bangkok as “a city where the familiar and the exotic collide like the flavors on a plate of pat tai” and this couldn’t be more true. Although a small trading post once upon a time, Bangkok is now a destination of total diversity which continues to prosper.

Observe how the Grand Palace and glorious temples intertwine with Bangkok’s busy streets and head off weaving through traffic in as tuk tuk before venturing to Wat Pho, home of the reclining Buddha. Then, cross the Chao Phraya River and stand in awe at some of the world’s most beautiful temples which make up the scenery of the “Venice of the East.” These temples were built around 1782 by the King Rama I and will do their best to leave you absolutely spellbound. 

Next, hop to Hua Hin, Thailand’s oldest resort and ultimate beach-hopping destination with golden sands and swaying palms. History and creativity are abundant in this fascinating town and ornate temples are ten a penny, making for attractive and unparalleled scenery. Then, lounge about on a sunbed, mojito in hand, gazing at the blue ocean ripples beneath a backdrop of forested peaks.

title: 9 Nights Thailand Twin Centre With Transfers and Breakfast
description:
Bangkok, although a prime back-packer destination, offers so much more than just raucous nightlife and inexpensive trinkets. Rub shoulders with locals and tourists alike at the river market of Chao Phraya, brimming with boats and the chatter of merchants as they hustle through to the early hours. Make your way to Wat Pho Temple and the Reclining Buddha and then to the Royal District and The Palace, a successful blend of urban meets ancient. 

Next, you’ll swap the lively streets of Bangkok for the sparkling island of Phuket, sitting only an hour’s plane journey away, thus not biting into your holiday time too much. Palms sway on white, sandy beaches, a rain forest stirs in the middle, and around it you’ll find a plethora of villages, quiet spots, busy spots, places for diving, surfing, sunbathing and chilling - the options really are endless! The fresh and varied cuisine is to die for (make sure you try barbecued and fried seafood, sticky ribs and Pad Thai) and the prices are inexpensive and affordable for both local dishes and beer.

Make your way to Wat Pho Temple and the Reclining Buddha and then to the Royal District and The Palace, a successful blend of urban meets ancient.

title: Bangkok Singapore And Bali Holiday With Breakfast
description:
Bangkok, Singapore and Bali Holiday With Breakfast - Save £500 per person!

Your journey begins in the bustling metropolis of Bangkok, Thailand's capital, where you'll be swept away by the exotic and diverse charms of this westernized Asian city. Immerse yourself in its vibrant markets, tantalize your taste buds with spicy culinary delights, and marvel at the ornate temples and glittering palaces that adorn its landscape.

Next, it's off to the world-famous city-state of Singapore, where you'll be mesmerized by the sights and sounds that await you. Feast on the delectable offerings at a hawker, catch breathtaking views of the city's skyscrapers from Merlion Park, immerse yourself in the rich cultural experiences of Little India and Chinatown, and unwind with a refreshing drink at Clarke Quay - just a few of the incredible experiences that await you in this cosmopolitan gem.

Finally, your journey takes you to the enchanting island paradise of Bali, also known as the Island of the Gods. Immerse yourself in the lush green paddy fields, discover ancient temples steeped in history, witness unforgettable sunsets, immerse yourself in the local art and culture, witness ancient dances, and be captivated by the breathtaking landscapes.
Don't miss out on this exceptional opportunity to experience the best of Southeast Asia in one incredible holiday. Book now and embark on an unforgettable adventure that will leave you with cherished memories for a lifetime!

title: Central Hotel Stays In Bangkok, Chiang Mai And Koh Samui
description:
Head off on the journey of a lifetime to three of Thailand’s treasures: Bangkok, Chiang Mai and Koh Samui. Kick back and relax at high-end hotels, dip your toes into emerald waters and drift between dazzling temples. The holiday of your dreams is only a click away… 

You’ll kick-start your adventure like a true A-Lister with a 2 nights’ stay in one of Bangkok’s 5 star hotels. Luxurious and centrally located, the Ramada Plaza has received a Certificate of Excellence from Trip Advisor and provides a perfect base for exploring the often dubbed “Venice of the East.” First, cross the Chao Phraya River and marvel at a plethora of splendid temples and other cultural landmarks which outline Bangkok’s history and heritage. Then, swap the sightseeing for some serious pampering at the hotel’s signature spa or head out and explore Bangkok’s vibrant nightlife. 

Next, an internal flight will whisk you off to blissful Chiang Mai, a far cry from the crowds and bright lights of Bangkok, where you’ll stay for 2 nights at a 4 star hotel. With golden temples galore, an unparalleled cuisine and a bustling night market, your time in Thailand’s northern capital promises to be memorable.

Last but not least, you’ll touch down on the island of Koh Samui, whose soft and silky sand sits idly behind a backdrop of aquamarine water. The island’s glittering ocean provides ample opportunity to cool off from Koh Samui’s average temperature of 28 degrees Celsius. Your home for 5 nights will be the 4 star Pavilion Samui Resort, nestled within acres of beautifully landscaped gardens and offering ultimate rest and relaxation to all its guests.

title: City Lights to Island Nights Bangkok Bali Lombok and Singapore
description:
Discover
the Enchanting Treasures of Southeast Asia

Embark on an unforgettable journey through three of Southeast Asia’s most captivating destinations, staying in luxurious 5-star hotels at every stop. This carefully curated tour offers a seamless blend of urban flair, cultural immersion, and tropical bliss—all with flights included (featuring both cabin bag and a generous 20kg hold allowance).

Itinerary Highlights

3 Nights
in Bangkok

Begin your adventure in Thailand’s vibrant capital, where modern skyscrapers and ancient temples exist side by side. Stay in a prestigious 5-star hotel with breathtaking city views, and indulge in the local street food, vibrant shopping districts, and the iconic Grand Palace.

3 Nights
in Ubud, Bali

Next, journey to the heart of Bali’s cultural gem: Ubud. Renowned for its lush rice terraces, serene temples, and thriving arts scene, Ubud offers a peaceful escape in a luxurious resort set amidst tropical foliage. Treat yourself to holistic spa treatments, explore local artisan crafts, and take in the area’s spiritual heritage.

3 Nights
in Lombok

Continue to the sun-kissed shores of Lombok, where pristine beaches and crystal-clear waters await. Spend your days lounging by the pool, snorkelling vibrant coral reefs, or discovering hidden waterfalls. By night, enjoy sumptuous dining experiences in your exclusive 5-star haven, savouring the island’s tranquillity and natural beauty.

2 Nights
in Singapore

Conclude your journey in the clean, cosmopolitan city of Singapore. Stay at a luxury 5-star hotel along the scenic waterfront. Enjoy world-class shopping on Orchard Road, take in the futuristic beauty of Gardens by the Bay, and savour a blend of cultures through the city's renowned culinary scene.

Go Solo fr. £1999

Why not add a trip to see the Komodo Dragons fr. £699pp? Ask your consultant for more details.

Beach Bliss in Lombok: Unwind on stunning beaches, experience local culture, and indulge in water activities or relaxation at your own pace.

Ayung Resort Ubud

 Nestled in Bali's lush rainforests, Ayung Resort Ubud offers a luxurious retreat blending natural beauty with cultural heritage. Enjoy elegant accommodations with traditional Balinese aesthetics and modern amenities, gourmet dining at Wijaya Kusuma, and personalized treatments at the wellness and spa center. accommodations.name: Royal Avila Boutique Resort
accommodations.description:
Royal Avila Boutique Resort

 A therapeutic sanctuary in the Northern coast of Lombok. Perched atop the majestic cliffs, an alluring volcanic hill overlooking Malimbu beach, Royal Avila offers warmth and intimate tropical Mediterranean-inspired stays in the elegance and exceptional kindness of Indonesian hospitality. Nestled within 2.5 hectares of lush tranquility this modern resort is a quintessential blend of authentic Indonesian and International sensibilities. Our 54 all-suites accommodation provide a sensorial experience of Lombok with remarkable sightlines, intuitive amenities, and emotionally rewarding encounters.

Grand Copthorne Waterfront

 The 5 star Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel in Singapore is a luxurious 5-star accommodation along the scenic Singapore River. Offering a blend of modern design and Asian tradition, the hotel features newly renovated rooms with full-length windows, fine dining options, a serene outdoor pool, and a fitness suite. Located near Clarke Quay and just minutes from the Havelock MRT station, guests enjoy easy access to top attractions. The hotel also emphasises sustainability with green initiatives and offers complimentary Wi-Fi and on-site parking.

title: Luxury 5 Star Holiday To Bangkok, Singapore And Bali With Breakfast
description:
Explore, Relax, and Luxuriate: The Ultimate Tri-City Getaway! 

 
Fly in Style: Your journey begins with a comfortable international flight, ensuring you're ready to dive into the adventures that await. Rest assured; all your in-between travel arrangements have been meticulously planned for you!

Bangkok Bliss: Start off in the heart of Thailand with a 4-night stay at the Grand Mercure Bangkok Atrium. Rated 5-stars and a traveller’s choice, experience Thai hospitality at its finest while being in proximity to the city's bustling attractions.

Singapore Sojourn: Next, jet off to the Garden City. A flight will whisk you from Bangkok to Singapore, where you'll spend 2 nights at the 5-Star Grand Park City Hall. Dive into the city's futuristic vibes, historical corners, and culinary delights, while enjoying a blend of luxury and comfort.

Bali Beachfront Dreams: As your journey continues, a flight will take you to the serene shores of Bali. Spend 5 rejuvenating nights at the 5-Star Nikko Bali Beach in Benoa. Allow the ocean waves to lull you into tranquillity and wake up to breath-taking beachfront views every morning.

Delectable Beginnings: At every destination, kick start your day with a scrumptious breakfast spread. From Thai delicacies to Balinese flavours, begin each morning with a feast that celebrates the local cuisine.

Seamless Transfers: Forget the travel hassle! Enjoy convenient and prompt airport transfers at every destination, ensuring that you spend maximum time exploring and relaxing.

Your luxurious tri-city escape awaits. Dive into a world of exploration, indulge in 5-star comforts, and create memories that will last a lifetime. Book today and give yourself the holiday you truly deserve!

title: Luxury Singapore Bali Rainforest And Bangkok
description:
Embark on a captivating 13-day's journey from the UK to Singapore, Ubud, Seminyak, and Bangkok, with all transfers included. Start with a luxurious two nights at the 5-star Grand Copthorne Waterfront Hotel in Singapore. exploring Marina Bay Sands, Gardens by the Bay, and Orchard Road.

 Then, fly to Ubud, Bali, for a three-night retreat at the 5-star Ayung Resort Ubud, set amidst tropical rainforests with stunning views and access to cultural sites like the Monkey Forest.

Next, transfer to Seminyak for a four-night stay at the 5-star Montigo Resorts , enjoying beachside activities, spa treatments, and vibrant nightlife.

Conclude your adventure with a three-night stay at the Grand Mercure Bangkok Atrium in Bangkok. Rated 5-stars and a traveller’s choice, experience Thai hospitality at its finest while being in proximity to the city's bustling attractions.

This seamless travel experience includes private transfers and daily breakfasts, ensuring a luxurious and memorable Southeast Asian adventure.

itinerary.title: Arrive Singapore
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Singapore airport, you will be transferred to your hotel.

itinerary.title: Singapore City Tour
itinerary.description:
You begin our journey from Suntec City to see the Fountain of Wealth before continuing towards the Padang area, followed by a stop at the Merlion Park where you can take beautiful pictures with the Singapore icon.

 Other prominent attractions in that area includes the picturesque Fullerton Hotel, Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay, Singapore Flyer, Marina Bay Sands Hotel and the Helix Bridge. A section of the tour will also bring you to Chinatown to see the Thian Hock Keng Temple, one of Singapore’s oldest and most important temple of the Hokkien people in the country. 

Next, we will explore Little India for some cultural sights and sounds. The last leg of the tour will bring you to Faber Peak Singapore @ Mount Faber, Singapore’s iconic hilltop destination to catch a fascinating view of the city skyline. Last stop, visit the Jewellery Wholesale factory.

itinerary.title: Bangkok Temples Tour
itinerary.description:
You cannot leave Thailand without taking this half-day tour visiting three of the most significant temples in Bangkok renowned for their cultural values and Buddhist sacredness.

The first stop of the day is the Wat Trimit, known as the Temple of the Golden Buddha. This modern temple was built to house the famous the 3-metre tall Buddha statue crafted from 5.5 tons of solid gold. This gleaming Sukhothai style statue was accidently discovered after a painted and decorated statue was being moved to a new location, when it was accidently dropped, revealing a beautiful solid gold Sukhothai style Buddha.

As you pass through Chinatown you will come across one of Bangkok´s oldest temples, Wat Pho, known as the Temple of the Reclining Buddha. At 46 metres in length and 15 metres high the Buddha is the largest in Bangkok and illustrates the passing of the Buddha into nirvana. This beautiful temple complex is home to Thailand´s largest collection of Buddha images, the first university and was the birthplace of traditional Thai massage.

The third and final temple is the Wat Benchamabophit, also known as the Marble Temple. This monastery was built out of white Italian marble from Carrara in the 19th century and houses one of the country's most beautiful Buddha images.

itinerary.title: Bangkok
itinerary.description: Exploration of Bangkok at your own pace by visit to the Jim Thompson House, a museum dedicated to Thai art and silk. Spend your afternoon in the heart of Bangkok’s shopping district, exploring the luxurious malls around Siam Square, including Siam Paragon and CentralWorld. In the evening, treat yourself to a rooftop drink at a sky bar like the one at Lebua State Tower, where you can unwind with panoramic views of the city's dazzling skyline.

title: Thailands Tranquil Trio Exploring Bangkok Embracing Elephant Hills And Relaxing In Khao Lak
description:
Discover
Thailand's Tranquil Trio & Save £400 Per Person!

Embark on an enchanting 10-nights journey through Thailand's diverse landscapes and cultures. Start with the vibrant streets of Bangkok, where tradition meets modernity amidst stunning temples and bustling markets. Then venture into the lush wilderness of Elephant Hills to connect with nature, before unwinding on the serene beaches of Khao Lak. This perfectly paced holiday combines the excitement of the city, the adventure of the jungle, and the tranquility of the beach, making it an ideal getaway for those looking to experience the best of Thailand.

Luxurious Bangkok Stay: Dive into luxury with a three-night stay at a prestigious 5-star hotel in Bangkok. Indulge in elegant accommodations and wake up to a delightful breakfast spread each morning, setting the tone for your day of exploration in the bustling metropolis.

Elephant Hills Adventure: Embark on a two-night escapade at Elephant Hills, nestled in the heart of the Thai jungle. Here, you'll forge unforgettable connections with gentle giants during ethical elephant encounters and immerse yourself in the natural beauty of the rainforest through guided treks and immersive experiences.

Khao Lak Stay: Escape to a tropical paradise with a luxurious five-night stay at a stunning beachfront resort in Khao Lak. Nestled on the serene coastlines of the Andaman Sea, this idyllic retreat offers the perfect blend of relaxation and natural beauty, all on a bed and breakfast basis. Whether you're seeking a peaceful getaway or an enchanting beachside adventure,
Khao Lak provides an exquisite backdrop for an unforgettable holiday.

This holiday package offers a perfect blend of excitement, adventure, and relaxation, tailored to give you a comprehensive taste of Thailand's rich offerings.

This perfectly paced holiday combines the excitement of the city, the adventure of the jungle, and the tranquility of the beach, making it an ideal getaway for those looking to experience the best of Thailand.

accommodations.description: Elephant Hills are Thailand’s first Luxury Tented Jungle Camps, combining the camp idea of African national parks with the Thai tropical forest environment of our elephant park. We offer nature soft adventure tours in and around the stunningly beautiful Khao Sok national park area with luxury tents for night time accommodations.

Graceland Khao Lak Beach Resort

 Nestled along the pristine shores of the Andaman Sea, Graceland Khao Lak Beach Resort offers a tranquil retreat in one of Thailand's most beautiful destinations. This luxurious resort combines the natural beauty of Khao Lak with exquisite accommodations, first-class amenities, and impeccable service to ensure a memorable stay for all guests. 

Accommodations: Graceland Khao Lak features a range of room options to cater to various preferences and group sizes, from deluxe rooms with garden views to lavish suites overlooking the ocean. Each room and suite is thoughtfully designed with contemporary décor, infused with traditional Thai elements. Modern comforts such as air conditioning, high-speed internet, and plush bedding provide a comfortable and relaxing e

You must ensure you have adequate travel insurance in place.

More info on Elephant Hills:
All Elephant Hills tour packages are fully inclusive of accommodation, full board, activities as per chosen itinerary, a fully trained, TAT-licensed English speaking Thai tour guide as well as joint transfers to and from Elephant Hills. 

Not included are soft drinks and alcoholic beverages, tips and gratuities as well as personal acquisitions from our souvenir shop or the local markets. 

Itineraries: All activities as stated in our itineraries are subject to change in the order they are undertaken, depending on the local conditions on the day. Please note all activities are subject to change due to local circumstances.

What to bring: Please bring non-slip shoes like sturdy sandals or trainers, loose comfortable clothes, sun protection and a rain coat (disposable raincoats are available to buy at our camp), swim wear for use at the pool or in the river and personal toiletries. Please also remember to bring your passport for the check in process.

Luggage: It is possible to bring your entire luggage to Elephant Hills for your tour if you are changing hotels when your tour program is finished; there is plenty of room in our tents.

title: Laur Elegance: A Serene Aegean Escape
description:
Laur Hotels Experience Elegance offers a vibrant beachfront escape in Didim, perfect for travellers seeking a blend of relaxation, fun, and all-inclusive comfort. Set along a beautiful stretch of sandy coast, the resort provides spacious and well-equipped rooms, many with refreshing sea or pool views. 

Guests can spend their days lounging by multiple swimming pools, enjoying water slides, or unwinding on the private beach, while wellness facilities such as a Turkish hammam, spa, and fitness centre add a touch of indulgence. A wide variety of dining options featuring local and international cuisine, lively entertainment programmes, and dedicated kids’ activities make it an excellent choice for families and couples alike. With its lively atmosphere, extensive facilities, and prime seaside location, Laur Hotels Experience Elegance delivers a complete sun-soaked holiday experience on Turkey’s Aegean coast.

title: 6 Nights in San Francisco and Las Vegas Twin Centre
description:
Explore Two Iconic Cities: San Francisco & Las Vegas 

San Francisco: Start your journey in San Francisco, a city celebrated for its rich history, diverse neighborhoods, and cosmopolitan culture. Walk across the iconic Golden Gate Bridge, explore the vibrant streets of Fisherman's Wharf, and navigate the famous Lombard Street, the crookedest street in the world. Immerse yourself in the artistic vibe of downtown San Francisco, visiting numerous galleries, museums, and the lively theatre district. In the evenings, relax at a jazz bar or indulge in the culinary delights of the Mission District and Union Square, where a variety of bistros and restaurants showcase the city's diverse flavors.

 Las Vegas: After exploring San Francisco, take an internal flight to Las Vegas, the ‘Entertainment Capital of the World.’ Spend 4 unforgettable nights at the Stratosphere Hotel & Casino. Experience the glitz, glamour, and neon brilliance that define this iconic city. Try your luck at the casinos, dine at world-class restaurants, and enjoy spectacular shows that make Las Vegas the ultimate entertainment destination.

The Marker San Francisco

 The Marker Hotel in San Francisco is a luxury boutique hotel located in the heart of the city. Known for its elegant and eclectic design, the hotel offers a unique blend of classic San Francisco charm with modern amenities and a vibrant atmosphere.

Overview:

Location: The Marker Hotel is situated just a few blocks from Union Square, one of San Francisco's most popular shopping and entertainment districts. The central location makes it convenient for guests to explore the city's attractions, such as the Moscone Center, the Theater District, and various dining options.

accommodations.name: Sahara Las Vegas
accommodations.description:
Sahara Las Vegas

 SAHARA Las Vegas epitomizes the timeless soul of Las Vegas. With a bold, sophisticated flair and a boutique approach to personal service, SAHARA provides guests an intimate and unexpected experience that sets a new standard for Vegas. Completely remodeled and reimagined for a new era, SAHARA offers three distinctive hotel towers, a variety of five-star dining options, and a world-class casino. With unexpected delights around every corner, SAHARA embodies the style, sophistication and playful sense of possibility that’s truly the spirit of Vegas - and brings the iconic Vegas experience to a whole new generation.

title: 10 Nights Single Traveler Cambodia and Vietnam Trip
description:
During this fantastic trip, undergo an exhilarating renovation of the senses. Venture into the jungle’s vines and creepers towards Angkor Wat, the 12th century temple and shrine of Khmer art and architecture, a Cambodian mascot with its sculpted towers of lotus blossom and stately surroundings worshiped by Buddhists monks.

Next up is Vietnam with are breathtaking destinations of diverse delights. Travelers are gripped by the sights and sounds, bewitched by their geniality and divine cuisine, captivated by fast-moving cities like Ho Chi Minh, the Mecca in which Vietnam flourishes, and enlightened by the palaces, cathedrals and the thousands of stalagmites of Sung Sot cave.

Such a concoction of history, French colonial influences, and tales of past upheaval ensures a majestic holiday. This trip also includes Cruise in the Halong Bay, a UNESCO world heritage Site. Lunch on Board with a Free Bottle of Wine, with a lot of Sights & activities along the way.
