itinerary.title: ARRIVE SIEM REAP--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
You will be received with a warm welcome at Siem Reap International airport and transferred to the hotel. The evening is free for you to relax at the hotel. In case you wish to explore the city, you can choose to visit Pub Street which is famous for its quality yet economical restaurants and bars. Enjoy Khmer dinner on the day of arrival.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: FULL DAY ANGKOR TOUR
itinerary.description:
After a sumptuous breakfast at the hotel, you’ll enjoy a full-day tour of the Angkor complex. You must not miss the antique capital of Angkor Thom. With sites like the South Gate with its huge statues depicting the churning of the ocean of milk; the Bayon Temple, unique for its 54 towers decorated with over 200 smiling faces of Avolokitesvara; the Phimeanakas; the Royal Enclosure; the Elephants Terrace and the Terrace of the Leper King; Angkor Thom has so much to offer. 

The most famous of all the temples on the plain of Angkor is Angkor Wats. The temple complex covers 81 hectares and is comparable in size to the Imperial Palace in Beijing. Its distinctive five towers are emblazoned on the Cambodian flag and the 12th century masterpiece is considered by art historians to be the prime example of classical Khmer art and architecture. Other sites that deserve a mention are: Prasat Kravan, also known as ‘The Royal Baths’, the site was once used for ritual bathing; Banteay Kdei, surrounded by 4 concentric walls; Neak Pean, a fountain built in the middle of a pool; Preah Khan temple, built by the King Jayavarman VII.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP - --DAY AT LEISURE OR OPTIONAL BOAT TRIP
itinerary.description:
After eating breakfast at the hotel, your day will be at leisure or you may head out to enjoy an optional boat ride on the Tonle Sap Lake. This beautiful fresh-water lake is the largest in South-east Asia and flows into Tonle Sap River, joining the Mekong in Phnom Penh. Built on the lake, is a wondrous ‘floating village’ with floating schools, floating police station, et al. The ‘village’ moves from place to place following water levels and current.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP - PHNOM PENH
itinerary.description:
After breakfast get ready for a coach journey from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh. On arrival, you will meet one of our representative and transferred to your hotel in Phnom Penh.

Continue your journey in the Pearl of the Orient, Phnom Penh, visiting by yourself the dazzling white and gold complex of the Royal Palace and the Silver Pagoda. Feel free to visit the Genocide Museum or venture to the former Tuol Svay Prey High School, which the Khmer Rouge set up as prison in May 1976, to detain those accused of opposing the Pol Pot regime. Be prepared to be shaken by this moving reminder of the genocide that occurred during Cambodia’s Khmer Rouge regime.

OVERNIGHT IN PHNOM PENH

itinerary.title: PHNOM PENH - HO CHI MINH CITY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, we depart to Ho Chi Minh City by morning bus (Giant Ibis). Upon arrival at Ho Chi Minh City, you are transferred to the hotel. If there’s time, you must stroll around the downtown part of the bustling Ho Chi Minh City to explore the different local ways of life. Ho Chi Minh City, still called Saigon by many locals, is a centre of commerce, finance, culture and tourism in Vietnam. As opposed to its northern counterpart, this metropolis is filled with automobiles, numerous shopping areas and sidewalk cafes.

OVERNIGHT IN HO CHI MINH CITY

itinerary.title: HO CHI MINH CITY SIGHTS & CU CHI TUNNELS EXCURSION
itinerary.description:
The plan is to devote half of today to exploring captivating Ho Chi Minh City. We pass by the now-demolished wartime American Embassy on our way to the Reunification Palace. This famous edifice was a former residence of the President of Southern Vietnam until end of April 1975. We also visit some striking French colonial structures including the Notre Dame Cathedral and the Old Saigon Post Office. Post this, we head to the Jade Emperor Pagoda and the War Remnant Museum. We conclude our sightseeing session with a walk around the retail Ben Thanh Market where anything and everything is for sale! In the afternoon, we travel about 30 km North-West of Ho Chi Minh City to Cu Chi town. 

Cu Chi tunnels were used as the base from which the Vietnamese mounted their operations of the Tet Offensive in 1968. The incredible underground network in Cu Chi stretched over 200 kilometres and became legendary during the American war. The extensive tunnels have been specially constructed with living areas, weapon factories, filed hospitals, command centres, plus accommodation. We visit an exposition hall and a widened section of the tunnels to get a feel of the underground life. There are also possibilities to fire off rounds from an AK47 or MK16 at the nearby rifle range.

OVERNIGHT IN HO CHI MINH CITY

itinerary.title: SAIGON - HANOI--EVENING AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Step back in time as you fly from the buzzing, modern city of Ho Chi Minh to Hanoi. Upon arrival at Noibai Hanoi Airport, you’ll be met by our tour guide & transferred to your hotel in Hanoi. Afternoon / Evening at leisure to explore the evening sights on your own.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI - HALONG PIER - CRUISE
itinerary.description:
An Early morning start today to get you to the Halong Pier, you will be picked up from your hotel with a packed breakfast. We arrive at Halong tourist wharf. Halong Bay has been recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site since 1994. The islands in Halong Bay are mainly limestone and schist islands. Karst, the result of repeated rising and lowering of the continent, underwent full erosion and weathering to create the unique Halong Bay in the world. 

We travel to Cruise where you are welcomed by a fresh drink, cruise briefing & safety instructions before setting you free on the deck to explore this Natural wonder. After Sightseeing you will be served with a Vietnamese sea-food lunch & Wine, while cruising towards Surprising Cave. In the evening you will disembark the Cruise for your road trip back to Hanoi. Evening is at leisure to stroll past the Hoan Kiem Lake or enjoy drink and dinner at one of the specialized Singles Pub.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you will enjoy a walking tour of the Ho Chi Minh complex, the Ho Chi Minh’s Mausoleum - the final resting place of Vietnamese great father; President Ho Chi Minh’s stilt house - where Ho lived on and off from 1958 to 1969. The next place is One Pillar Pagoda, a group of structures consisting of a pagoda and a tower built in the middle of a square pond. From there, leave for the Temple of Literature, the first college of Vietnam. While heading back to Hoan Kiem Lake, have a brief stopover at Ngoc Son Temple. Thereafter, you can take a Cyclo tour around the Old Quarter Streets.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI--DAY AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Have a leisurely breakfast at the hotel. The day is at leisure to walk around the lake. You can choose to visit the Church, the famous red bridge. In the evening, you will enjoy the Water Puppet Show near the lake (Tickets will be provided).

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI--BACK TO UK OR TAKE OUR OPTIONAL HOI AN TRIP
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you can devote the entire day to shopping and buying souvenirs for friends. As the hotel is located in central Hanoi, all the shopping areas are not too far. Then, according to your flight schedule, you shall be driven to the airport.

FLIGHT BACK TO UK

accommodations.name: Angkor Panoramic Boutique Hotel, Siem Reap
accommodations.description:
Angkor Panoramic Boutique Hotel, Siem Reap

 Angkor Panoramic Boutique Hotel is a charming boutique located in central Siem Reap, 6 km from Angkor Wat and about 50 m from the riverfront. Enjoying a convenient location, Angkor Panoramic Boutique is only a 15-minute drive from Siem Reap International Airport and a 10-minute walk from the Night Market and Pub Street.

The modern rooms come with cable TV channels and a safety deposit box. A minibar and tea/coffee making facilities are also provided for guests' convenience.

Guests can request for a massage on site. For convenience, the hotel offers a 24-hour front desk, ticketing services and a tour desk.

Hot meals are served at the rooftop restaurant, which overlooks the peaceful river views. Drinks can be enjoyed at the bar.

accommodations.name: Ohana Phnom Penh Palace Hotel
accommodations.description:
Ohana Phnom Penh Palace Hotel

 Situated next to Kandal Market, Ohana Phnom Penh Palace Hotel offers accommodation in the heart of the Riverside area. The attractions of the Royal Palace and the National Museum are less than 1 km away, and the Central Market and Wat Phnom are also 1.3 km. The nearest airport, Phnom Penh International Airport is 10.2 km away. The air-conditioned rooms have flat-screen TVs with international channels, a window and a minibar. At the rooftop, It features 2 outdoor spa swimming pools as well as a restaurant and bar where guests can enjoy meals or have a drink whilst taking in panoramic views of the city, Tonlé Sap Lake and Mekong Rivers.

accommodations.name: Me Gustas MetroPoint, Ho Chi Minh City
accommodations.description:
Me Gustas MetroPoint, Ho Chi Minh City

 Me Gustas MetroPoint is located in central Ho Chi Minh City, less than 1 km to Antique Street and Ben Thanh Market. Me Gustas MetroPoint is a 25-minute drive to Tan Son Nhat International Airport. The air-conditioned rooms are each equipped with a cable/satellite TV, minibar and fridge. Private bathrooms come with bathtub and shower facilities. The hotel features a business center, a tour desk and laundry services. European and Asian cuisines are served at the hotel’s restaurant.

accommodations.name: Hanoi Boutique Hotel
accommodations.description:
Hanoi Boutique Hotel

 Introducing Hanoi's new Boutique Hotel, right in the Heart of Old Quarter! Hanoi Boutique Hotel promises unbeatable service combined with unparalleled accommodations. Designed with western guests in mind, hotel has spared no expense in creating a luxurious atmosphere for guests who seek high quality. 

Installed in each and every room are the most modern air conditioners available anywhere, plus safes programmed by each guest with their own password, laptop computers with free internet, and of course comfortable beds, roomy baths, and minibars.

Olive Restaurant serves up an array of international dishes, while The Lounge & Café Terrace offers a variety of snacks and beverages. Room service is provided round the clock.

accommodations.name: Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa
accommodations.description:
Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa

 Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa is an ideal choice for an unforgettable, once-in-a-lifetime romantic getaway. This enchanting resort is ideally located alongside the romantic and tranquil Thu Bon River of Hoi An and surrounded by beautiful Nipa palm trees. 

CD TVs with satellite channels are featured in all 36 soundproofed rooms, and you can enjoy free WiFi, free wired Internet, and private yards. Minibars provide free refreshments, and room service makes dining in an option. Nice touches to enhance your stay include rainfall showerheads and bathrobes, while memory foam beds and premium bedding invite a good night's rest. 

At Muca Hoi An Boutique Resort & Spa, guests may take part in a cooking class or enjoy fishing at the private pond. The friendly

title: 12 Days In Cambodia And Vietnam
description:
“Southeast Asia is a fulfilment of my fantasies of the way travel should be.”- Anthony Bourdain

Cambodia and Vietnam are breathtaking destinations of diverse delights. Travelers are gripped by the sights and sounds, bewitched by their geniality and divine cuisine, captivated by fast-moving cities like Ho Chi Minh, the Mecca in which Vietnam flourishes, and enlightened by  the palaces, cathedrals and the thousands of stalagmites of Sung Sot cave. Such a concoction of history, French colonial influences, and tales of past upheaval ensures a majestic holiday.

During our fantastic 12 day trip across Cambodia and Vietnam, undergo an exhilarating renovation of the senses. Venture into the jungle’s vines and creepers towards Angkor Wat, the 12th century temple and shrine of Khmer art and architecture, a Cambodian mascot with its sculpted towers of lotus blossom and stately surroundings worshipped by Buddhists monks.

Wander through the ancient capital of Khmer, empire to the exuberant cities of Siem Reap, Ho Chi Minh and Hanoi, before cruising in Agatha Christie style across Halong Bay, passing some of the 3,000 limestone islands. Add spice by devouring the Vietnamese cuisine of B?n Thành Market infused with a vast spectrum of enticing and evocative flavours, making taste buds explode. “I changed my whole palate as a lot of the dishes I used to love became boring to me.” - Anthony Bourdain.

itinerary.title: ARRIVAL SIEM REAP
itinerary.description:
You will be received with a warm welcome at Siem Reap International airport and transferred to the hotel.

The evening is free for you to relax at the hotel. In case you wish to explore the city, you can choose to visit Pub Street which
is famous for its quality yet economical restaurants and bars. Enjoy Khmer dinner on the day of arrival.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: ANGKOR TOUR
itinerary.description:
After a sumptuous breakfast at the hotel, you’ll enjoy a full-day tour of the Angkor complex.

You must not miss the antique capital of Angkor Thom. With sites like the South Gate with its huge statues depicting the churning of the ocean of milk; the Bayon Temple, unique for its 54 towers decorated with over 200 smiling faces of Avolokitesvara; the Phimeanakas; the Royal Enclosure; the Elephants Terrace and the Terrace of the Leper King; Angkor Thom has so much to offer.

The most famous of all the temples on the plain of Angkor is Angkor Wats. The temple complex covers 81 hectares and is comparable in size to the Imperial Palace in Beijing. Its distinctive five towers are emblazoned on the Cambodian flag and the 12th century masterpiece is considered by art historians to be the prime example of classical Khmer art and architecture.

Other sites that deserve a mention are: Prasat Kravan, also known as ‘The Royal Baths’, the site was once used for ritual bathing; Banteay Kdei, surrounded by 4 concentric walls; Neak Pean, a fountain built in the middle of a pool; Preah Khan temple, built by the King Jayavarman VII.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP AT LEISURE OR OPTIONAL BOAT TRIP
itinerary.description:
After eating breakfast at the hotel, your day will be at leisure or you may head out to enjoy an optional boat ride on the Tonle Sap Lake.

This beautiful fresh-water lake is the largest in South-east Asia and flows into Tonle Sap River, joining the Mekong in Phnom Penh. Built on the lake, is a wondrous ‘floating village’ with floating schools, floating police station, et al. The ‘village’ moves from place to place following water levels and current.

OVERNIGHT IN SIEM REAP

itinerary.title: SIEM REAP - PHNOM PENH
itinerary.description:
After breakfast get ready for a coach journey from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh. On arrival, you will meet one of our representative and transferred to your hotel in Phnom Penh. Continue your journey in the Pearl of the Orient, Phnom Penh, visiting by yourself the dazzling white and gold complex of the Royal Palace and the Silver Pagoda. 

Feel free to visit the Genocide Museum or venture to the former Tuol Svay Prey High School, which the Khmer Rouge set up as prison in May 1976, to detain those accused of opposing the Pol Pot regime. Be prepared to be shaken by this moving reminder of the genocide that occurred during Cambodia’s Khmer Rouge regime.

OVERNIGHT IN PHNOM PENH

itinerary.title: PHNOM PENH - HO CHI MINH CITY
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you be transferred to Phnom Penh bus station for coach transfer to Ho Chi Minh City (still called Saigon by many locals). Upon arrival at Ho Chi Minh City, you are transferred to the hotel.

If there’s time, you must stroll around the downtown part of the bustling Ho Chi Minh City to explore the different local ways of life. Ho Chi Minh City, still called Saigon by many locals, is a centre of commerce, finance, culture and tourism in Vietnam. As opposed to its northern counterpart, this metropolis is filled with automobiles, numerous shopping areas and sidewalk cafes.

OVERNIGHT IN HO CHI MINH CITY

itinerary.title: HO CHI MINH CITY SIGHTS & CU CHI TUNNELS EXCURSION
itinerary.description:
The plan is to devote half of today exploring the captivating Ho Chi Minh City.

We pass by the now-demolished wartime American Embassy on our way to the Reunification Palace. This famous edifice was a former residence of the President of Southern Vietnam until end of April 1975.

We also visit some striking French colonial structures including the Notre Dame Cathedral and the Old Saigon Post Office. Post this, we head to the Jade Emperor Pagoda and the War Remnant Museum. We conclude our sightseeing session with a walk around the retail Ben Thanh Market where anything and everything is for sale!

In the afternoon, we travel about 30 km North-West of Ho Chi Minh City to Cu Chi town. 

Cu Chi tunnels were used as the base from which the Vietnamese mounted their operations of the Tet Offensive in 1968. The incredible underground network in Cu Chi stretched over 200 kilometres and became legendary during the American war.

The extensive tunnels have been specially constructed with living areas, weapon factories, filed hospitals, command centres, plus accommodation. We visit an exposition hall and a widened section of the tunnels to get a feel of the underground life. There are also possibilities to fire off rounds from an AK47 or MK16 at the nearby rifle range.

OVERNIGHT IN HO CHI MINH CITY

itinerary.title: FLIGHT FROM SAIGON - HANOI OR EXTEND TO DANANG IF OPTIONAL HOI AN EXTENSION IS CHOSEN
itinerary.description:
Step back in time as you fly from the buzzing, modern city of Ho Chi Minh to Hanoi. Upon arrival at Noibai Hanoi Airport, you’ll be met by our tour guide & transferred to your hotel in Hanoi.

Afternoon / Evening at leisure to explore the evening sights on your own. 

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI - HALONG PIER - LA REGINA ROYAL CRUISE
itinerary.description:
You will get picked up at the hotel lobby for a road journey to Halong by a shuttle bus.Upon arrival in Halong, check in the overnight cruise to discover the magnificent beauty of this “Descending-dragon” bay, created by thousands odd-shaped limestone rocks and karst grottoes. After a lunch on board, we can visit the Grottos, stop for swimming or kayaking, passing floating houses and approach islets to enjoy their beauty in close distance. Back to the cruise, enjoy cooking demonstration before having dinner. Evening attend activities on the boat or relax at your own leisure

OVERNIGHT ON CRUISE

itinerary.title: LA REGINA ROYAL CRUISE - HALONG PIER
itinerary.description:
The day starts early and has a lot of adventures to offer.
06:15: Tai Chi class on the Sun-Deck
07:00: Have light breakfast with Coffee, tea, and bakeries on board.
 You should wake up early to enjoy the dawn on the sea in the early morning. The Vietnamese call the "East Sea" by a point-oriented name "Eastern Sea", so you may guess how miraculous seeing the sunrise over the islets of the Bay. After breakfast our boat will continue to cruise in marvelous areas, where almost each islet has its own name, adopting its shape or reminiscent like Sail Island, Fisherman’s head rock, Butterfly rock or Turtle islet etc. Check out your room and have brunch on board while the cruise coming back the pier. Disembark from the cruise, transfer back to hotel in Hanoi.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you will enjoy a walking tour of the Ho Chi Minh complex, the Ho Chi Minh’s Mausoleum - the final
resting place of Vietnamese great father; President Ho Chi Minh’s stilt house - where Ho lived on and off from 1958 to 1969.

The next place is One Pillar Pagoda, a group of structures consisting of a pagoda and a tower built in the middle of a square pond. From there, leave for the Temple of Literature, the first college of Vietnam. 

While heading back to Hoan Kiem Lake, have a brief stopover at Ngoc Son Temple. In the evening, experience the Water Puppet Show near the lake and later you can enjoy a relaxing 30 minutes spa session.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI AT LEISURE
itinerary.description:
Have a leisurely breakfast at the hotel and you may choose to you can take a Cyclo tour around the Old Quarter Streets.

OVERNIGHT IN HANOI

itinerary.title: HANOI OR TAKE OUR OPTIONAL HOI AN TRIP
itinerary.description:
After breakfast at the hotel, you can devote the entire day to shopping and buying souvenirs for friends. As the hotel is located in central Hanoi, all the shopping areas are not too far. Then, according to your flight schedule, you shall be driven to the airport.

END OF TOUR

accommodations.name: Tara Angkor Hotel, Siem Reap
accommodations.description:
Tara Angkor Hotel, Siem Reap

 Tara Angkor Hotel is the 4-Star Luxury Hotel built in the mystical land of Angkor. Ideally and conveniently located. Tara Angkor Hotel is situated only 6 km from the World Heritage site of Angkor Wat Temples, 15 min drive from the Siem Reap International Airport, a few minutes stroll to the Angkor National Museum and a short ride to the city town center with an array of Cambodian souvenirs, shopping and culture.

Tara Angkor’s rooms feature wooden flooring and garden or city views. Each room has cable TV, a safety deposit box, and tea/coffee making facilities.

accommodations.name: Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel
accommodations.description:
Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel

 Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel is within a 5-minute walk from the Royal Palace. The property is just 500 metres from the Riverfront Park, while Wat Phnom and Chaktomouk Conference Hall 2 are 1 km away. It is 3.8 km to Aeon Mall. Phnom Penh International Airport is 9 km from Harmony Phnom Penh Hotel.
Fitted with parquet flooring, air-conditioned rooms open up to views of the city. The seating area is fitted with a flat-screen TV, minibar and a desk. En suite bathrooms include shower facilities, a hairdryer and free toiletries.
Guests can approach the 24-hour front desk for currency exchange, business facilities and concierge services. A fitness centre is available for working out or guests can indulge in in-house spa treatments. Guests may also rent a car to explore the

accommodations.name: Emm Hotel Saigon
accommodations.description:
Emm Hotel Saigon

 Centrally located between Saigon’s glittery downtown of District 1 and its historic homes of District 3, ÊMM Hotel Saigon is an easy walk to such city icons as Notre Dame Cathedral and Unification Palace as well as a variety of restaurants, shopping centers and local markets. For guests traveling by air, ÊMM Hotel Saigon is just a 15-minute taxi ride from Tan Son Nhat International Airport.

accommodations.name: La Regina Royal Cruise, Halong
accommodations.description:
La Regina Royal Cruise, Halong

 Known as the charming younger sister of La Regina Legend, La
Regina Royal is not merely as elegant and luxurious in every detail as her
sister, but also youth oriented with a cozy atmosphere and a delightful blend
of traditional Vietnamese design. A cruise trip with La Regina Royal is a
combination of the bay’s most magnificent scenery, local attractions and on
board amenities. Get ready for your most unforgettable moment on La Regina
Royal.

accommodations.name: Grand Citidel Hotel, Hanoi
accommodations.description:
Grand Citidel Hotel, Hanoi

 Hanoi is a charming and enchanting city. Grand Cititel Hanoi is where culture and art merge in the vibrant heart of Hanoi, offering customers an authentic experience, immersing them in the world of exquisite art and understated yet distinctive elegance. Our hotel boasts 97 rooms, a restaurant, a gym, a welcoming lobby, a stylish bar, and a stress-relieving spa, providing a haven for relaxation and comfort. With three luxurious room categories and naturally illuminated event spaces, our hotel offers a space that is sophisticated, practical, and more inviting than ever.

accommodations.name: Hoi An Beach Resort
accommodations.description:
Hoi An Beach Resort

 Nestled between Cua Dai Beach and De Vong River, Hoi An Beach Resort features 2 outdoor pools. Hoi An Beach Resort is a 45-minute drive from Danang International Airport and 4 km from Hoi An’s town centre. Rooms at Resort Hoi An come with private balconies overlooking the garden, river or sea. Each room is equipped with a TV, safety deposit box and tea/coffee making facilities. Local cooking classes begin with a guided boat trip to Hoi An Market. Waterlily Spa offers Vietnamese massage therapies. Other recreational activities include a game of billiards or a workout in the fitness centre. At River Breeze Restaurant, guests can eat indoors or on the balcony overlooking the river. Snacks and refreshments can be enjoyed at the Sunshine Bar and the beachfront Sands Bar.
